pine-island-camp.author: Alum who attended in the 90s
pine-island-camp.text: No electricity, tents on an island, real expeditions. It sounds extreme but it builds a kind of confidence I've never seen anywhere else. Boys come back changed.

pine-island-camp.author: Parent of a second-year camper
pine-island-camp.text: Genuinely rustic - be honest with yourself about whether your kid wants this. Ours loves it, but the lack of creature comforts is not for everyone.

camp-runoia.author: Mother of two campers
camp-runoia.text: Runoia is the anti-scene camp. No fuss about labels or looks, just girls being kids. The directors know every camper by name and it shows.

camp-runoia.author: Former camper, now in college
camp-runoia.text: Ten summers there. The traditions, the lake at dusk, learning to ride - it shaped who I am. Shorter sessions make it a great first camp too.

camp-kieve.author: Father of a longtime camper
camp-kieve.text: The tripping program is the real deal - each summer the expeditions get bigger. My son talks about his Allagash trip the way some people talk about college.

camp-kieve.author: Alum and former counselor
camp-kieve.text: Kieve takes leadership seriously without being preachy. Long waitlists for a reason. Only knock: it books up years in advance.

wavus-camp-for-girls.author: Parent of a three-summer camper
wavus-camp-for-girls.text: My daughter came home from her wilderness trip muddy, exhausted, and prouder of herself than I've ever seen her. Wavus knows exactly what it's doing.

wavus-camp-for-girls.author: Teen camper
wavus-camp-for-girls.text: The trips are amazing and the community is so supportive. Food in the dining hall is just okay, and you will miss your phone at first. Worth it.

chewonki-camp-for-boys.author: Parent of a nature-obsessed kid
chewonki-camp-for-boys.text: If your son would rather flip rocks in a tide pool than play travel soccer, this is his place. The natural history focus is genuine, not a gimmick.

chewonki-camp-for-boys.author: Alum from the 2000s
chewonki-camp-for-boys.text: Chewonki made me an environmentalist before I knew the word. Cabin life is simple and screen-free; a few kids find the pace slow at first.

chewonki-camp-for-girls.author: Parent of a first-time camper
chewonki-camp-for-girls.text: The setting is unreal - a wilderness lake you can only reach by boat. My daughter came back calmer and more capable. It is remote, so drop-off is a trek.

wyonegonic-camps.author: Third-generation camp family
wyonegonic-camps.text: My grandmother, my mother, me, and now my daughter. The simplicity is the point - no electricity in cabins, and the girls genuinely don't miss it.

wyonegonic-camps.author: Former camper and counselor
wyonegonic-camps.text: Wyo teaches you to paddle, to lead, and to be a good friend. Down-to-earth compared to some Maine camps. The lake view of Pleasant Mountain never gets old.

wyonegonic-camps.author: Parent of a 10-year-old
wyonegonic-camps.text: Wonderful staff and traditions. Just know it's genuinely rustic - my daughter took a summer to embrace the no-frills cabins.

winona-camps.author: Father of two Winona boys
winona-camps.text: Tents, not cabins, and boys who learn to sail, paddle, and look adults in the eye. Old-school in the best way. Sibling dances with Wyonegonic are a sweet touch.

winona-camps.author: Alum from the 80s
winona-camps.text: Decades later I still measure summer against Winona. The wilderness trips and the horsemanship program are underrated gems.

camp-o-at-ka.author: Parent of a first-time camper
camp-o-at-ka.text: One-week starter sessions were perfect for our nervous 8-year-old, and the price is fair for Maine. Chapel is gentle and values-focused, not heavy-handed.

camp-o-at-ka.author: Alum
camp-o-at-ka.text: Great sailing and a real sense of honor and service. Facilities are older, but that's part of the charm for most of us.

camp-timanous.author: Parent of a shy camper
camp-timanous.text: Small enough that no kid slips through the cracks. Our son found his people here. Less flashy than the big Maine sports camps - by design.

camp-timanous.author: Alum
camp-timanous.text: The red and grey teams, the pageant, swimming before breakfast - the traditions run deep and the values stick with you.

camp-wildwood-me.author: Parent of a sports-loving son
camp-wildwood-me.text: Great coaching, tons of lake time, and counselors who actually teach. My son counts down the days all year.

camp-wildwood-me.author: Alum
camp-wildwood-me.text: Competitive but friendly. If your kid isn't into sports at all, there are better fits in Maine, but for athletes it's a blast.

camp-cedar-me.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-cedar-me.text: Facilities rival a small college and the counselors are excellent. Full seven weeks only, so it's a commitment - and the tuition matches.

camp-cedar-me.author: Alum
camp-cedar-me.text: Cedar summers were the highlight of my childhood. Color war is legendary. Not the camp for a kid who wants to try a two-week sampler.

camp-agawam.author: Agawam alum and parent
camp-agawam.text: The nonprofit council structure keeps the mission pure - it's about building good men, not margins. My son wears his Agawam values year-round.

camp-agawam.author: Parent
camp-agawam.text: Old wooden lodges, handwritten letters home, and boys who learn to serve each other. Genuinely special, though the full-season format isn't for everyone.

camp-wigwam.author: Parent of two sons
camp-wigwam.text: Wigwam gets the balance right - real sports instruction but nobody rides the bench. The theater productions surprised us with how good they are.

camp-wigwam.author: Alum
camp-wigwam.text: A century of history you can feel. Some cabins are creaky and the wifi-free life takes adjusting, but that's Maine camping.

camp-androscoggin.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-androscoggin.text: Impeccably run. The counselor quality stands out - many are alumni. Seven weeks and the tuition are serious commitments, but the boys are devoted.

camp-androscoggin.author: Alum from the 2010s
camp-androscoggin.text: Andro is family. Grey and Maroon competitions, the lake, lifelong friends. I still go back to visit.

camp-cobbossee.author: Parent
camp-cobbossee.text: A sports camp with history in its bones - the baseball program especially. Counselors coach hard but keep it fun.

camp-cobbossee.author: Alum
camp-cobbossee.text: Loved my summers there. It's an athletic culture, so a kid who'd rather do art all day might feel out of place.

camp-kawanhee.author: Third-generation Kawanhee parent
camp-kawanhee.text: The setting under Mt. Blue is stunning and the alumni association keeps the old spirit alive. Feels like a family reunion every summer.

camp-kawanhee.author: Former counselor
camp-kawanhee.text: Wonderful traditions and waterfront. Being in Weld means you're far from everything - which is either the best or worst part depending on your view.

camp-caribou.author: Mother of a formerly homesick camper
camp-caribou.text: The directors called us personally during a rough first week and turned it around completely. He's now a five-year camper who cries when he leaves.

camp-caribou.author: Alum
camp-caribou.text: The peninsula setting means water on three sides. Family ownership shows in the details. Food is genuinely good for camp.

birch-rock-camp.author: Parent of a quirky kid
birch-rock-camp.text: Birch Rock celebrates the kid who isn't the star athlete. The White Cap swim tradition taught my son perseverance better than any classroom.

birch-rock-camp.author: Alum and former staff
birch-rock-camp.text: Small on purpose. Everyone is known, everyone belongs. Rustic cabins and simple food - nobody cares.

indian-acres-camp.author: Parent with a son and daughter at the two camps
indian-acres-camp.text: Having brother and sister camps minutes apart made logistics easy, and the sibling events are sweet. Solid, well-run traditional camp.

indian-acres-camp.author: Alum
indian-acres-camp.text: Great tennis and waterfront. Fryeburg summers get hot and buggy, but the Saco trips make up for it.

forest-acres-camp.author: Parent of a dancer
forest-acres-camp.text: The dance and gymnastics programs are stronger than most Maine camps, and my daughter loved the flexibility of a 3-week session.

forest-acres-camp.author: Alum
forest-acres-camp.text: Warm community, great friendships. Cabins are basic and the schedule leans structured, which suited me fine.

camp-owatonna.author: Parent
camp-owatonna.text: A kind, values-centered community. Campers come from all over the globe, which gives a small camp a surprisingly big worldview.

camp-owatonna.author: Alum
camp-owatonna.text: Loved the lake and the brother-sister events with Newfound. The spiritual grounding is real, so it fits best for families comfortable with that.

camp-newfound.author: Mother of a camper
camp-newfound.text: My daughter's bunkmates were from three continents. The community is gentle and genuinely inclusive of new campers.

camp-powhatan-me.author: Parent
camp-powhatan-me.text: Well-run and personal. Directors clearly know every boy. Pleasant Lake is gorgeous and the waterfront program takes full advantage.

camp-powhatan-me.author: Alum
camp-powhatan-me.text: Classic camp done right. Nothing gimmicky - which I appreciated more as I got older.

west-end-house-camp.author: Boston parent
west-end-house-camp.text: Unbeatable value - a genuine Maine lake camp our family could actually afford, with generous financial aid on top.

west-end-house-camp.author: Alum
west-end-house-camp.text: Kids from all backgrounds, coaches who care, hundred-year-old traditions. Facilities are humble but the experience isn't.

flying-moose-lodge.author: Father of a two-summer camper
flying-moose-lodge.text: Not really a 'camp' so much as a continuous expedition program. My son learned to portage a canoe and cook over fire at 12. For outdoorsy kids only.

flying-moose-lodge.author: Alum
flying-moose-lodge.text: The real thing. Kerosene lamps, trip food, mountain summits. If your kid needs a pool and a snack bar, look elsewhere.

camp-william-hinds.author: Scoutmaster
camp-william-hinds.text: One of the better-run scout camps in New England. Strong waterfront staff and a beautiful pond. Dining hall food is standard scout fare.

camp-william-hinds.author: Parent of a Scout
camp-william-hinds.text: Affordable week of real outdoor skills. It's troop-based, so the experience depends a lot on your unit's leaders.

camp-bomazeen.author: Longtime Scout leader
camp-bomazeen.text: Beautiful shorefront and good rifle and waterfront programs. Facilities are aging and programs have shifted over the years, but the spirit endures.

camp-roosevelt-me.author: Parent of a first-year Scout
camp-roosevelt-me.text: Solid, affordable week in the woods. My son earned four merit badges and came home filthy and happy. Bring bug spray - a lot of it.

maine-high-adventure.author: Crew advisor
maine-high-adventure.text: The least famous of the BSA high-adventure bases and maybe the most underrated. Moose sightings daily, waters to yourself, great guides.

camp-arcadia-me.author: Arcadia alum, now a camp mom
camp-arcadia-me.text: Sailing wooden boats my grandmother might have sailed - the continuity is magical. Girls unplug completely and rediscover themselves.

camp-arcadia-me.author: Camper, age 14
camp-arcadia-me.text: The traditions sound old-fashioned until you're inside them, and then they're everything. Banquet is the best night of the year.

camp-walden-me.author: Parent of two Walden girls
camp-walden-me.text: The female leadership modeling is exceptional - my daughters idolize their counselors for the right reasons. Strong tennis and swim programs.

camp-walden-me.author: Alum
camp-walden-me.text: Deep traditions and lifelong friends. It books early and costs what elite Maine camps cost.

camp-wawenock.author: Mother of a six-summer camper
camp-wawenock.text: The single full-session model means every girl arrives and leaves together - no mid-summer social reshuffling. Rare retention for a reason.

camp-wawenock.author: Alum
camp-wawenock.text: Wawenock is a masterclass in intentional community. Seven weeks felt short by August.

wohelo-camps.author: Parent
wohelo-camps.text: Five generations of the same family running one camp - and it shows in every detail. The waterskiing program is the best we've seen in Maine.

wohelo-camps.author: Alum from the 90s
wohelo-camps.text: The Sunday white ceremonies by the lake still give me chills. Traditions with actual meaning behind them.

camp-mataponi.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-mataponi.text: The waterfront on Sebago is spectacular and the programming runs like clockwork. It's a big camp with big energy - shy kids may need a minute.

camp-mataponi.author: Alum
camp-mataponi.text: Color war, the lake, lifelong best friends. Mataponi summers were everything. Waitlists are real - apply early.

camp-pinecliffe.author: Second-generation Pinecliffe parent
camp-pinecliffe.text: My daughter was sorted onto the same team I was in 1989. The continuity is remarkable and the friendships are for life.

camp-pinecliffe.author: Alum
camp-pinecliffe.text: Wonderful, spirited summers. The seven-week commitment and price tag are significant - it's an all-in kind of camp.

camp-fernwood.author: Parent
camp-fernwood.text: The equestrian program is a standout, and the full-summer format builds friendships you just can't get in two weeks.

camp-fernwood.author: Alum
camp-fernwood.text: Fernwood is home. Simple as that. Letters only, no screens, and none of us would change it.

fernwood-cove.author: Parent of a first-time camper
fernwood-cove.text: Four weeks was the perfect on-ramp, and the staff's communication with parents is the best we've experienced anywhere.

fernwood-cove.author: Camper, age 12
fernwood-cove.text: I was nervous and by day three I didn't want to ever leave. The counselors notice everything.

camp-tapawingo-me.author: Alum and current camp parent
camp-tapawingo-me.text: The singing - at meals, at campfire, everywhere - is what my daughter mentions first. A gentle, grounded community.

camp-tapawingo-me.author: Former counselor
camp-tapawingo-me.text: Lovely traditions and a supportive vibe. Facilities are simple and the town of Sweden is tiny, so it's all about camp life.

alford-lake-camp.author: Mother of a tent-dwelling 11-year-old
alford-lake-camp.text: Girls in tents, no electricity, and more confidence per week than a year of school. The international camper mix is a quiet strength.

alford-lake-camp.author: Alum
alford-lake-camp.text: ALC's simplicity is its genius. I still hear 'I can and I will' in my head before hard things.

camp-somerset-for-girls.author: Parent
camp-somerset-for-girls.text: The facilities are brand new and gorgeous - closer to a resort than a rustic camp. Staff energy is off the charts.

camp-somerset-for-girls.author: Parent of a 9-year-old
camp-somerset-for-girls.text: Great first camp with lots of comforts. Still building the multi-generational traditions the older Maine camps have - give it time.

kents-hill-sports-camp.author: Parent of a hockey player
kents-hill-sports-camp.text: Where else can a girl skate on real ice at summer camp in July? Facilities are prep-school quality and coaching is encouraging, not scream-y.

camp-natarswi.author: Parent of a Girl Scout
camp-natarswi.text: Katahdin looming over the waterfront is unforgettable. Affordable, empowering, and genuinely wild. Cabins and washhouses are rustic scout-grade.

camp-pondicherry.author: Mother of a first-time camper
camp-pondicherry.text: A one-week horse session for under what private camps charge per day. My daughter came home confident and smelling like a barn - both wins.

camp-pondicherry.author: Camper, age 13
camp-pondicherry.text: Fun weeks and nice counselors. Food is basic camp food and the showers are cold sometimes.

camp-kippewa.author: Parent
camp-kippewa.text: Small and personal - my daughter was never a number. The two-week option was a lifesaver for a hesitant camper.

camp-wekeela.author: Parent of a brother and sister
camp-wekeela.text: One camp for both kids, and both are obsessed. Directors are hands-on and the international staff bring great energy.

camp-wekeela.author: Alum
camp-wekeela.text: Beautiful lake, big activity list, spirited events. It's grown over the years, so it feels less intimate than it once did.

camp-waziyatah.author: Parent
camp-waziyatah.text: Kids pick their own activities, which kept our picky 12-year-old fully engaged. One-week sessions are a rarity in Maine and great for testing the waters.

camp-waziyatah.author: Alum
camp-waziyatah.text: Fun, friendly, zero snobbery. Facilities are decent rather than fancy. Yes, people still ask about Bug Juice.

hidden-valley-camp-me.author: Parent of a creative kid
hidden-valley-camp-me.text: Llamas, a glassblowing studio, and zero pressure to be sporty. Our art-obsessed son finally found a camp that fits.

hidden-valley-camp-me.author: Alum
hidden-valley-camp-me.text: HVC lets kids be exactly who they are. International campers, creative freedom, and the famous llama walks. Food is vegetarian-friendly and actually good.

camp-med-o-lark.author: Parent of a theater kid
camp-med-o-lark.text: Total elective freedom transformed our reluctant camper into a kid who begged to return. The showcase performances are near-professional.

camp-med-o-lark.author: Alum
camp-med-o-lark.text: A haven for artsy kids who don't fit the sports-camp mold. Traditional camp folks might find the structure loose - that's the point.

maine-teen-camp.author: Parent of a 15-year-old
maine-teen-camp.text: Most camps lose kids at 13; MTC is designed for exactly that age. My son started a band with kids from three countries.

maine-teen-camp.author: Camper, age 16
maine-teen-camp.text: You're treated like a young adult, not a big kid. Nobody's forcing you into color war. First-time campers fit right in.

kingsley-pines-camp.author: Parent
kingsley-pines-camp.text: The choice-based schedule was the selling point, and it delivered - our daughter tried sailing, ceramics, and improv in one session.

kingsley-pines-camp.author: Former counselor
kingsley-pines-camp.text: Friendly, well-supervised, lovely lake. Two-week sessions turn over quickly, so long-arc friendships take a full session or two.

camp-nashoba-north.author: Parent of two campers
camp-nashoba-north.text: The owners are on the ground all day, every day. Coed done right - brothers and sisters together, real friendships across bunks.

camp-north-star-me.author: Parent of a first-timer
camp-north-star-me.text: One-week starter sessions in a state where most camps demand four-plus weeks. The variety kept our kid busy dawn to dusk.

camp-north-star-me.author: Camper, age 13
camp-north-star-me.text: So many activities. Because kids come and go on different session lengths, you say a lot of goodbyes mid-summer.

camp-laurel-south.author: Parent of returning campers
camp-laurel-south.text: Everything is first-class - fields, waterfront, food. Four weeks hits the sweet spot between a taster and a full summer.

camp-laurel-south.author: Alum
camp-laurel-south.text: The energy never stops. If your kid thrives on action and spirit events, there's no better fit. Quieter kids may need a beat to adjust.

camp-micah.author: Parent
camp-micah.text: Jewish identity woven in with a light, joyful touch - Shabbat by the lake is beautiful. Directors are true pros.

camp-micah.author: Camper, age 14
camp-micah.text: The ruach (spirit) is unmatched. Song sessions, color war, best friends from all over. Food is solid, bunks get loud.

camp-kingswood.author: Parent
camp-kingswood.text: Jewish camping in Maine without the five-figure price tag. Generous scholarships, kosher kitchen, and a real sense of community.

camp-kingswood.author: Alum
camp-kingswood.text: Kingswood is haimish and fun. Facilities are older than the fancy camps nearby, but nobody I know would trade our summers.

camp-encore-coda.author: Parent of a violinist
camp-encore-coda.text: Half music, half camp - the balance is perfect. Her playing jumped a level and she still came home with lake stories.

camp-encore-coda.author: Alum
camp-encore-coda.text: Jazz band at 10am, sailing at 2pm. Where my musician friends and I finally felt normal. Practice cabins in the pines are magic.

new-england-music-camp.author: Parent of a trumpet player
new-england-music-camp.text: Faculty from major orchestras and conservatories, yet the vibe stays supportive. The outdoor concerts draw the whole community.

new-england-music-camp.author: Alum
new-england-music-camp.text: Transformative musically. It is rehearsal-heavy - kids who want half their day at the waterfront should know the priorities going in.

camp-all-star.author: Parent of a multi-sport kid
camp-all-star.text: Great facilities and the elective model let him mix basketball, hockey, and wakeboarding. Dorm living differs from classic cabin camp - know that going in.

chop-point-camp.author: Parent
chop-point-camp.text: Faith is present but never forced, and the tidal-water setting is unlike any lake camp. Trips to Acadia and Katahdin were highlights.

chop-point-camp.author: Alum
chop-point-camp.text: Small enough that everyone matters. Sailing on the Kennebec taught me more than any classroom. Simple bunks, big hearts.

camp-bishopswood.author: Parent
camp-bishopswood.text: Under two grand for two weeks on one of Maine's prettiest lakes. Inclusive, kind, and low-key spiritual - our non-religious kid felt at home.

camp-mechuwana.author: Parent
camp-mechuwana.text: Week-long sessions under $800 make camp possible for our family. Sweet, sincere staff and a genuinely welcoming atmosphere.

camp-mechuwana.author: Alum
camp-mechuwana.text: Grew up going every summer. Simple cabins, campfire worship, lake swims. Not fancy, deeply kind.

pilgrim-lodge.author: Parent of an LGBTQ+ teen
pilgrim-lodge.text: The most affirming camp community we've found in Maine. My kid felt safe and celebrated from day one.

fair-haven-camps.author: Parent
fair-haven-camps.text: Old-fashioned Bible camp in the best sense - unplugged, outdoors, and inexpensive. Facilities are dated but well-loved.

camp-lawroweld.author: Parent
camp-lawroweld.text: Beautiful setting under Mt. Blue and a surprisingly full activity list for the price. Vegetarian meals reflect the Adventist tradition.

camp-sebago-me.author: Grateful parent
camp-sebago-me.text: Our kids got a real Sebago Lake summer camp week at essentially no cost. Staff treat every child with dignity.

camp-neofa.author: Parent
camp-neofa.text: Bare-bones but big-hearted. My kids swam, fished, and made friends for less than the cost of a week of daycare.

agassiz-village.author: Boston parent
agassiz-village.text: Sliding-scale pricing made a real Maine camp possible for us. The counselors focus on confidence and it shows.

agassiz-village.author: Alum
agassiz-village.text: Agassiz gave city kids like me the woods, the lake, and adults who believed in us. Buildings are old; mission is timeless.

camp-susan-curtis.author: Maine parent
camp-susan-curtis.text: Completely free, and they treat the kids like the future leaders they are - not charity cases. My daughter's confidence soared.

camp-susan-curtis.author: Alum
camp-susan-curtis.text: Camp Susan Curtis changed my trajectory. The staff kept in touch all year. Forever grateful.

seeds-of-peace-camp.author: Alum from the Middle East program
seeds-of-peace-camp.text: I argued across the table from kids I was raised to fear, then played soccer with them an hour later. It rewires you.

seeds-of-peace-camp.author: Parent of a US camper
seeds-of-peace-camp.text: Half camp, half diplomacy bootcamp. Emotionally intense by design - teens should come ready to engage seriously.

ymca-camp-of-maine.author: Parent
ymca-camp-of-maine.text: A real Maine lake camp for a fraction of the private-camp cost. One-week sessions were perfect for our first-timer.

ymca-camp-of-maine.author: Camper, age 12
ymca-camp-of-maine.text: Super fun and the counselors are hilarious. Cabins are pretty basic and it can get loud at night.

camp-jordan-ymca.author: Downeast parent
camp-jordan-ymca.text: The only real overnight option in our corner of Maine, and it delivers - clean waterfront, caring staff, fair prices.

tanglewood-4h-camp.author: Parent of a nature kid
tanglewood-4h-camp.text: Streams, salamanders, and zero screens. The ecology focus is real - staff are half naturalist, half camp counselor.

tanglewood-4h-camp.author: Camper, age 11
tanglewood-4h-camp.text: We built shelters and swam in the river. Best week of my summer, even with the mosquitoes.

blueberry-cove-camp.author: Parent
blueberry-cove-camp.text: Sixty kids on the actual ocean. Tide pooling, rowing, blueberry pancakes. It's humble and utterly magical.

bryant-pond-4h-camp.author: Maine parent
bryant-pond-4h-camp.text: My son learned fly fishing, canoeing, and firearm safety from actual Maine Guides. Wholesome, practical, affordable.

camp-centerstage.author: Parent of a theater kid
camp-centerstage.text: A full musical staged in twelve days, and every kid gets meaningful stage time. Inclusive of all abilities, which we loved.

acadia-institute-of-oceanography.author: Parent of a future marine biologist
acadia-institute-of-oceanography.text: Actual science on actual boats in Acadia. Our daughter came home talking about salinity gradients. Small, nerdy, wonderful.

acadia-institute-of-oceanography.author: Alum
acadia-institute-of-oceanography.text: AIO put me on the path to a marine bio degree. It's science-first - kids wanting a sports camp should look elsewhere.

hurricane-island-outward-bound.author: Parent of a 16-year-old
hurricane-island-outward-bound.text: Two weeks sleeping on an open boat under the stars, navigating by chart. She came home a different, sturdier person.

hurricane-island-outward-bound.author: Alum
hurricane-island-outward-bound.text: Physically and mentally demanding - wet, cold, occasionally miserable, absolutely transformative. Know what you're signing up for.

pine-tree-camp.author: Parent of a camper who uses a wheelchair
pine-tree-camp.text: The entire waterfront is accessible - my son kayaked for the first time in his life. Staff never say 'can't.' No place like it in Maine.

pine-tree-camp.author: Parent
pine-tree-camp.text: They see the camper, not the diagnosis. Sessions fill fast and we wish there were more weeks.

darrow-wilderness-trip-camp.author: Father of a camper
darrow-wilderness-trip-camp.text: Real expedition canoeing in genuinely remote country - wood canvas canoes, campfires, loons. Base camp comforts are minimal by design.

camp-capella.author: Parent of a camper with complex needs
camp-capella.text: The staff-to-camper ratio and genuine warmth make this the one week a year our whole family exhales.

moose-river-outpost.author: Parent
moose-river-outpost.text: Whitewater rafting and mountain hikes with counselors who take both adventure and faith seriously. The drive to Jackman is long but worth it.

maine-golf-and-tennis-academy.author: Parent of a junior golfer
maine-golf-and-tennis-academy.text: On-site course, daily instruction, and video swing analysis - his handicap dropped four strokes in two weeks.

maine-golf-and-tennis-academy.author: Camper, age 14
maine-golf-and-tennis-academy.text: Great coaching and you play every single day. Less variety than a regular camp - you have to love golf or tennis.

gander-brook-christian-camp.author: Parent
gander-brook-christian-camp.text: Sincere, old-fashioned church camp - a cappella singing at campfire is a highlight. Rustic bunkhouses, big community feel.

new-england-frontier-camp.author: Parent
new-england-frontier-camp.text: Strong male mentorship for my son during boys' week - archery, boating, and honest conversations about character.

robin-hood-camp.author: Parent
robin-hood-camp.text: Golf, water-skiing, horses, and sailing taught at a genuinely high level. It's posh and proud of it.

robin-hood-camp.author: Alum
robin-hood-camp.text: Incredible facilities and a jet-set camper mix. Less of the scrappy, muddy Maine camp magic - depends what you want.

camp-belknap.author: Parent of two Belknap boys
camp-belknap.text: The leadership culture is real — older boys genuinely look out for the younger ones. Getting a spot is the hard part; families register absurdly early.

camp-belknap.author: Alum from the 2000s
camp-belknap.text: Twenty years later my closest friends are still Belknap guys. It's rustic and screen-free, and that's the whole point.

camp-belknap.author: First-time camp parent
camp-belknap.text: Wonderful staff and values, though the waitlist and old-school facilities aren't for everyone. Our son came home more independent.

camp-winaukee.author: Parent of an island camper
camp-winaukee.text: The island campus is something special — real independence for the older boys. Sports are taken seriously but the coaches keep it fun.

camp-winaukee.author: Former camper
camp-winaukee.text: Loved the lake and color war intensity. It skews sporty and social, so a kid who isn't into athletics might feel out of step.

camp-kabeyun.author: Parent of a creative kid
camp-kabeyun.text: Our son isn't a jock and Kabeyun was the first camp where he felt fully himself. No forced activities, no cut-throat anything.

camp-kabeyun.author: Longtime alum
camp-kabeyun.text: The freedom is the magic. You learn to fill your own time well. Cabins are genuinely rustic — pack for it.

camp-robindel.author: Mother of a returning camper
camp-robindel.text: The counselors write actual letters home and know every girl. Waterfront program is superb. It's an investment, but she counts the days all year.

camp-robindel.author: Alum and camp parent
camp-robindel.text: Traditions run deep and the friendships last. Full-summer culture means shorter-session kids can feel like visitors.

camp-birchmont.author: Parent of siblings
camp-birchmont.text: Great option for a brother and sister at one camp. Facilities are top-notch and the staff communication was excellent.

camp-birchmont.author: Teen camper
camp-birchmont.text: So many activities you can't do them all. Food was hit or miss but the lake days and trips to Wolfeboro made up for it.

camp-sentinel.author: Church family parent
camp-sentinel.text: Warm staff, unplugged week, and a price that doesn't require a second mortgage. Faith content is present but not heavy-handed.

camp-sentinel.author: Returning camper
camp-sentinel.text: The campfires and the pond are the best. Cabins are pretty basic and it's small, so you see the same people a lot.

william-lawrence-camp.author: Parent of a shy camper
william-lawrence-camp.text: The small size was perfect — staff noticed our son and drew him out. Feels like camp did in 1975, in the best way.

william-lawrence-camp.author: Counselor alum
william-lawrence-camp.text: Tight community and great traditions. Facilities are dated in spots but the boys honestly don't care.

camp-bernadette.author: Catholic school parent
camp-bernadette.text: Sweet, wholesome camp where my daughter learned to sail. Mass is part of the week but the vibe is joyful, not strict.

camp-bernadette.author: Alum from the 90s
camp-bernadette.text: Bernie girls are loyal for life. Cabins are simple and the schedule is fairly set, but the friendships carried me for decades.

camp-fatima.author: Parent of three campers
camp-fatima.text: All my boys have gone and the counselors set a genuinely kind tone. The special-needs week says everything about this place's heart.

camp-fatima.author: Former camper
camp-fatima.text: Great lake, great friends, slightly institutional food. The faith element is there but nobody's pushy about it.

camp-brookwoods.author: Christian homeschool parent
camp-brookwoods.text: Deep faith formation plus real adventure — our son soloed a sailboat and hiked the Whites. Staff are college men of character.

camp-brookwoods.author: Camper dad
camp-brookwoods.text: Excellent program, though families should know the Christian teaching is central, not decorative. Worth it for us.

camp-deer-run.author: Parent of a horse-crazy camper
camp-deer-run.text: She got real saddle time and came home singing worship songs and tying knots. Counselors clearly invest in each girl.

camp-deer-run.author: Alum
camp-deer-run.text: Beautiful spot on the big lake. Days are full and faith-centered; girls wanting a secular camp should look elsewhere.

ymca-camp-lawrence.author: Parent of a first-timer
ymca-camp-lawrence.text: The boat ride to the island sets the tone — total adventure. Affordable, and the staff kept us posted with photos.

ymca-camp-lawrence.author: Alum counselor
ymca-camp-lawrence.text: Island living is unbeatable, though everything from mail to groceries moves on boats, so expect rustic and roll with it.

ymca-camp-nokomis.author: Mom of two campers
ymca-camp-nokomis.text: Both daughters beg to go back. The island makes it feel like a storybook, and staff handled first-week homesickness beautifully.

ymca-camp-nokomis.author: Teen camper
ymca-camp-nokomis.text: Swimming off the docks at sunset is everything. Bathrooms are a walk and showers are quick — island life.

camp-foss.author: Third-generation Foss parent
camp-foss.text: My grandmother went here. The songs haven't changed and neither has the warmth. Reasonably priced for what you get.

camp-foss.author: Camper
camp-foss.text: I love banana boat days and candle boats at the end of session. Wish sessions were longer honestly.

camp-quinebarge.author: Parent of a 2023 camper
camp-quinebarge.text: We hesitated after the bad press from a few summers back, but our daughter had a great, well-supervised session. Animals were the highlight.

camp-quinebarge.author: Former camper
camp-quinebarge.text: Quinebarge is for the kids who don't fit the sporty camp mold. Lumberjacking and the barn made my summers.

camp-quinebarge.author: Cautious parent
camp-quinebarge.text: Sweet concept and kind counselors, but organization and food were uneven our summer. Improving, from what other families say.

camp-deerwood.author: Legacy family parent
camp-deerwood.text: Squam is heaven and Deerwood treats it right. Directors know every boy by name within days. Old-school in all the good ways.

camp-deerwood.author: Alum
camp-deerwood.text: Waterskiing at dawn on glass-flat Squam is a core memory. Not the camp for kids who need constant novelty.

camp-hale-squam.author: Boston parent
camp-hale-squam.text: My son had never left the city and came home talking about loons and swim tests. This camp changes kids' horizons, free of charge.

camp-hale-squam.author: Alum counselor
camp-hale-squam.text: Kids who grow up here come back to staff it. Facilities are simple, mission is huge.

camp-mowglis.author: Parent of a Mowglis boy
camp-mowglis.text: The crew rowing and mountain trips built a work ethic school never did. Uniforms and structure sound stiff but the boys thrive on it.

camp-mowglis.author: Alum from decades back
camp-mowglis.text: Mowglis industriousness stays with you for life. It is intentionally old-fashioned — no screens, cold lake dips, real responsibility.

camp-onaway.author: Onaway alum and mother
camp-onaway.text: Three generations of our family have worn the uniform. The full-season commitment is real, but the depth of friendship is unmatched.

camp-onaway.author: Parent
camp-onaway.text: Beautiful, unplugged, and sincere. Seven weeks is a big first step, so it suits girls who already love being away.

camp-pasquaney.author: Alum spanning generations
camp-pasquaney.text: Pasquaney shaped how I speak, lead, and treat people. The single long session and formal traditions aren't for everyone — that's the point.

camp-pasquaney.author: Parent of a new camper
camp-pasquaney.text: Extraordinary mentorship from returning staff. It is expensive and unapologetically old-fashioned; our son grew enormously.

camp-berea.author: Youth group parent
camp-berea.text: Our whole church sends kids each summer. Production value is high and the gospel message is front and center.

camp-berea.author: Teen camper
camp-berea.text: The lake toys are insane and the speakers actually get teenagers. One week always feels too short.

camp-merriwood.author: Merriwood mother and alum
camp-merriwood.text: The traditions — banquet, songs, team weeks — bond these girls for life. My daughter's confidence doubled in one summer.

camp-merriwood.author: Parent
camp-merriwood.text: Terrific counselors and communication. It's a classic-camp culture with team competition, which our quieter kid took a session to warm to.

camp-moosilauke.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-moosilauke.text: Perfect blend of competition and camp silliness. Coaching is legitimately good, and the mountain trips are a rite of passage.

camp-moosilauke.author: Alum
camp-moosilauke.text: A hundred-plus years of tradition you can feel at evening campfire. Cabins are simple; the pond is glorious.

camp-pemigewassett.author: Parent of a bug-obsessed kid
camp-pemigewassett.text: Pemi's nature lodge is a wonder — our son presented his beetle collection at council fire and nobody laughed. Kindest camp culture we've seen.

camp-pemigewassett.author: Multi-decade alum
camp-pemigewassett.text: Same family running it for a century, and it shows. Not flashy, deeply humane, endless traditions.

camp-merrowvista.author: Parent of a trek camper
camp-merrowvista.text: The four-week bike trek transformed our daughter. Merrowvista is values-driven without being preachy, and pricing is fair.

camp-merrowvista.author: Former village camper
camp-merrowvista.text: Balanced living — craft, sport, and reflection every day. Less lake-centric than Winnipesaukee camps; the pond is small but the trails are endless.

camp-cody.author: Parent of a picky camper
camp-cody.text: Facilities rival a resort and the choice-based schedule kept our son engaged all day. Communication from the office was constant.

camp-cody.author: Two-summer camper
camp-cody.text: Amazing lake and activities. It's big, so you have to put yourself out there to find your people.

camp-robin-hood-nh.author: Toronto camp parent
camp-robin-hood-nh.text: Worth the border crossing every summer. Directors are hands-on and the traditions — like the Robin Hood characters — charm the kids.

camp-robin-hood-nh.author: Alum
camp-robin-hood-nh.text: Great sports and lifelong friends. Full-summer culture dominates, and it's priced accordingly.

camp-tohkomeupog.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-tohkomeupog.text: Tohko boys hike real mountains and come home sturdy. Family ownership means the values never wobble.

camp-tohkomeupog.author: Alum counselor
camp-tohkomeupog.text: The tripping program is the soul of the place. Cabins are bare-bones and the lake is cold in June — badge of honor.

camp-waukeela.author: Waukeela mom
camp-waukeela.text: The 'Waukeela spirit' is real — girls cheer for each other, not against. Communication and photos were excellent all session.

camp-waukeela.author: Alum
camp-waukeela.text: Crystal Lake mornings are unforgettable. It's a traditional girls-camp culture with lots of singing; sporty kids still find their lane.

ymca-camp-huckins.author: Mom who camped here herself
ymca-camp-huckins.text: Signing up is a competitive sport — set an alarm for registration day. Once you're in, it's pure magic and shockingly affordable.

ymca-camp-huckins.author: Parent of a nervous first-timer
ymca-camp-huckins.text: Staff handled homesickness like pros. My daughter came home singing grace before dinner and asking for four weeks next year.

ymca-camp-huckins.author: Teen camper
ymca-camp-huckins.text: Best two weeks of my year, every year. Only gripe is how fast spots disappear for the popular sessions.

camp-calumet.author: Lutheran parent
camp-calumet.text: Calumet meets every kid where they are — including our son with an IEP. Grace-filled, goofy, and affordable.

camp-calumet.author: Longtime counselor
camp-calumet.text: The community sings, the lake sparkles, and campers of every ability are folded in. Facilities are camp-simple.

camp-marist.author: Parent from abroad
camp-marist.text: Our kids flew in from Spain like many campers do — a genuinely international family feel with Mass on Sundays and waterskiing all week.

camp-marist.author: Alum
camp-marist.text: The Brothers create a kind, structured world. Some facilities show their age, but the beach and friendships are timeless.

cragged-mountain-farm.author: Parent of a free-spirit
cragged-mountain-farm.text: No bells, no bugles — kids share chores and build the culture themselves. Our daughter found her whole personality here.

cragged-mountain-farm.author: Alum
cragged-mountain-farm.text: CMF taught me to cook over fire and speak in community meeting. Gloriously muddy and low-tech.

kingswood-camp.author: Dad of a sporty kid
kingswood-camp.text: Every day has real games with real stakes but the counselors keep egos in check. Lake Tarleton is jaw-dropping.

kingswood-camp.author: Camper
kingswood-camp.text: The draft system makes every week fresh. Wifi-free and cabin life is basic, which honestly grew on me.

windsor-mountain-camp.author: Parent of an artsy teen
windsor-mountain-camp.text: Circus, drumming, social justice workshops — a camp for kids who'd rather create than compete. Staff diversity is a genuine strength.

windsor-mountain-camp.author: Alum
windsor-mountain-camp.text: Interlocken-era spirit lived on: hilarious, warm, a little chaotic. Facilities were always secondary to the people.

ymca-camp-takodah.author: Parent of two Takodans
ymca-camp-takodah.text: The staff culture is exceptional — many grew up here. Friendship Fires at the end of session had my kids in happy tears.

ymca-camp-takodah.author: Alum
ymca-camp-takodah.text: Affordable, sincere, and rooted. Cabins are old but cared for, and nobody's checking labels on your gear.

camp-spofford.author: Youth pastor
camp-spofford.text: Our group returns every summer — solid teaching, big lake energy, and staff who remember returning kids by name.

camp-spofford.author: Parent
camp-spofford.text: Wholesome week at a fair price. Worship is central; the packed schedule left our introvert a bit tired.

camp-notre-dame-nh.author: Parent of a first-timer
camp-notre-dame-nh.text: Small enough that the director greeted our daughter by name on day two. Sweet, old-fashioned lake camp.

camp-notre-dame-nh.author: Alum
camp-notre-dame-nh.text: The beach and boat days are the memory-makers. Buildings are vintage; the spirit is fresh.

camp-wiyaka.author: Working parent
camp-wiyaka.text: Real sleepaway camp for under what most day camps charge. Counselors are hometown kids who grew up there and it shows.

camp-wiyaka.author: Alum
camp-wiyaka.text: Wiyaka is scrappy and proud of it. Swim lessons in the pond, s'mores, zero pretension.

camp-glen-brook.author: Waldorf school parent
camp-glen-brook.text: The rhythm of farm chores and handwork settled our anxious kid in a way nothing else has. Food from the garden is remarkable.

camp-glen-brook.author: Camper
camp-glen-brook.text: I made a spoon in the forge and named a calf. Less 'activities' than other camps — more real life.

barbara-c-harris-camp.author: Episcopal parent
barbara-c-harris-camp.text: Inclusive, joyful, and genuinely welcoming to questioning kids. Facilities are newer than most church camps by decades.

barbara-c-harris-camp.author: Camper
barbara-c-harris-camp.text: Chapel is actually fun and the pool plus pond combo is great. One week goes fast.

camp-union-nh.author: Parent
camp-union-nh.text: Small enough that our son never got lost in the shuffle. Lots of European campers made for great cabin conversations.

camp-union-nh.author: Camper
camp-union-nh.text: Loved paddleboarding and the trips to hike Monadnock. Some activities depend on which counselors are around that week.

brantwood-camp.author: Referred family parent
brantwood-camp.text: A counselor called before the session to welcome my son personally. Free, but nothing about it feels bargain-rate.

brantwood-camp.author: Alum
brantwood-camp.text: Brantwood saw something in city kids like me before we saw it ourselves. The hilltop hikes stay with you.

camp-young-judaea-nh.author: Jewish day school parent
camp-young-judaea-nh.text: CYJ gave our kids Jewish joy, not Jewish homework. The Israeli staff connections are lasting and real.

camp-young-judaea-nh.author: Alum
camp-young-judaea-nh.text: Shabbat in whites by the lake is the image I keep. Ruach (spirit) levels are off the charts; introverts adjust in time.

camp-tevya.author: Second-generation Tevya parent
camp-tevya.text: I went, my kids go — the Friday night circle still gives me chills. Strong swim program and warm, veteran leadership.

camp-tevya.author: Camper
camp-tevya.text: Color war and the lake are the best parts. Kosher food is fine, just repetitive by week four.

camp-yavneh.author: Observant parent
camp-yavneh.text: Yavneh threads the needle — kids from Orthodox to Reform homes daven and play ball together. Judaics are substantive, not token.

camp-yavneh.author: Alum
camp-yavneh.text: Kerem summer (the oldest division) is a rite of passage people talk about for decades. Deep friendships, deep roots.

camp-tel-noar.author: Parent of a young camper
camp-tel-noar.text: Tel Noar is the right-sized Jewish camp — our seven-year-old wasn't swallowed up. Waterfront staff were superb.

camp-tel-noar.author: Alum
camp-tel-noar.text: Tight community where everyone knows everyone. Facilities are tidy if not fancy; spirit makes up the difference.

camp-allen-nh.author: Parent of a camper with special needs
camp-allen-nh.text: The only place my daughter is just 'a camper,' not a diagnosis. Staff ratios and patience are extraordinary.

camp-allen-nh.author: Longtime counselor
camp-allen-nh.text: Pure joy, serious care planning behind the scenes. Sessions fill early because families never leave.

camp-carpenter.author: Cub Scout parent
camp-carpenter.text: Perfect training-wheels sleepaway — one week, structured, and the BB-gun range is all my son talked about.

camp-carpenter.author: Den leader
camp-carpenter.text: Well-run program areas and safety-first staff. Dining hall food is scout-camp standard, no more no less.

hidden-valley-scout-reservation.author: Scoutmaster
hidden-valley-scout-reservation.text: Our troop banks a year of merit badges in one week. Waterfront staff run a tight, safe program.

hidden-valley-scout-reservation.author: Scout parent
hidden-valley-scout-reservation.text: Kids came home muddy and proud. Facilities are classic scout-camp rough; bring bug spray by the gallon.

camp-wa-klo.author: Wa-Klo mother
camp-wa-klo.text: The mountain view from the waterfront never gets old, and neither do the traditions. My daughter earned responsibilities she brags about all year.

camp-wa-klo.author: Alum
camp-wa-klo.text: Classic girls-camp rituals and true friendships. Some cabins creak with age — part of the charm, mostly.

ymca-camp-coniston.author: Parent of three campers
ymca-camp-coniston.text: Registration morning is a family event because spots vanish in minutes. Worth it — Coniston counselors are the gold standard.

ymca-camp-coniston.author: Alum
ymca-camp-coniston.text: An entire lake to ourselves and traditions that make you cry at closing campfire. Cabins simple, memories deluxe.

ymca-camp-coniston.author: First-time parent
ymca-camp-coniston.text: Fantastic experience overall; just wish it were easier to get a session. Aid process was dignified and generous.

camps-kenwood-evergreen.author: Parent of a son and daughter
camps-kenwood-evergreen.text: Siblings get their own camps but wave across the pond. Directors talk about social growth like educators, not salespeople.

camps-kenwood-evergreen.author: Alum
camps-kenwood-evergreen.text: Top-shelf sports and waterfront. Full-summer price tag stings, but the community is real.

camp-wilmot.author: Church parent
camp-wilmot.text: Scholarships meant both kids could go. It's humble and homey — hymns at campfire, veggies from the garden.

camp-wilmot.author: Camper
camp-wilmot.text: Small camp where everyone's your friend by Tuesday. Not many fancy activities, but the counselors make it fun.

ymca-camp-mi-te-na.author: Alum father
ymca-camp-mi-te-na.text: Same lake, same chapel point, same values I grew up with. My son's counselor was a former camper, as it should be.

ymca-camp-mi-te-na.author: Parent
ymca-camp-mi-te-na.text: Affordable and sincere. Cabins are old-school YMCA; boys come back happy and filthy.

ymca-camp-lincoln-nh.author: Working parent
ymca-camp-lincoln-nh.text: Bus pickup, real swim instruction, and counselors who greet my kids by name. Summers are solved.

ymca-camp-lincoln-nh.author: Parent of a kindergartner
ymca-camp-lincoln-nh.text: Great first camp. Popular weeks fill early and pond-day logistics can feel chaotic at pickup.

camp-starfish.author: Parent of an ADHD camper
camp-starfish.text: The 1:1 staffing isn't marketing — it's real. First camp that never called us to pick him up early. Life-changing.

camp-starfish.author: Special education teacher
camp-starfish.text: Structure, predictability, and celebration of small wins. Kids who fail elsewhere thrive here.

camp-kettleford.author: Scout parent
camp-kettleford.text: My daughter came home smelling like campfire and confidence. Classic scout skills, sweet unit traditions.

camp-kettleford.author: Non-scout parent
camp-kettleford.text: Welcoming even though we're not a scouting family. Rainy-day plans were a little improvised.

camp-seawood.author: Seacoast parent
camp-seawood.text: Affordable, outdoorsy, and five minutes from home. Counselors kept even the youngest unit engaged.

camp-seawood.author: Parent
camp-seawood.text: Sweet traditional program. No pond swimming on site, so water days are simpler than at bigger camps.

camp-wanakee.author: Methodist parent
camp-wanakee.text: Wanakee's counselors radiate patience. Our anxious kid was welcomed exactly as she is.

camp-wanakee.author: Alum counselor
camp-wanakee.text: Small, muddy, sacred. The theme days and chapel in the pines shaped who I am.

copper-cannon-camp.author: Referred parent
copper-cannon-camp.text: No fees, no fine print — just a phenomenal week my kids would never otherwise get. The staff treat every child with dignity.

copper-cannon-camp.author: Alum
copper-cannon-camp.text: Copper Cannon was the first place I hiked a mountain. Free camp, priceless memories.

kroka-expeditions.author: Parent of an expedition camper
kroka-expeditions.text: Kroka expects a lot — carry your gear, cook your food, mind the group. Our teen came home transformed and asking for the semester program.

kroka-expeditions.author: Alum
kroka-expeditions.text: The realest outdoor education in New England. Not a pampering camp; bring grit.

kroka-expeditions.author: Parent
kroka-expeditions.text: Extraordinary staff and ethics. The rustic farm base and composting toilets are a leap for some kids.

camp-wanocksett.author: Scoutmaster
camp-wanocksett.text: Hiking Monadnock straight from camp is a highlight every year. Program staff are enthusiastic Eagle types.

camp-wanocksett.author: Scout parent
camp-wanocksett.text: Good value week of badges and canoeing. Standard scout-camp grub and platform tents.

camp-timberlake-fw.author: Parent of a Timberlake boy
camp-timberlake-fw.text: He split wood, milked goats, and sat in silent meeting — and loved all of it. No screens, no scores, real community.

camp-timberlake-fw.author: Alum
camp-timberlake-fw.text: The barefoot, shirt-optional wildness is legendary and the values are sincere. Not for kids who need hot showers on demand.

indian-brook-fw.author: Feminist mom
indian-brook-fw.text: Indian Brook gave my daughter a voice and calluses. The community meets kids exactly where they are.

indian-brook-fw.author: Alum
indian-brook-fw.text: Transformative, muddy, and song-filled. The composting toilets and cold pond are a genuine adjustment.

saltash-mountain-fw.author: Parent of a trekker
saltash-mountain-fw.text: Sixty kids, one lake, long trails. The intimacy is the point — everyone matters or nothing works.

saltash-mountain-fw.author: Alum
saltash-mountain-fw.text: My hardest and favorite summers. If your kid hates hiking in rain, look elsewhere.

flying-cloud-fw.author: Parent of a nature kid
flying-cloud-fw.text: He made fire by friction and came home quieter, kinder, and prouder. Total unplug — letters only.

flying-cloud-fw.author: Alum
flying-cloud-fw.text: Living in the woods for six weeks rewires you at 13, in the best way. Truly primitive; know what you're signing up for.

tamarack-farm-fw.author: Parent of a teen farmer
tamarack-farm-fw.text: She drove fence posts, cooked for sixty, and debated community rules in meeting. College felt easy after Tamarack.

tamarack-farm-fw.author: Alum
tamarack-farm-fw.text: Work is real and so is the freedom. Teens who want waterskiing and tournaments should pick a different camp.

brown-ledge-camp.author: Mother of a self-starter
brown-ledge-camp.text: No bugle, no forced rotations — my daughter jumped horses and skied slalom because she chose to, daily. Freedom breeds motivation here.

brown-ledge-camp.author: Alum
brown-ledge-camp.text: BLC taught me to manage my own time before high school did. The champagne fleet of ski boats is legendary.

brown-ledge-camp.author: Parent
brown-ledge-camp.text: Brilliant for independent kids; drifters can flounder without a schedule. Staff do watch for that.

camp-dudley-kiniya.author: Dudley family parent
camp-dudley-kiniya.text: The motto isn't a slogan — girls practice putting others first all day. Hymn sings on the hill are unforgettable.

camp-dudley-kiniya.author: Alum
camp-dudley-kiniya.text: Kiniya blends Dudley tradition with its own century of girls' camp history. Leadership track for teens is exceptional.

ymca-camp-abnaki.author: Vermont parent
ymca-camp-abnaki.text: Honest, affordable boys' camp — swim, paddle, campfire, repeat. Our son grew two inches of confidence.

ymca-camp-abnaki.author: Alum counselor
ymca-camp-abnaki.text: The point on North Hero is magic at dusk. Facilities are vintage Y camp, maintained with love.

camp-hochelaga.author: Alum grandmother
camp-hochelaga.text: Three generations of our women learned to swim off that dock. Prices stay fair because the mission is access.

camp-hochelaga.author: Parent
camp-hochelaga.text: Wonderfully unfussy and empowering. Cabins are snug and the lake wind keeps everyone humble.

camp-thorpe.author: Parent of a camper with Down syndrome
camp-thorpe.text: Thorpe is the week my son plans his year around. He is celebrated there, not accommodated.

camp-thorpe.author: Counselor
camp-thorpe.text: The mountain setting and the mission make it the best job I've had. Small camp, huge heart.

keewaydin-dunmore.author: Tripping family parent
keewaydin-dunmore.text: The progression is brilliant — by 15 they're running rapids in Quebec. Wilderness skills taught like a craft.

keewaydin-dunmore.author: Alum
keewaydin-dunmore.text: Keewaydin men portage. That's the culture, a century deep. In-camp life is rustic and proudly untrendy.

songadeewin-of-keewaydin.author: Parent of a paddler
songadeewin-of-keewaydin.text: Songa girls carry their own canoes, full stop. The confidence that builds is visible by pickup day.

songadeewin-of-keewaydin.author: Alum
songadeewin-of-keewaydin.text: Wilderness trips with an all-girls crew changed my sense of what I could do. Camp songs live rent-free in my head.

point-counterpoint.author: Parent of a violinist
point-counterpoint.text: Conservatory-level coaching without conservatory-level cutthroat. She practiced willingly — by a lake — for the first time ever.

point-counterpoint.author: Alum cellist
point-counterpoint.text: Playing Brahms as the sun sets over Dunmore is peak childhood. Small, intense, and warm.

camp-betsey-cox.author: Parent of a barn kid
camp-betsey-cox.text: Daily riding without show-barn pressure, plus goats, looms, and total acceptance. My daughter calls it her real home.

camp-betsey-cox.author: Alum
camp-betsey-cox.text: Betsey Cox let me be weird and capable at once. Gloriously unfancy — think farmhouse, not resort.

camp-sangamon.author: Parent of a non-sporty son
camp-sangamon.text: No color war, no cuts, no bench. He blacksmithed and built a birdhouse and belonged completely.

camp-sangamon.author: Alum
camp-sangamon.text: The choose-your-own-day freedom taught me initiative. Rustic in the real sense — pack a headlamp.

camp-killooleet.author: Folk-family parent
camp-killooleet.text: Every kid learns guitar or banjo almost by osmosis. Kind, unhurried, and deeply musical — Pete Seeger's spirit lingers.

camp-killooleet.author: Alum
camp-killooleet.text: Seven weeks, one community, hundreds of songs. Not a camp for trophy hunters and proud of it.

night-eagle-wilderness.author: Parent of a woods kid
night-eagle-wilderness.text: Thirty boys, tipis, and zero electricity. He came home knowing plants, knots, and himself.

night-eagle-wilderness.author: Alum
night-eagle-wilderness.text: Closest thing to living in another century. You will get dirty and love it — or you won't, so know your kid.

lochearn-camp.author: Lochearn mom
lochearn-camp.text: White-uniform Sundays and clan songs sound old-fashioned until you watch your daughter glow through them. Superb staff retention.

lochearn-camp.author: Alum
lochearn-camp.text: The lake and the sisterhood are timeless. It's a traditions-heavy culture — girls either adore it or take a session to settle.

camp-billings.author: Upper Valley parent
camp-billings.text: Half the price of the private camps on the same lake and the kids can't tell the difference. Wonderful old dining hall energy.

camp-billings.author: Alum
camp-billings.text: Green and White teams forever. Facilities are worn in places, but the traditions carry it.

aloha-camp.author: Aloha mother and alum
aloha-camp.text: The white dresses on Sunday, the gratitude circle, the loons at dusk — Aloha is a whole value system, gently taught.

aloha-camp.author: Parent
aloha-camp.text: Zero screens, deep traditions, and staff who've returned for decades. Worth the notable price.

aloha-hive.author: Parent of a 7-year-old
aloha-hive.text: Hive is built for little-kid wonder — counselors sang my daughter's homesickness away by night two.

aloha-hive.author: Alum
aloha-hive.text: My Hive summers are why I sent my own girls. Simple, silly, and completely safe-feeling.

camp-lanakila.author: Lanakila parent
camp-lanakila.text: They teach boys emotional honesty without ever calling it that. The Indian-summer traditions and craft shops are superb.

camp-lanakila.author: Alum
camp-lanakila.text: Lanakila made kindness cool decades before that was a slogan. Simple cabins, rich days.

camp-farwell.author: Parent of a rider
camp-farwell.text: Daily saddle time and a barn staff who teach horsemanship, not just riding. Girls from a dozen countries in her cabin.

camp-farwell.author: Alum
camp-farwell.text: Charming and historic with sweet traditions. Small pond rather than a grand lake — the horses make up for it.

camp-farnsworth.author: Scout mom
camp-farnsworth.text: Farnsworth does the campfire-and-canoe classics beautifully and affordably. Unit houses have real history in their walls.

camp-farnsworth.author: Camper
camp-farnsworth.text: The polar bear swims and banana boats are the best. Some cabins are old and spidery, fair warning.

camp-downer.author: Vermont parent
camp-downer.text: Downer is the people's camp of Vermont — cheap, cheerful, and staffed by grown-up campers. Sessions fill fast.

camp-downer.author: Alum
camp-downer.text: State-forest setting, lifelong friends, zero pretension. The dining hall sing-alongs are core memories.

camp-akeela.author: Parent of an autistic camper
camp-akeela.text: The first camp that didn't just tolerate our son — they get him. Staff are trained, ratios are high, and he has friends now.

camp-akeela.author: Camper
camp-akeela.text: Everyone here likes the stuff I like. Dungeons and Dragons AND waterskiing. I never want to leave.

camp-akeela.author: Parent
camp-akeela.text: Wonderful mission, executed well. Tuition is steep, though aid exists and the specialized staffing explains it.

green-mountain-camp.author: Brattleboro parent
green-mountain-camp.text: Sliding-scale pricing meant both daughters could go. Tiny, loving, and delightfully low-tech.

green-mountain-camp.author: Alum
green-mountain-camp.text: Generations of local girls learned campfire cooking on that hill. It's humble and it's ours.

camp-catherine-capers.author: Horse mom
camp-catherine-capers.text: She rode every single day and learned stable management, not just lessons. Sixty campers means everyone matters.

camp-catherine-capers.author: Alum
camp-catherine-capers.text: The barn was my whole world each August. Facilities are simple farmhouse-style; the ponies are the luxury.

kinhaven-music-school.author: Parent of an oboist
kinhaven-music-school.text: Kinhaven is musical rigor wrapped in kindness — no chair rankings, just ensembles that lift everyone. The faculty concerts alone are worth it.

kinhaven-music-school.author: Alum musician
kinhaven-music-school.text: Washing dishes with your quartet then playing Dvorak in a barn — Kinhaven made me a lifelong musician.

shelburne-farms-camp.author: Burlington parent
shelburne-farms-camp.text: My kids milked a cow and tasted their own butter by Friday. Spots evaporate the morning registration opens.

shelburne-farms-camp.author: Parent
shelburne-farms-camp.text: Idyllic setting and gentle educators. Short hours, so working parents need a plan for pickup.

hosmer-point.author: Parent of a rower
hosmer-point.text: Where else does a ten-year-old learn to scull? Food is farm-fresh and the pond is glassy every morning.

hosmer-point.author: Camper
hosmer-point.text: Kingdom Trails biking day and pond swims — I didn't miss my phone once. Okay, once.

smirkus-camp.author: Circus parent
smirkus-camp.text: Professional-grade coaching with zero diva culture. My daughter went from cartwheels to trapeze in two weeks.

smirkus-camp.author: Camper
smirkus-camp.text: The final show under the big top is the best feeling ever. Even meals feel like circus family.

windridge-tennis-camp.author: Tennis dad
windridge-tennis-camp.text: Real stroke development, not babysitting with racquets. Her serve came home transformed.

windridge-tennis-camp.author: Alum
windridge-tennis-camp.text: Generations of New England kids learned tennis at Windridge. Courts are excellent; cabins standard camp fare.

covenant-hills-camp.author: Church parent
covenant-hills-camp.text: Counselors pray with the kids and mud-wrestle with them too. Affordable and heartfelt.

covenant-hills-camp.author: Teen camper
covenant-hills-camp.text: Chapel songs stuck in my head all year, in a good way. Small camp, so bring a friend if you're shy.

bethany-birches-camp.author: Single parent
bethany-birches-camp.text: The sliding scale made camp possible for us, and nobody knows who paid what. Kids sleep in open-air shelters and adore it.

bethany-birches-camp.author: Camper
bethany-birches-camp.text: The pond, the big slide, and cookouts every night. I forgot screens existed.

lotus-lake-camp.author: Montpelier parent
lotus-lake-camp.text: Bus service and a real lake beat any parking-lot camp. My kids learned to kayak by August.

lotus-lake-camp.author: Parent
lotus-lake-camp.text: Sweet old-school day camp. Communication is a bit paper-and-clipboard, which honestly fits the vibe.

camp-outright.author: Parent of a trans camper
camp-outright.text: The first summer program where my kid didn't have to explain themselves. They came home lighter.

camp-outright.author: Camper
camp-outright.text: Talent night made me cry happy tears. One week is not enough — we all say it every year.

roaring-brook-camp.author: Parent of an outdoorsy son
roaring-brook-camp.text: Sixty boys, woodsmoke, and competence-building all day. He sharpened his own knife and my pride.

roaring-brook-camp.author: Alum
roaring-brook-camp.text: Learned more useful skills there than anywhere since. Rustic is an understatement — that's the appeal.

camp-mah-kee-nac.author: Parent of two campers
camp-mah-kee-nac.text: Our boys count down the days all year. Strong athletics, warm counselors, and the lake setting is gorgeous. It is a big investment, but the confidence they come home with is real.

camp-mah-kee-nac.author: Alum from the 1990s
camp-mah-kee-nac.text: The traditions have barely changed and that is the point. Lifelong friends, great coaching, and the Berkshires views never get old.

camp-mah-kee-nac.author: First-summer parent
camp-mah-kee-nac.text: Very sports-forward culture, so a kid who is not athletic may need a minute to find his lane. Communication from camp was excellent though.

camp-taconic.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-taconic.text: Taconic gets the balance right - sporty kids and theater kids both thrive. Facilities are top-notch and the owners know every child by name.

camp-taconic.author: Former counselor
camp-taconic.text: Wonderful community and beautiful campus. Long sessions mean deep friendships, though the price puts it out of reach for many families.

camp-lenox.author: Parent of a camper
camp-lenox.text: If your kid loves sports this is the place - real coaching, great fields, and the lake keeps it feeling like camp rather than a training academy.

camp-lenox.author: Camper, age 14
camp-lenox.text: Color war is unreal and the counselors are hilarious. Bunks are older than the website photos suggest but nobody cares after day two.

camp-emerson-ma.author: Parent of an anxious first-timer
camp-emerson-ma.text: The staff handled homesickness with so much patience. My daughter chose her own activities and came home a different, braver kid.

camp-emerson-ma.author: Two-summer camper
camp-emerson-ma.text: You get to try everything from rocketry to cooking. Smaller than other camps so the sports leagues are not a big deal here.

camp-half-moon.author: Local parent
camp-half-moon.text: Flexible sessions were perfect for our schedule and the lakefront is lovely. Programming is classic camp rather than flashy, which suited us fine.

camp-becket.author: Parent of a longtime camper
camp-becket.text: Becket quietly builds character like nowhere else. No screens, real chores, real friendships. My son calls it his favorite place on earth.

camp-becket.author: Alum and former counselor
camp-becket.text: The values stick with you for life - the quiet chapel talks, the four-week commitment, the brotherhood. Rustic cabins are part of the charm.

camp-becket.author: Parent
camp-becket.text: Wonderful ethos, though the four-week minimum is a big first step for younger boys.

chimney-corners-camp.author: Mother of a CCC girl
chimney-corners-camp.text: My daughter found her truest self here. Strong female role models, beautiful traditions, and zero pressure to be anything but kind.

chimney-corners-camp.author: Alum
chimney-corners-camp.text: Twenty years later my bunkmates are still my best friends. Cabins are simple and the summer flies by too fast.

belvoir-terrace.author: Parent of a dancer
belvoir-terrace.text: The instruction is genuinely professional level - our daughter grew more in six weeks than in a year of classes at home. Gorgeous estate campus.

belvoir-terrace.author: Former camper
belvoir-terrace.text: Amazing if you are serious about your art. Less woodsy free-for-all than typical camp, so know what you are signing up for.

urj-crane-lake-camp.author: Parent of a returning camper
urj-crane-lake-camp.text: Joyful Judaism at its best - my kids come home singing the songs for months. Staff communication is strong and the community is tight.

urj-crane-lake-camp.author: Teen camper
urj-crane-lake-camp.text: Shabbat at the lake is the best feeling. Food is hit or miss but the friends make up for it.

camp-kinderland.author: Third-generation camp parent
camp-kinderland.text: Where else do kids do peace olympics and cannonballs off the dock in the same afternoon? The values-driven community is the real draw.

camp-kinderland.author: Alum
camp-kinderland.text: Deeply formative and famously scrappy - do not expect fancy facilities. The history and songs make it unlike anywhere else.

camp-ramah-new-england.author: Parent of three Ramahniks
camp-ramah-new-england.text: Ramah is a way of life - the davening, the Hebrew, the friendships. Tikvah made camp possible for our son with autism and we are forever grateful.

camp-ramah-new-england.author: Former camper
camp-ramah-new-england.text: Amazing community and epic Yom Sport. Kosher camp food is what it is, and the schedule is fuller than at secular camps.

camp-atwater.author: Parent and alum
camp-atwater.text: Atwater is living history - my father went, I went, now my daughter goes. The pride and mentorship kids absorb here is priceless.

camp-atwater.author: Alum
camp-atwater.text: A national treasure with deep traditions. Facilities are humble compared to pricier camps but the community is unmatched.

camp-howe.author: Parent of a child with ADHD
camp-howe.text: The only camp where my son has ever felt fully accepted. Staff are trained for inclusion and it shows in every interaction.

camp-howe.author: Longtime camper
camp-howe.text: Everyone belongs here, which is rare. Cabins are basic and it rains a lot in the hills, but I would not trade it.

camp-marshall-ma.author: Horse-loving camper's mom
camp-marshall-ma.text: The riding program is a steal compared to private barns, and my daughter adores the goats. Real old-fashioned camp.

camp-marshall-ma.author: Parent
camp-marshall-ma.text: Great value and sweet staff. Facilities are dated but the kids are too busy with the animals to notice.

camp-middlesex-4h.author: Parent of a first-timer
camp-middlesex-4h.text: Affordable, kind, and genuinely outdoorsy. Do not expect resort amenities - it is bug spray and campfires, and my kid loved it.

camp-frank-a-day.author: Newton parent
camp-frank-a-day.text: Perfect first overnight camp - close enough to home, counselors who clearly love the place, and a beautiful lake.

camp-frank-a-day.author: Camper
camp-frank-a-day.text: Banana boat days are the best. Some cabins are pretty old but camp spirit is huge.

ymca-camp-hi-rock.author: Parent
ymca-camp-hi-rock.text: The setting is unreal - a private lake with no road noise, no screens, nothing but woods. Staff come back year after year.

ymca-camp-hi-rock.author: Counselor alum
ymca-camp-hi-rock.text: Magical place to work. It is truly remote, so pack accordingly and do not expect cell service.

camp-wonderland-ma.author: Grateful parent
camp-wonderland-ma.text: We could never afford sleepaway camp, and Wonderland gave my kids a real one. Staff treat every child with dignity.

camp-wonderland-ma.author: Former camper
camp-wonderland-ma.text: Great week with swimming every day. It is faith-based but gentle about it, and the counselors were awesome.

camp-bauercrest.author: Second-generation Crest dad
camp-bauercrest.text: The brotherhood is real - my son's counselors were campers I remember from my own summers. Sports all day, Shabbat together at night.

camp-bauercrest.author: Camper
camp-bauercrest.text: Best hoops of my life and the whole camp knows your name. Bunks are old school and the summer feels too short.

camp-pembroke.author: Pembroke mom and alum
camp-pembroke.text: The singing, the sisterhood, the Friday night white dresses on the hill - it is everything I remember and my daughter is just as hooked.

camp-pembroke.author: Camper
camp-pembroke.text: My favorite place ever, though the lake beach gets crowded on hot days. Counselors feel like big sisters.

camp-avoda.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-avoda.text: Avoda is old-school in the best sense - every kid on a team, every kid known by name. My son begs to go back before we even pick him up.

camp-avoda.author: Alum
camp-avoda.text: Decades of tradition and lifelong friends. Facilities are simple but the leagues and lake are all you need.

camp-haiastan.author: Armenian-American parent
camp-haiastan.text: My kids came home dancing kochari and calling camp friends from three countries. Nothing else keeps the culture alive like this place.

camp-haiastan.author: Alum
camp-haiastan.text: Everyone in the Armenian community knows someone who met their best friend at Haiastan. Simple cabins, huge heart.

clara-barton-camp.author: Parent of a T1D camper
clara-barton-camp.text: The first week since diagnosis that I did not worry every minute. Nurses everywhere, and my daughter met girls who check blood sugar just like her.

clara-barton-camp.author: Camper, age 12
clara-barton-camp.text: Nobody stares when you dose insulin at dinner. Best week of my year, every year.

camp-joslin.author: Dad of a newly diagnosed son
camp-joslin.text: He came home doing his own site changes and talking about ga-ga ball nonstop. The staff, many with T1D themselves, are heroes.

camp-jabberwocky.author: Parent of a camper with cerebral palsy
camp-jabberwocky.text: Jabberwocky treats my son like a star, not a diagnosis. The island embraces these campers like family. There is nothing like it anywhere.

camp-jabberwocky.author: Longtime volunteer counselor
camp-jabberwocky.text: Pure joy, organized chaos, and the best theater on the Vineyard. Spots are very limited, which is the only heartbreak.

camp-bournedale.author: Parent
camp-bournedale.text: Coaches who actually teach, a warm director, and a gorgeous pond. My son grew as an athlete and a kid.

camp-bournedale.author: Camper
camp-bournedale.text: League nights are intense in a fun way. Food is decent, bunks get hot in July.

camp-wing-ma.author: Boston parent
camp-wing-ma.text: Scholarships made camp possible for us, and my daughter was treated exactly like every other kid. Counselors are trained for real-life stuff, not just s'mores.

camp-burgess-and-hayward.author: Parent of two campers
camp-burgess-and-hayward.text: Brother and sister each get their own camp but share the big events. The farm and the pond are highlights, and pricing is fair for the Cape.

camp-burgess-and-hayward.author: Former Hayward camper
camp-burgess-and-hayward.text: Freshwater pond, salty day trips, campfires every week. Cabins are rustic and the mosquitoes are committed.

camp-wingate-kirkland.author: Parent of a nonbinary camper
camp-wingate-kirkland.text: WK walks the walk on inclusion. My kid chose their own activities, made real friends, and felt completely safe being themselves.

camp-wingate-kirkland.author: Camper
camp-wingate-kirkland.text: The beach days and the talent show are the best. It is small, so you know literally everyone by week two.

camp-farley.author: Cape parent
camp-farley.text: Affordable, welcoming, and the animal barn is my kids' happy place. Feels like the community camp every town should have.

camp-squanto-ma.author: Scoutmaster
camp-squanto-ma.text: Well-run program areas and a solid staff. Dining hall food is scout camp food, but the forest setting is fantastic.

camp-squanto-ma.author: Scout, age 14
camp-squanto-ma.text: Got three merit badges and canoed at sunrise. The week goes by too fast.

camp-resolute-ma.author: Cub Scout parent
camp-resolute-ma.text: Great intro to overnight camping with familiar troop leaders around. Facilities are worn but the program is well organized.

grotonwood.author: Parent of a special-needs camper
grotonwood.text: Their special needs weeks are a godsend - patient staff and real camp activities, not a watered-down version.

grotonwood.author: Church youth group leader
grotonwood.text: Lovely lake and heartfelt worship nights. Cabins could use updating but the spirit is strong.

camp-winnekeag.author: Adventist parent
camp-winnekeag.text: Wonderful blend of adventure and faith - my kids waterskied for the first time and came home singing camp songs.

rowe-center-summer-camp.author: Parent of a quirky teenager
rowe-center-summer-camp.text: Rowe is where kids who feel like outsiders find their people. My son came home more himself than he has ever been.

rowe-center-summer-camp.author: Alum
rowe-center-summer-camp.text: Changed my life as a teen. It is rustic and unconventional - if you want jet skis and trophies, look elsewhere.

bu-tanglewood-institute.author: Parent of a violinist
bu-tanglewood-institute.text: Being surrounded by BSO artists all summer transformed her playing and her ambition. Worth every penny for a committed musician.

bu-tanglewood-institute.author: Alum, now a conservatory student
bu-tanglewood-institute.text: BUTI is where I realized music was my life. The practice expectations are heavy - come ready to work.

walnut-hill-summer-dance.author: Dance parent
walnut-hill-summer-dance.text: Faculty caliber is exceptional and the dorms are lovely. Our daughter left with noticeably cleaner technique and clearer goals.

explo-summer-program.author: Parent of a curious 12-year-old
explo-summer-program.text: He built a robot, argued a mock trial, and made friends from five countries in three weeks. Learning with zero grade anxiety.

explo-summer-program.author: Former student
explo-summer-program.text: Classes are genuinely fun and the campus life feels grown-up. It is not a woodsy camp - more like a preview of college.

andover-summer-session.author: Parent
andover-summer-session.text: Outstanding teaching and a truly international dorm. Our son worked harder than during the school year and loved it.

andover-summer-session.author: Former summer student
andover-summer-session.text: Amazing peers and resources. Just know it is real school with real homework - the fun is squeezed around it.

nmh-summer-session.author: International student parent
nmh-summer-session.text: Supportive teachers and a stunning campus. Our daughter's English improved dramatically and she felt cared for, not processed.

berkshire-soccer-academy.author: Club soccer parent
berkshire-soccer-academy.text: The coaching quality rivals elite ID camps but the girls also get an actual camp experience. Our daughter came home sharper and happier.

camp-favorite-ma.author: Girl Scout mom
camp-favorite-ma.text: Sleeping in platform tents on the Cape for a fraction of private camp prices. Rustic bathrooms, big confidence gains.

camp-sewataro.author: Parent of a kindergartner
camp-sewataro.text: Beautiful grounds, doting counselors, and my daughter learned to actually swim. Enrollment fills fast and it is not cheap.

camp-sewataro.author: Sudbury parent
camp-sewataro.text: Classic camp done well. The bus ride was long from our town, but the ponies won my son over completely.

camp-thoreau-ma.author: Concord parent
camp-thoreau-ma.text: Feels like the childhood summers you read about - frogs, canoes, red-clay ball fields. Staff retention is remarkable.

hale-summer-camp.author: Boston parent
hale-summer-camp.text: An unbelievable amount of real nature 20 minutes from downtown. Swim program is excellent and aid made it affordable for us.

hale-summer-camp.author: Parent
hale-summer-camp.text: My kids love the ponds and trails. It is a big operation, so drop-off lines can test your patience.

camp-harbor-view.author: Dorchester parent
camp-harbor-view.text: The boat ride alone is magic, and it costs five dollars for the month. Mentors stay connected with the kids all year.

camp-harbor-view.author: Former camper
