camp-harbor-view.text: CHV showed me a Boston I had never seen from the water. Spots go quickly and not everyone gets in.

everwood-day-camp.author: Parent of a picky camper
everwood-day-camp.text: My son designs his own day, which ended years of camp complaints. Staff energy is off the charts.

linx-camps.author: Working parent
linx-camps.text: Week-by-week signup and long care hours saved our summer. My kids bounced between basketball and Lego robotics happily.

linx-camps.author: Parent
linx-camps.text: Polished and professional, though it can feel more like enrichment programming than woodsy camp. Great for sampler kids.

nobles-day-camp.author: Dedham parent
nobles-day-camp.text: Facilities are extraordinary and the counselor-to-kid ratio is genuinely good. Sign up early - it fills by winter.

drumlin-farm-camp.author: Parent of an animal kid
drumlin-farm-camp.text: My daughter held a chicken on day one and never looked back. Naturalist staff are the real deal.

drumlin-farm-camp.author: Parent
drumlin-farm-camp.text: Wonderful ethos, but sessions sell out in about a day when registration opens. Set an alarm.

jcc-camp-grossman.author: Parent
jcc-camp-grossman.text: Friday song sessions are adorable and the inclusion staff supported our son's IEP needs without making him feel different.

brewster-day-camp.author: Summer visitor from NYC
brewster-day-camp.text: We enroll the kids every August during our Cape rental. Flexible weeks, beach trips, and the staff remember them each year.

camp-hazen-ymca.author: Parent of a nervous first-timer
camp-hazen-ymca.text: Counselors called us proactively during week one and by pickup our daughter refused to leave. The Y values are lived, not laminated.

camp-hazen-ymca.author: Camper
camp-hazen-ymca.text: Cedar Lake is awesome and the banana boat is legendary. Dining hall gets loud in the best way.

camp-hazen-ymca.author: Parent
camp-hazen-ymca.text: Great program, rustic cabins, and sessions book up by February. Plan ahead.

incarnation-camp.author: NYC parent
incarnation-camp.text: Kids from every borough and background sharing one giant lake since the 1880s. Sliding-scale pricing is real and the community shows it.

incarnation-camp.author: Alum
incarnation-camp.text: Pameacha wilderness trips made me who I am. Facilities are old and proud of it - pack for rustic.

bucks-rock-camp.author: Parent of a creative teen
bucks-rock-camp.text: Total creative freedom with professional mentors in every studio. My kid finally found a place with no tryouts and no bench.

bucks-rock-camp.author: Alum
bucks-rock-camp.text: Glassblowing at 14 changed my life. The unstructured day is heaven for self-starters, but drifters can drift.

camp-awosting.author: Parent
camp-awosting.text: Go-karts, wakeboarding, and a director who knows every boy. The two-week option was perfect for our rookie.

camp-awosting.author: Camper
camp-awosting.text: Best activities of any camp I have tried. Bunks are basic but you barely spend time in them.

camp-chinqueka.author: Mother of two campers
camp-chinqueka.text: My daughters waterski, ride, and do ceramics in one day. Small enough that shy kids do not get lost.

camp-washington-ct.author: Episcopal parent
camp-washington-ct.text: Gentle faith, zero judgment, and counselors who model real kindness. My non-churchy kid felt completely at home.

ymca-camp-jewell.author: Parent
ymca-camp-jewell.text: The riding program made my daughter's summer, and financial aid made it possible. Old camp done right.

ymca-camp-jewell.author: Former counselor
ymca-camp-jewell.text: Wonderful community and gorgeous setting. Cabins and dining hall show their age - part of the charm, mostly.

ymca-camp-woodstock.author: Third-generation camp family
ymca-camp-woodstock.text: My grandfather's name is carved in the dining hall. The traditions and the pond have not changed, and that is exactly why we return.

camp-sloane-ymca.author: Parent
camp-sloane-ymca.text: Tent living sounded scary and became my son's favorite thing. Campers and staff from all over the world.

camp-sloane-ymca.author: Camper
camp-sloane-ymca.text: Tenting is awesome except during thunderstorms. The lake and arts barn are great.

ymca-camp-mohawk.author: Mohawk alum and mom
ymca-camp-mohawk.text: The same songs, the same lake, the same feeling of being completely accepted. My daughter is the third generation here.

camp-workcoeman.author: Scout dad
camp-workcoeman.text: West Hill Pond is the cleanest lake in the state and the program is well drilled. Bring a fan for the platform tents.

camp-sequassen.author: Scoutmaster
camp-sequassen.text: Solid staff and strong waterfront. Facilities vary site to site, but the scouting program delivers.

camp-laurelwood.author: New Haven area parent
camp-laurelwood.text: Close to home, deeply haimish, and my kids' camp friends became our family friends. Havdalah on the lake is beautiful.

hole-in-the-wall-gang-camp.author: Parent of a camper in treatment
hole-in-the-wall-gang-camp.text: For one week my son was a kid first and a patient second. The med team handled everything invisibly. We owe this place everything.

hole-in-the-wall-gang-camp.author: Volunteer counselor
hole-in-the-wall-gang-camp.text: The most intentional, joyful place I have ever worked. Every detail exists so sick kids can just play.

channel-3-kids-camp.author: Hartford parent
channel-3-kids-camp.text: They found a scholarship for both my kids and treated us like family, not charity. Real swimming, real campfires, real counselors.

camp-horizons-ct.author: Parent of a camper with Down syndrome
camp-horizons-ct.text: The staff ratio means my daughter is truly known, not just supervised. She talks about her counselors all year long.

camp-claire-ct.author: Parent
camp-claire-ct.text: Eighty kids, a river dock, and counselors who grew up there. It is humble and completely magical.

camp-asto-wamah.author: Alum and camp parent
camp-asto-wamah.text: Same cabins, same lake, same songs I sang in the eighties. My kids love it precisely because nothing is fancy.

camps-kenwood-kenmont.author: Parent of a son and daughter
camps-kenwood-kenmont.text: Each kid gets their own camp but they wave at each other across the lake. Facilities and food are a cut above.

camps-kenwood-kenmont.author: Camper
camps-kenwood-kenmont.text: Great sports and the lake toys are awesome. Wish sessions were longer honestly.

camp-wah-nee.author: Westchester parent
camp-wah-nee.text: Wah-Nee is my kids' entire personality from June to August. Huge spirit, tight friendships, counselors who genuinely care.

camp-wah-nee.author: Alum
camp-wah-nee.text: Color war and the social scene are unmatched. It leans social and sporty - quieter kids need a beat to settle in.

camp-hemlocks-ct.author: Parent of a wheelchair user
camp-hemlocks-ct.text: The only camp where every path, dock, and cabin actually works for my son's chair. He gets a real camp week, not an accommodation.

camp-wightman.author: Church parent
camp-wightman.text: Sweet, sincere staff and a pretty lake. Simple accommodations, honest prices, and my kids come home humming worship songs.

choate-summer-programs.author: Parent
choate-summer-programs.text: Superb teaching and a genuinely international cohort. Our daughter left writing at another level and craving boarding school.

choate-summer-programs.author: Former summer student
choate-summer-programs.text: Great campus and classes, though it is school in summer - expect homework, not campfires.

mystic-seaport-sailing-camp.author: Parent of a boat-obsessed kid
mystic-seaport-sailing-camp.text: Sleeping in a hammock on an 1882 tall ship is the coolest thing my son has ever done. Instruction was patient and thorough.

mystic-seaport-sailing-camp.author: Camper
mystic-seaport-sailing-camp.text: Sailing every day was amazing. The ship gets hot at night and you will smell like the river - worth it.

camp-laurel-ct.author: Girl Scout parent
camp-laurel-ct.text: Affordable, empowering, and pleasantly low-tech. The tents and latrines are an adjustment for glamping kids.

camp-ingersoll.author: Middletown parent
camp-ingersoll.text: Counselor energy is unreal - my kids sprint out of the car at drop-off. Flexible weeks fit our chaotic schedule.

winding-trails-day-camp.author: Farmington parent
winding-trails-day-camp.text: I went here, my kids go here, and the pond swims and archery are exactly as great as I remember. Registration fills stunningly fast.

ymca-camp-sloper.author: Southington parent
ymca-camp-sloper.text: Half the town works or camps here in July. Well organized, great value, and the Sloper spirit is a real thing.

camp-courant.author: Hartford parent
camp-courant.text: Free bus, free meals, real swimming lessons. My kids get the summer other kids get, and it costs us nothing. Thank God for this place.

ness-ocean-adventure-camp.author: Parent of a sailor
ness-ocean-adventure-camp.text: Real sailing instruction plus touch tanks and marine science. My daughter went from timid to skippering in two summers.

ymca-camp-fuller.author: Parent
ymca-camp-fuller.text: My kids learned to actually sail, not just ride in a boat. Salt pond location beats any lake. Cabins are barebones - fair warning.

ymca-camp-fuller.author: Alum
ymca-camp-fuller.text: Windsurfing at Fuller in the nineties is still my happy place. The salt air and the sailing program are unchanged.

yawgoog-scout-reservation.author: Eagle Scout alum
yawgoog-scout-reservation.text: A hundred summers of tradition and you feel every one at the Sunday dress parade. Best Scout camp in the Northeast, period.

yawgoog-scout-reservation.author: Scout parent
yawgoog-scout-reservation.text: Well-run, historic, and affordable. It is huge, so quieter kids should stick close to their troop at first.

camp-hoffman-ri.author: Scout mom
camp-hoffman-ri.text: Wonderful outdoorsy confidence-builder. Tents and bugs are part of the deal, and my daughter came home proud of both.

camp-jori.author: Providence parent
camp-jori.text: JORI feels like a family reunion every summer. Inclusive of every kind of Jewish family, and generous with aid.

camp-aldersgate-ri.author: Methodist parent
camp-aldersgate-ri.text: Kind staff, honest prices, and my son's faith questions were welcomed rather than lectured at. A hidden gem.

canonicus-camp.author: Parent
canonicus-camp.text: Beautiful grounds and sincere counselors. Programming is simple - swimming, crafts, chapel - and that simplicity is the appeal.

camp-watchaug.author: Westerly parent
camp-watchaug.text: Pond swims, kayaks, and sandy kids asleep by eight. Exactly what a Rhode Island summer should be.

ymca-camp-westwood-ri.author: Coventry parent
ymca-camp-westwood-ri.text: Solid Y camp with real swimming and boating. Nothing flashy, everything dependable, and the price is right.

camp-ker-anna.author: Cumberland parent
camp-ker-anna.text: Wholesome, well-supervised, and reasonably priced. My kids love the pool and I love the values.

camp-mikell-ga.author: Alum who now sends her kids
camp-mikell-ga.text: Mikell is a second home for a lot of Episcopal families in Georgia. The waterfall, the singing, the sense of belonging - my kids count down the days every year.

camp-mikell-ga.author: Parent of a first-time camper
camp-mikell-ga.text: Warm, inclusive staff and a gorgeous setting. Facilities are older and it's very much church camp rustic, but that's part of the charm.

athens-y-camp-ga.author: Third-generation camp dad
athens-y-camp-ga.text: My grandfather, my father, and now my sons. The traditions and the honor code haven't changed, and that's exactly why we keep going back.

athens-y-camp-ga.author: Former camper
athens-y-camp-ga.text: Old-school in every sense - cold lake, simple cabins, lots of sports. Great for boys who like structure; less so if your kid wants modern comforts.

ywco-camp-for-girls-ga.author: Mom of two campers
ywco-camp-for-girls-ga.text: A gem that Athens families quietly pass down. My girls love the horses and the counselors feel like big sisters. Sign up early - spots go fast.

camp-barney-medintz-ga.author: Parent of returning campers
camp-barney-medintz-ga.text: Barney is legendary in Atlanta for a reason - ruach for days, fantastic waterfront, and staff who genuinely know each kid. Enrollment fills fast.

camp-barney-medintz-ga.author: Former counselor
camp-barney-medintz-ga.text: The village setup keeps a 600-kid camp feeling small. Shabbat on the lake is something campers talk about all year.

camp-barney-medintz-ga.author: Parent
camp-barney-medintz-ga.text: Wonderful community and inclusion services, though it's big and busy - shy kids may need a session to find their footing.

urj-camp-coleman-ga.author: Alum and camp parent
urj-camp-coleman-ga.text: Coleman friendships last decades - the singing, the lake, Shabbat white clothes walking down the hill. My kids now cry when it's time to leave.

urj-camp-coleman-ga.author: Parent
urj-camp-coleman-ga.text: Joyful, warm Reform community. Cabins are rustic and the hills are no joke, but the staff care is top notch.

camp-ramah-darom-ga.author: Parent of a Tikvah camper
camp-ramah-darom-ga.text: Ramah Darom's inclusion program changed our family's summers. Beautiful campus, real Jewish learning without feeling like school.

camp-ramah-darom-ga.author: Teen camper
camp-ramah-darom-ga.text: The lake and the mountain views are unreal. Davening every morning isn't everyone's favorite, but the community makes it worth it.

camp-dixie-ga.author: Parent of returning campers
camp-dixie-ga.text: Camp Dixie feels like 1950 in the best way - kids run barefoot, build things, and come home confident. Not fancy, and that's the point.

camp-dixie-ga.author: Alum
camp-dixie-ga.text: The elective system taught me independence young. Cabins are genuinely rustic, so pack for it.

camp-pinnacle-ga.author: Parent
camp-pinnacle-ga.text: Sweet, faith-centered weeks at a price real families can afford. My daughter came home singing camp songs for a month.

camp-pinnacle-ga.author: Former camper
camp-pinnacle-ga.text: Loved the counselors and the mountain setting. Chapel time is a big part of each day, so know that going in.

camp-juliette-low-ga.author: Mother of a shy camper
camp-juliette-low-ga.text: CJL turned my hesitant daughter into a girl who builds fires and leads hikes. Sleeping in platform tents all session is not for everyone, but she loved it.

camp-juliette-low-ga.author: Alum
camp-juliette-low-ga.text: The mountain, the songs, the sisterhood - decades later my camp friends are still my people.

winshape-camps-ga.author: Parent of two campers
winshape-camps-ga.text: Runs like clockwork - incredible staff, beautiful Berry campus, and yes, Chick-fil-A on the menu. Registration morning is a competitive sport.

winshape-camps-ga.author: Camper
winshape-camps-ga.text: The worship nights and my skill tribe were the best part. Counselors actually hang out with you the whole time.

winshape-camps-ga.author: Parent
winshape-camps-ga.text: Excellent value and organization. It's big and boisterous, and clearly evangelical - a great fit if that's what you want.

camp-woodruff-ga.author: Scoutmaster
camp-woodruff-ga.text: Woodruff sets the standard for Scout camps in the Southeast - staff spirit, program quality, mountain setting. Our troop drives past three closer camps to get here.

camp-woodruff-ga.author: Scout parent
camp-woodruff-ga.text: Fantastic week for the price. It's a real Scout camp though - steep trails, tents, and no coddling.

camp-twin-lakes-ga.author: Parent of a camper with a chronic illness
camp-twin-lakes-ga.text: The one week a year my son is just a kid, not a patient. The adaptive facilities and medical team are extraordinary.

camp-twin-lakes-ga.author: Volunteer counselor
camp-twin-lakes-ga.text: Purpose-built for access - wheelchair-height zip line, adapted pool, all of it. The joy here is unmatched.

camp-glisson-ga.author: Parent
camp-glisson-ga.text: Swimming under Cane Creek Falls is pure magic. Gentle faith formation, counselors who write campers letters afterward - Glisson gets it right.

camp-glisson-ga.author: Alum
camp-glisson-ga.text: Went every summer growing up. Cabins have been upgraded over the years but it keeps its soul.

rock-eagle-4h-ga.author: Parent of a first-timer
rock-eagle-4h-ga.text: Unbeatable price for a real week of camp. It's enormous and scheduled to the minute, which suited my nervous 9-year-old fine.

rock-eagle-4h-ga.author: 4-H alum
rock-eagle-4h-ga.text: The rock eagle mound, the pool, cabin cleanup competitions - core Georgia childhood memories.

camp-westminster-ga.author: Atlanta parent
camp-westminster-ga.text: Twenty minutes from our house but feels like real camp. Horseback program is the highlight, and the gospel focus is genuine without being heavy-handed.

camp-all-american-ga.author: Parent of three
camp-all-american-ga.text: Slick operation - carpool line moves fast, coaches are great, and my kids beg for more weeks. Gospel message is front and center.

camp-all-american-ga.author: Parent
camp-all-american-ga.text: Great sports variety and they handled my son's allergies carefully. It's very big and very loud.

camp-kingfisher-ga.author: Parent of a nature kid
camp-kingfisher-ga.text: My daughter held a snake, canoed the Chattahoochee, and came home filthy and thrilled every day. Book early - weeks fill by March.

high-meadows-camp-ga.author: Longtime camp family
high-meadows-camp-ga.text: The anti-plastic-gym camp: ponies, mud, tie-dye, freedom. Worth every penny and the waitlist.

high-meadows-camp-ga.author: Parent
high-meadows-camp-ga.text: Kids adore it. It is pricey for day camp and pickup logistics take patience.

camp-timber-ridge-ga.author: Parent of a Brownie
camp-timber-ridge-ga.text: Affordable, empowering, and close to the city. Some weeks felt understaffed at pickup, but my daughter had a blast and gained real confidence.

camp-misty-mountain-ga.author: Girl Scout mom
camp-misty-mountain-ga.text: The horse program is the real deal and the counselors were wonderful about homesickness. Great value compared to private camps.

camp-southern-ground-ga.author: Parent of a camper with ADHD
camp-southern-ground-ga.text: First camp where my son wasn't 'the difficult kid.' Staff are trained for neurodiversity and the food is unbelievable for camp.

camp-southern-ground-ga.author: Parent
camp-southern-ground-ga.text: Facilities are nicer than most resorts. One-week sessions only, and demand makes it hard to get multiple weeks.

strong-rock-camp-ga.author: Buckhead parent
strong-rock-camp-ga.text: Feels like a legacy camp even though it's newer - great riding, tidy cabins, strong values. Our kids' counselors were exceptional.

strong-rock-camp-ga.author: Parent
strong-rock-camp-ga.text: Well run and beautiful. Pricier than church camps nearby, and sessions with friends require early planning.

camp-highland-ga.author: Dad of a teenage boy
camp-highland-ga.text: The rare camp that keeps a 15-year-old off his phone happily - wake sports, bikes, and counselors who invest in the kids spiritually.

camp-lookout-ga.author: Chattanooga-area parent
camp-lookout-ga.text: The overlook worship at sunset is worth the drive alone. Humble facilities, huge hearts.

camp-woodmont-ga.author: Parent of a 7-year-old first-timer
camp-woodmont-ga.text: Perfect starter camp - under 100 kids, directors know every child's name by day two, and the homesickness handling was masterful.

camp-woodmont-ga.author: Parent
camp-woodmont-ga.text: Sweet and nurturing. Activities are simpler than big camps, which is exactly right for the younger set.

valley-view-ranch-ga.author: Mom of a horse-obsessed daughter
valley-view-ranch-ga.text: She rode twice a day, learned to tack and care for 'her' horse, and sobbed leaving it. Rustic bunkhouse living - this is a ranch, not a resort.

ymca-camp-high-harbour-ga.author: Parent
ymca-camp-high-harbour-ga.text: Lake Burton is spectacular and the counselor energy is contagious. Register the day it opens or you won't get your week.

ymca-camp-high-harbour-ga.author: Camper
ymca-camp-high-harbour-ga.text: Banana boating and the blob are the best. Cabins get hot in July and the bugle comes early.

bert-adams-scout-camp-ga.author: Assistant Scoutmaster
bert-adams-scout-camp-ga.text: Solid program and easy logistics from metro Atlanta. July heat in the Piedmont is brutal - pick an early week if you can.

camp-nakanawa-tn.author: Fourth-generation Nakanawa family
camp-nakanawa-tn.text: Amazons vs. Valkyries structures a girl's whole summer, and the friendships are lifelong. It's traditional to its core - no phones, no frills, all heart.

camp-nakanawa-tn.author: Parent
camp-nakanawa-tn.text: Extraordinary traditions and leadership development. The long sessions and old-style cabins are a big commitment for a first-timer.

camp-marymount-tn.author: Nashville parent
camp-marymount-tn.text: Marymount is where half of Nashville grew up. Sweet mix of faith and old-fashioned fun, and the staff loyalty says everything - counselors were all campers once.

camp-marymount-tn.author: Alum
camp-marymount-tn.text: Loved the lake and campfire Mass. Cabins are rustic and the summer heat is real, but I'd go back tomorrow.

ymca-camp-widjiwagan-tn.author: Parent of day and overnight campers
ymca-camp-widjiwagan-tn.text: Widji's waterfront is unmatched in middle Tennessee. Facilities feel new, staff are pros. Registration day is a bloodsport.

ymca-camp-widjiwagan-tn.author: Camper
ymca-camp-widjiwagan-tn.text: The lake toys and the giant swing are awesome. Food is actually good too.

ymca-camp-ocoee-tn.author: Chattanooga parent
ymca-camp-ocoee-tn.text: Gorgeous mountain lake setting and hundred-year traditions like Vespers point. My kids love the rafting trips for older campers.

ymca-camp-ocoee-tn.author: Alum
ymca-camp-ocoee-tn.text: Rustic in the best way, though the cabins and bathhouses show their age.

ymca-camp-montvale-tn.author: Knoxville parent
ymca-camp-montvale-tn.text: Great value with a real Smokies feel. Programming is simpler than the big-name camps, which suits younger kids well.

camp-wesley-woods-tn.author: East Tennessee parent
camp-wesley-woods-tn.text: Creek hikes in the Smokies and campfire worship - my son came home calmer and kinder. Facilities are humble church-camp standard.

camp-riva-lake-tn.author: Mother of two campers
camp-riva-lake-tn.text: With only ~80 girls, everyone truly knows everyone. Waterskiing every morning on glassy water - my daughters are ruined for bigger camps.

camp-davy-crockett-tn.author: Scout dad
camp-davy-crockett-tn.text: Good staff and a nice lakefront. Some program areas feel dated, but the boys earned badges and came home happy.

boxwell-scout-reservation-tn.author: Scoutmaster
boxwell-scout-reservation-tn.text: Well-oiled operation with lots of badge options. It's a big place - troops that plan ahead get more out of it.

camp-buck-toms-tn.author: Eagle Scout alum
camp-buck-toms-tn.text: Sunsets over Watts Bar from the parade field are burned into my memory. Great sailing and swimming program.

nacome-camp-tn.author: Presbyterian parent
nacome-camp-tn.text: NaCoMe is gentle camp done right - creek play, porch swings, campfires. My anxious kid thrived.

deer-run-camps-tn.author: Franklin parent
deer-run-camps-tn.text: Counselors pour into the kids and the adventure activities keep tweens engaged. Overnight cabins are comfortable by camp standards.

deer-run-camps-tn.author: Parent
deer-run-camps-tn.text: Great local option. Faith content is substantial - exactly what we wanted, worth knowing for others.

camp-sycamore-hills-tn.author: Girl Scout mom
camp-sycamore-hills-tn.text: Affordable, empowering, and the counselors handled first-week jitters beautifully. Bathhouses are a hike from some units.

camp-tanasi-tn.author: Parent
camp-tanasi-tn.text: Norris Lake is stunning and the girls actually unplug. My daughter earned her kayaking chops here.

john-knox-center-tn.author: Parent
john-knox-center-tn.text: Sailing on Watts Bar and vespers by the water - a lovely, gentle week. Wish sessions were longer than seven days.

indian-creek-camp-tn.author: Adventist parent
indian-creek-camp-tn.text: Waterskiing instruction is genuinely excellent and the price is a gift. Vegetarian food surprised my kids - in a good way.

doe-river-gorge-tn.author: Tri-Cities parent
doe-river-gorge-tn.text: The train ride into the gorge sets the tone - pure adventure all week. Strong gospel emphasis and genuinely fearless programming.

doe-river-gorge-tn.author: Teen camper
doe-river-gorge-tn.text: Cliff jumping and the gorge zip line were insane. Best week of my summer two years running.

gsmi-tremont-tn.author: Parent of a budding biologist
gsmi-tremont-tn.text: She came home able to identify birds by call and desperate to return. Living inside the national park is the real luxury - the bunks certainly aren't.

st-columba-memphis-tn.author: Memphis parent
st-columba-memphis-tn.text: Feels like sleepaway camp in day-camp doses - canoes, mud, campfires. The staff's warmth stands out.

mccallie-sports-camp-tn.author: Parent of a multi-sport kid
mccallie-sports-camp-tn.text: Real coaching, great facilities, air-conditioned dorms - my son improved in three sports and made friends from five states.

mccallie-sports-camp-tn.author: Camper
mccallie-sports-camp-tn.text: Awesome fields and pool. Days are packed, so come ready to hustle.

camp-skyline-ranch-al.author: Mother and Skyline alum
camp-skyline-ranch-al.text: The river, the rock, the songs at Vespers - Skyline sisterhood is real. My daughter now wears my old camp shirt.

camp-skyline-ranch-al.author: Parent
camp-skyline-ranch-al.text: Beautiful traditions and attentive counselors. It's popular, so cabins are cozy-full in peak sessions.

camp-desoto-al.author: Parent
camp-desoto-al.text: DeSoto's culture is the draw: girls cheer for each other constantly. My daughter came home more confident and kinder.

camp-desoto-al.author: Alum
camp-desoto-al.text: The white-dress candlelight service on the last night still gives me chills decades later.

alpine-camp-for-boys-al.author: Birmingham dad
alpine-camp-for-boys-al.text: Alpine builds boys up the old way - skits, sports, river rope swings, and counselors worth imitating. Worth the waitlist.

alpine-camp-for-boys-al.author: Alum counselor
alpine-camp-for-boys-al.text: Working the Line as a counselor after ten camper summers was the honor of my twenties.

camp-laney-al.author: Parent of two Laney boys
camp-laney-al.text: Laney keeps it classic - no screens, tons of sports, letters home on Sundays. My boys count the days all year.

riverview-camp-for-girls-al.author: First-time camp mom
riverview-camp-for-girls-al.text: The one-week option was perfect for our 7-year-old. Riding program and the riverfront are lovely, staff communication superb.

camp-ascca-al.author: Parent of a wheelchair user
camp-ascca-al.text: My son water-skied. Actually skied. ASCCA's staff and adaptive gear make the impossible routine.

camp-winnataska-al.author: Birmingham alum and parent
camp-winnataska-al.text: Kasota Falls, the pageant, hymns in the outdoor chapel - Winnataska hasn't lost a step in a hundred years. My whole family bleeds it.

camp-winnataska-al.author: Parent
camp-winnataska-al.text: Wonderful traditions and price. Cabins are truly rustic, and July weeks fill within days.

ymca-camp-chandler-al.author: Montgomery parent
ymca-camp-chandler-al.text: Lake Jordan waterfront is the star - my kids live for the banana boat. Counselors set a kind tone.

ymca-camp-cosby-al.author: Parent
ymca-camp-cosby-al.text: Great value and my son loved the jet-ski-pulled tubes. Communication was solid; the camp store bill was a surprise.

ymca-camp-cosby-al.author: Camper
ymca-camp-cosby-al.text: The blob and skiing every day. Cabin cheers at dinner get LOUD.

camp-mac-al.author: Parent of a brother-sister pair
camp-mac-al.text: Sending both kids to one camp with separate programs is the dream. Old-fashioned, structured, and completely wholesome.

camp-sumatanga-al.author: Methodist parent
camp-sumatanga-al.text: The hike to the mountain chapel at sunrise is something my daughter still talks about. Sweet staff, simple living.

camp-mcdowell-al.author: Episcopal parent
camp-mcdowell-al.text: McDowell is where every kind of kid belongs - canyon swims, singing, real community. The waterfall hikes seal it.

girl-scout-camp-coleman-al.author: Birmingham parent
girl-scout-camp-coleman-al.text: Convenient and confidence-building. Facilities are older but the program keeps girls busy and proud.

camp-alamisco-al.author: Parent
camp-alamisco-al.text: Ski instruction on Lake Martin for church-camp prices. My kids didn't even miss the burgers.

comer-scout-reservation-al.author: Scout parent
comer-scout-reservation-al.text: Great location and friendly staff. Program areas could use investment, but the boys had a strong week.

camp-fletcher-al.author: Bessemer parent
camp-fletcher-al.text: Affordable, diverse, and genuinely outdoors - creek stomping, archery, campfires. A Birmingham-area treasure.

camp-lee-al.author: Youth group parent
camp-lee-al.text: Our church's kids come home fired up every summer. Standard camp bunks, better-than-average camp food.

space-camp-al.author: Parent of a space-obsessed 11-year-old
space-camp-al.text: The mission simulations are astonishingly realistic - she came home wanting to be a flight director. Worth the trip to Huntsville.

space-camp-al.author: Alum
space-camp-al.text: Went in 1998, still think about my mission patch. The multi-axis trainer alone is worth it.

space-camp-al.author: Parent
space-camp-al.text: Incredible program, but know it's dorm-style and tightly scheduled - more academy than campfire camp.

camp-of-the-rising-son-ms.author: Jackson-area parent
camp-of-the-rising-son-ms.text: Counselors here genuinely love the kids - my son's still writing his. Classic lake camp with a strong gospel core.

twin-lakes-camp-ms.author: Parent
twin-lakes-camp-ms.text: Every kid in Jackson seems to pass through Twin Lakes. Superb waterfront, kind staff, easy drive. Weeks sell out by spring.

twin-lakes-camp-ms.author: Camper
twin-lakes-camp-ms.text: The Blob and the zip into the lake are the best things ever. Cabins get hot but you're never in them.

camp-bratton-green-ms.author: Episcopal parent
camp-bratton-green-ms.text: Bratton-Green feels like family. The special session for campers with disabilities says everything about this place's heart.

camp-lake-stephens-ms.author: Oxford parent
camp-lake-stephens-ms.text: A sweet, well-run week close to home. Nothing flashy - lake, friends, s'mores, faith - and that's plenty.

camp-wesley-pines-ms.author: Parent
camp-wesley-pines-ms.text: Humble and heartfelt. My daughter loved the counselors; pack bug spray for the pines.

camp-hopewell-ms.author: Parent of a shy camper
camp-hopewell-ms.text: Small enough that no kid slips through the cracks. Hopewell was the perfect first overnight week for us.

camp-garaywa-ms.author: Baptist mom
camp-garaywa-ms.text: Three generations of our family have been Garaywa girls. Sweet, structured, scripture-filled weeks.

camp-tiak-ms.author: Scout dad
camp-tiak-ms.text: Hot, piney, and full of tradition. Staff hustle makes up for weathered facilities.

camp-kickapoo-ms.author: Cub Scout parent
camp-kickapoo-ms.text: Great old-school scout property minutes from Jackson. Bring a water bottle - Mississippi summer is not shy.

jacobs-camp-ms.author: Alum from a small Delta town
jacobs-camp-ms.text: For Jewish kids scattered across the South, Jacobs is where you're not the only one. That feeling is priceless.

jacobs-camp-ms.author: Parent
jacobs-camp-ms.text: Warm, spirited, well-staffed. Mississippi summer heat is fierce - the pool schedule matters.

strong-river-camp-ms.author: Multi-generation camp family
strong-river-camp-ms.text: River rope swings, horses, fresh figs off the tree - Strong River is Mississippi summer distilled. Little marketing, all legacy.

camp-iti-kana-ms.author: Girl Scout mom
camp-iti-kana-ms.text: Simple and confidence-building. My daughter came home with new songs and a badge sash full of stories.

ymca-camp-piomingo-ky.author: Louisville parent
ymca-camp-piomingo-ky.text: Piomingo counselors are the secret sauce - silly, kind, and completely invested. My kids never want the two weeks to end.

ymca-camp-piomingo-ky.author: Alum
ymca-camp-piomingo-ky.text: Rustic cabins, epic campfires, decent food. It's aged but the spirit is intact.

ymca-camp-ernst-ky.author: Cincinnati parent
ymca-camp-ernst-ky.text: Ernst is the camp every kid in Cincinnati talks about - the blob videos alone sell it. Waitlists are real; set an alarm for signup day.

ymca-camp-ernst-ky.author: Camper
ymca-camp-ernst-ky.text: Banana boat, giant swing, cabin chants. Ten out of ten, would blob again.

crossings-cedarmore-ky.author: Youth pastor
crossings-cedarmore-ky.text: Our students meet God here every summer. Production quality rivals big conferences, and the staff serve churches well.

crossings-jonathan-creek-ky.author: Church chaperone
crossings-jonathan-creek-ky.text: Kentucky Lake sunsets and a slick program. Dorm-style lodging means bring earplugs.

camp-loucon-ky.author: Methodist parent
camp-loucon-ky.text: Loucon met my daughter exactly where she was. Unhurried, warm, and truly for every kid.

aldersgate-camp-ky.author: Parent
aldersgate-camp-ky.text: Feels like camp from a storybook - creek, woods, campfire. Facilities are basic, hearts are big.

cathedral-domain-ky.author: Episcopal alum
cathedral-domain-ky.text: The wooden cathedral in the pines is magic at evening worship. The Domain welcomes every kid exactly as they are.

lake-cumberland-4h-camp-ky.author: 4-H parent
lake-cumberland-4h-camp-ky.text: Incredible value and my son loved canoeing on the lake. Cinder-block cabins, county-fair energy, zero pretension.

camp-crooked-creek-ky.author: Scoutmaster
camp-crooked-creek-ky.text: Well-staffed program areas and easy logistics from Louisville. Dining hall meals are exactly what you'd expect - fuel, not cuisine.

camp-john-currie-4h-ky.author: West Kentucky parent
camp-john-currie-4h-ky.text: Lakefront location is fantastic for the price. Scheduled to the minute, which kept my first-timer from getting homesick.

camp-nathanael-ky.author: Grandparent of campers
camp-nathanael-ky.text: Generations of Knott County kids have had their best summer weeks here for pocket change. Simple, sincere, unforgettable.

ymca-camp-wewa-fl.author: Orlando parent
ymca-camp-wewa-fl.text: Real overnight camp 30 minutes from Orlando is rare - Wewa delivers with lake fun and sweet traditions. Cabins are old Florida, fans included.

ymca-camp-winona-fl.author: Daytona-area parent
ymca-camp-winona-fl.text: A hundred years of camp magic - my kids waterskied by week's end. Watch for gators is a joke the counselors love a little too much.

ymca-camp-winona-fl.author: Alum
ymca-camp-winona-fl.text: The lake and the campfire circle are timeless. Humidity and mosquitoes are part of the deal.

ymca-camp-immokalee-fl.author: Jacksonville parent
ymca-camp-immokalee-fl.text: Straightforward, affordable lake camp with real history. Some buildings show their age, but the waterfront program shines.

camp-crystal-lake-fl.author: Gainesville parent
camp-crystal-lake-fl.text: Public-school owned, wonderfully unfussy, and the lake is gorgeous. The name gets a laugh, the camp earns the love.

camp-kulaqua-fl.author: Parent
camp-kulaqua-fl.text: The spring, the waterslides, the animals - Kulaqua is basically a Christian summer resort for kids at church-camp prices.

warren-willis-camp-fl.author: UMC parent
warren-willis-camp-fl.text: The counselor band, lake afternoons, small-group depth - Warren Willis sets the bar for church camp in Florida.

warren-willis-camp-fl.author: Alum
warren-willis-camp-fl.text: Ten summers as camper then counselor. That place shaped my faith and my friendships more than anywhere.

camp-cloverleaf-fl.author: 4-H parent
camp-cloverleaf-fl.text: Basic, cheerful, and cheap - my daughter fished every day and loved her county cabin mates.

camp-timpoochee-fl.author: Panhandle parent
camp-timpoochee-fl.text: Sailing on the bay for under $400 - unbeatable. Bunkhouses are spartan; the sunsets are not.

camp-ocala-4h-fl.author: Parent
camp-ocala-4h-fl.text: Sleeping in the national forest is the real amenity. Rustic in every sense - kids came home muddy and proud.

img-academy-camps-fl.author: Parent of a tournament tennis player
img-academy-camps-fl.text: Coaching, facilities, and competition you cannot replicate at home. It is intense and expensive - know your kid wants it.

img-academy-camps-fl.author: Camper
img-academy-camps-fl.text: Trained harder than ever and met athletes from 20 countries. Dorms and food are fine, but you're there to work.

img-academy-camps-fl.author: Parent
img-academy-camps-fl.text: Elite training, corporate feel. Big group sizes in peak weeks meant less individual attention than the price suggests.

camp-weed-fl.author: Episcopal parent
camp-weed-fl.text: Gentle, inclusive, and joyful - my kids adore their counselors and the lake. Classic church camp done with care.

camp-san-pedro-fl.author: Catholic parent
camp-san-pedro-fl.text: Mass by the lake, kind counselors, and genuine unplugged fun 20 minutes from downtown Orlando. Books up early.

camp-shalom-boca-fl.author: Boca parent
camp-shalom-boca-fl.text: Well-oiled machine: great swim instruction, Shabbat sing-alongs, real inclusion support. Bus service makes life easy.

camp-shalom-boca-fl.author: Parent
camp-shalom-boca-fl.text: Kids love it and staff are professional. It's big and the premium specialty tracks cost extra.

camp-live-oak-fl.author: Fort Lauderdale parent
camp-live-oak-fl.text: My kids actually go outside all day - kayaking, sea turtles, shade of the banyans. A relief from screen-heavy camps.

camp-boggy-creek-fl.author: Parent of a camper with epilepsy
camp-boggy-creek-fl.text: Full medical team on site, zero cost, and my daughter finally got a summer camp story of her own. We cried at pickup - happy tears.

camp-wingmann-fl.author: Parent
camp-wingmann-fl.text: Small, sincere, and beautiful under the oaks. My son felt known by name from day one.

circle-f-dude-ranch-fl.author: Parent
circle-f-dude-ranch-fl.text: Horses every day plus lake skiing - my daughter's dream combo. Lots of international campers made it feel special.

circle-f-dude-ranch-fl.author: Alum
circle-f-dude-ranch-fl.text: Rustic Florida ranch living: hot, buggy, and absolutely wonderful.

seacamp-fl.author: Parent of a scuba-certified teen
seacamp-fl.text: She came home dive-certified and determined to study marine biology. Dorms are basic; the reef is the amenity.

seacamp-fl.author: Alum
seacamp-fl.text: Seacamp changed my life trajectory - I'm literally a marine scientist now. The night snorkels are unforgettable.

camp-la-llanada-fl.author: Girl Scout mom
camp-la-llanada-fl.text: Budget-friendly and empowering. Florida summer means everything revolves around the pool and lake, as it should.

florida-elks-youth-camp-fl.author: Sponsored camper's parent
florida-elks-youth-camp-fl.text: Our lodge sponsored my son - a free week with horses, a water park-level pool, and go-karts. He thinks he won the lottery. He kind of did.

camp-ksc-fl.author: Space Coast parent
camp-ksc-fl.text: Where else does camp include a Saturn V and a launch pad tour? Structured and educational - more academy than free play.

sanibel-sea-school-fl.author: Vacationing parent
sanibel-sea-school-fl.text: We plan our Sanibel trips around camp weeks now. Kids came home salty, sandy, and lecturing us about conservation.

camp-agawak.author: Parent of two returning campers
camp-agawak.text: Agawak is the definition of camp spirit — my girls count down the days all year. Communication from the directors is excellent. It's pricey, but the loyalty of alumni families tells you everything.

camp-agawak.author: Alum from the 1990s
camp-agawak.text: The songs, the lake, the lifelong friends — Agawak stays with you. My daughter goes now and the traditions are exactly as I remember them.

clearwater-camp-for-girls.author: Parent of a first-time camper
clearwater-camp-for-girls.text: Clearwater felt like stepping back in time in the best way — wooden sailboats, singing after dinner, no screens. My shy daughter came home confident. Small camp, big heart.

clearwater-camp-for-girls.author: Former camper, now in college
clearwater-camp-for-girls.text: I loved my summers on the island. It's rustic — don't expect fancy facilities — but the counselors and the lake more than make up for it.

red-pine-camp-for-girls.author: Third-generation camp parent
red-pine-camp-for-girls.text: My mother went to Red Pine, I went, and now my daughters go. The staff longevity is remarkable and the waterfront program is superb. Cabins are simple and that's the point.

camp-deerhorn.author: Dad of a 10-year-old
camp-deerhorn.text: Deerhorn genuinely builds gentlemen. Staff culture is warm, not macho, and my son begged to stay longer. The flexible session lengths made trying it easy.

camp-deerhorn.author: Former counselor
camp-deerhorn.text: Best staff culture I've worked in. The Broeren family is hands-on daily. Food is solid camp fare, cabins are open-air and rustic.

camp-horseshoe-wi.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-horseshoe-wi.text: Horseshoe runs like a well-oiled machine — great communication, tons of photos, and my son's confidence on the ballfield and in the cabin grew every summer.

camp-horseshoe-wi.author: Camper, age 14
camp-horseshoe-wi.text: Color Days are the best week of my year. It can get pretty competitive, which I love, but quieter kids might need a minute to find their lane.

camp-ojibwa.author: Alum and now camp dad
camp-ojibwa.text: Ojibwa is a way of life — the Collegiate League taught me more about competing and losing gracefully than anything since. My son is a lifer now too.

camp-ojibwa.author: Parent of a less sporty kid
camp-ojibwa.text: Fantastic energy and traditions, but be honest with yourself: this is a sports camp. Our son who lives for baseball thrived; his artsy brother preferred elsewhere.

camp-menominee.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-menominee.text: Smaller than the other Northwoods sports camps, which we loved — every boy plays, nobody rides the bench. Directors know each kid personally.

camp-kawaga.author: Camp grandfather and alum
camp-kawaga.text: Three generations of our family have sung the same songs on the same point. Kawaga's brotherhood is real and the waterfront at sunset still gets me.

camp-kawaga.author: First-time camp parent
camp-kawaga.text: Wonderful summer overall. Communication was good, though the eight-week commitment and cost took a leap of faith for us.

camp-timberlane-for-boys.author: Mom of a kid who hated sports camps
camp-timberlane-for-boys.text: Timberlane let my son pick his own schedule — sailing and mountain biking every day, zero forced baseball. The counselors are exceptional and he found his people.

camp-timberlane-for-boys.author: Camper, age 13
camp-timberlane-for-boys.text: Best place on earth. Ski show, the Green and Gold, cabin nights. Only bad part is leaving in August.

camp-highlands-for-boys.author: Alum, camper in the 1980s
camp-highlands-for-boys.text: The same family has run Highlands for over a century and it shows — unhurried, honest, and rooted. My boys learned to sail, portage, and shake hands like they mean it.

red-arrow-camp.author: Parent of a returning camper
red-arrow-camp.text: Red Arrow is camp the way it looked 80 years ago — log buildings, real woodcraft, no frills. Small enough that every boy matters. Not for kids who need creature comforts.

chippewa-ranch-camp.author: Mom of a horse-crazy daughter
chippewa-ranch-camp.text: The riding program is the real deal — daily lessons, horse shows, actual horsemanship. Add the lake and the sisterhood and my daughter is a lifer.

chippewa-ranch-camp.author: Former camper
chippewa-ranch-camp.text: Loved my summers there. Cabins get chilly in June and the days are full-on, but the horses and the friendships were everything.

camp-nicolet.author: Parent of a first-time camper
camp-nicolet.text: Nicolet let our nervous 9-year-old ease in gently — she picked her activities and the counselors checked in constantly. Came home taller somehow.

camp-marimeta.author: Parent of two campers
camp-marimeta.text: Marimeta spirit is contagious — my girls sing the songs all winter. Great gymnastics facilities and the sister-cabin traditions are sweet.

camp-marimeta.author: Camper, age 12
camp-marimeta.text: I love ski shows and banquet. Rest hour is boring but honestly I probably need it.

camp-birch-knoll.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-birch-knoll.text: Beautiful lakefront, kind counselors, and my daughter's confidence in the water skyrocketed. Getting there is a haul from Chicago but worth it.

camp-birch-trail.author: Mom of an anxious first-timer
camp-birch-trail.text: Birch Trail's staff handled homesickness like pros — proactive emails, real strategies, zero judgment. The camp's kindness culture isn't a slogan, it's the whole thing.

camp-birch-trail.author: Former counselor
camp-birch-trail.text: The staff training on camper wellbeing was better than my education courses. Girls leave that place lifted up.

camp-woodland-wi.author: Parent of a brother-sister pair
camp-woodland-wi.text: Having our son at Towering Pines and daughter at Woodland was perfect — separate camps, shared campfires. Woodland's riding program is lovely.

towering-pines-camp.author: Dad of a 9-year-old
towering-pines-camp.text: Towering Pines is run by the same family that's had it for decades and it feels like joining their household. Unplugged, sturdy, kind.

camp-nebagamon.author: Alum and camp parent
camp-nebagamon.text: Nebagamon shaped who I am — the trips, the village councils, the total absence of machismo. Watching my son fall for the same place is surreal.

camp-nebagamon.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-nebagamon.text: The tripping program is unmatched and the culture is warm and literate. Yes it's expensive, and yes there's a waitlist — apply early.

camp-manito-wish-ymca.author: Parent of a teen tripper
camp-manito-wish-ymca.text: Manito-wish turned my 15-year-old into someone who can portage a canoe, read a map, and lead a group. The progression from in-camp weeks to big expeditions is brilliant.

camp-manito-wish-ymca.author: Alum, Boundary Waters section
camp-manito-wish-ymca.text: My 21-day trip out of Manito-wish is still the hardest, best thing I've done. Camp itself is simple; the trail is the teacher.

camp-jorn-ymca.author: Parent of two campers
camp-jorn-ymca.text: Camp Jorn gives you the Northwoods experience without the private-camp price tag. Counselors were fantastic; food was standard camp fare.

camp-interlaken-jcc.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-interlaken-jcc.text: Interlaken nails the balance — proudly Jewish but welcoming to every level of observance. Friday nights in whites by the lake made my kid cry happy tears.

camp-interlaken-jcc.author: Camper, age 13
camp-interlaken-jcc.text: Song sessions go SO hard. My whole cabin is my group chat now.

camp-ramah-in-wisconsin.author: Parent of a Tikvah camper
camp-ramah-in-wisconsin.text: Ramah's inclusion program changed our family's life — our son is a full member of camp, not a guest. The Jewish living is rich without being rigid.

camp-ramah-in-wisconsin.author: Alum
camp-ramah-in-wisconsin.text: Deep friendships, great learning, unbeatable ruach. The davening schedule isn't for everyone, but that's kind of the point.

osrui.author: Parent of two campers
osrui.text: OSRUI's song sessions and Shabbat are pure magic, and the unit structure means the experience grows with your kid. Some cabins are showing their age.

osrui.author: Alum, now a songleader
osrui.text: Half the Jewish musicians I know got their start at OSRUI. It's where Reform camping was invented and you can feel it.

bnai-brith-beber-camp.author: Parent of a first-time camper
bnai-brith-beber-camp.text: Beber let our kid pick his own activities and eased him into Jewish camp life without pressure. Facilities are nicer than most — real mattresses!

camp-chi.author: Chicago camp parent
camp-chi.text: Chi is huge, which means every kid finds their niche — ours did horses and ceramics. Buses from Chicago make logistics painless.

camp-chi.author: Camper, age 15
camp-chi.text: Love the lake and Maccabiah. It's a big camp so you have to put yourself out there, but the counselors help.

herzl-camp.author: Twin Cities parent and alum
herzl-camp.text: Herzl is the center of gravity for Midwest Jewish kids — my camp friends stood at my wedding and now our kids bunk together. Ruach for days.

herzl-camp.author: Parent of a returning camper
herzl-camp.text: Wonderful spirit and staff. Facilities are simple and sessions fill fast — register in the fall or you'll be waitlisted.

camp-young-judaea-midwest.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-young-judaea-midwest.text: CYJ Midwest is small enough that the whole camp feels like one bunk. Israeli staff bring real warmth. Facilities are basic but nobody cares.

camp-moshava-wild-rose.author: Parent of three Moshava kids
camp-moshava-wild-rose.text: Mosh is where our kids' Jewish identity comes alive — the singing and dancing are unreal. Bunks are old-school, and the kids wouldn't change a thing.

ymca-camp-anokijig.author: Parent of two campers
ymca-camp-anokijig.text: Anokijig is the best value in Wisconsin camping — horses, sailing, crafts, all in one week. Sign up in January; popular weeks vanish.

ymca-camp-anokijig.author: Former camper
ymca-camp-anokijig.text: 'We care' isn't just their motto, the staff genuinely live it. I did horse camp for five summers.

ymca-camp-minikani.author: Milwaukee parent
ymca-camp-minikani.text: Minikani counselors are rock stars — my kids know all their names and camp songs by heart. Close to the city but feels far away.

phantom-lake-ymca-camp.author: Parent of a first-time camper
phantom-lake-ymca-camp.text: Over 125 years old and it shows in the best way — traditions everywhere, zero pretension, and scholarships that make camp possible for everyone.

ymca-camp-icaghowan.author: St. Paul parent
ymca-camp-icaghowan.text: Icaghowan is small and sweet — my daughter slept in a treehouse cabin and did horseback every day. An hour from the cities, easy first camp.

ymca-camp-st-croix.author: Minneapolis parent
ymca-camp-st-croix.text: St. Croix has that big-hearted Y counselor energy. Our son's cabin canoed the river and came back filthy and delighted. Registration fills quickly.

honey-rock-camp.author: Parent of a teen camper
honey-rock-camp.text: HoneyRock disciples kids without being preachy — the faith conversations happen on the trail and at the campfire. College-age staff are outstanding.

fort-wilderness.author: Parent of three campers
fort-wilderness.text: The frontier vibe is a blast — blacksmithing, horses, canoe trips — and the gospel focus is front and center. Book early; weeks sell out by winter.

camp-forest-springs.author: Parent of a middle schooler
camp-forest-springs.text: Every cabin has both a counselor and a counselor-in-training, so kids get real attention. Our son came home talking about his faith unprompted.

camp-luther-wi.author: Parent from a Wisconsin congregation
camp-luther-wi.text: Camp Luther is our church kids' summer home — joyful worship, campfires, and counselors who remember your kid's name year to year.

camp-phillip.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-phillip.text: Solid Lutheran teaching, sweet staff, and a petting farm — our kids ask to go back before we've left the parking lot.

camp-gray.author: Catholic camp parent
camp-gray.text: Camp Gray's counselors radiate joy — my kids came home with new prayer habits and inside jokes in equal measure. Beautiful bluff country setting.

camp-timber-lee.author: Parent of an animal lover
camp-timber-lee.text: The nature center sealed the deal — snakes, raptors, the works. Faith content is present but gentle. Cabins were warm in July, pack a fan.

covenant-harbor.author: Chicagoland parent
covenant-harbor.text: An hour from home and the kids get a Geneva Lake waterfront most resorts would envy. Great chapel talks, tired happy kids. Register in November, seriously.

camp-wapogasset.author: Parent of two Wapo kids
camp-wapogasset.text: Wapo worship is loud, joyful, and completely kid-owned. Our church sends a bus every summer and the seats are fought over.

wehakee-camp-for-girls.author: Parent of a first-time camper
wehakee-camp-for-girls.text: WeHaKee's choose-your-own-schedule approach was perfect for our independent kid. Faith is present but light-touch; kindness is the real curriculum.

wisconsin-lions-camp.author: Parent of a camper with vision loss
wisconsin-lions-camp.text: The first place my son was just a camper, not 'the blind kid.' Adaptive everything, expert staff, and it costs families nothing. We cried at pickup.

camp-wawbeek.author: Parent of a wheelchair user
camp-wawbeek.text: Wawbeek is built for our daughter, not adapted as an afterthought. She zip-lined. We have the photo on the fridge.

camp-tesomas.author: Scoutmaster of 20 years
camp-tesomas.text: We've sampled camps in four states; Tesomas is the gold standard — dining hall program, staff spirit, merit badge quality. Our troop won't go anywhere else.

tomahawk-scout-reservation.author: Parent and assistant leader
tomahawk-scout-reservation.text: Enormous, well-run operation — sailing, ATVs for older Scouts, strong safety culture. It's big, so quieter kids should stick close to their troop.

ymca-camp-widjiwagan.author: Parent of a three-summer tripper
ymca-camp-widjiwagan.text: Widji trips get progressively wilder — BWCA, then Canada, then the Arctic. My daughter came back from 30 days on a river visibly changed. Not a craft-and-campfire camp.

ymca-camp-widjiwagan.author: Alum, Arctic alumna
ymca-camp-widjiwagan.text: Widji taught me I could carry my own weight, literally and otherwise. The respect-based culture ('Widji ways') stays with you for life.

ymca-camp-menogyn.author: Parent of a 14-year-old
ymca-camp-menogyn.text: You drop your kid at a boat landing and a canoe takes them across — that sets the tone. Menogyn trail staff are extraordinary; my son planned meals, navigated, led.

ymca-camp-warren.author: Twin Cities parent
ymca-camp-warren.text: Warren feels like the 1930s in the best way — wooden sailboats, cabin wood stoves, no electricity in camper cabins. Single-gender sessions were great for our son.

ymca-camp-warren.author: Former camper
ymca-camp-warren.text: Loved the sailing program and Norway Point. Mosquitoes are real, pack accordingly.

ymca-camp-ihduhapi.author: Parent of a nervous 8-year-old
ymca-camp-ihduhapi.text: Perfect starter camp — close enough that we could have rescued her, though she never asked. Counselors sent postcards home. She's begging for two weeks next year.

camp-lincoln-mn.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-lincoln-mn.text: Lincoln lets boys build their own schedule — ours did sailing, golf, and wilderness trips. Flexible session lengths were perfect as he grew into camp.

camp-lake-hubert.author: Mom of two campers
camp-lake-hubert.text: My daughters chose completely different schedules and both thrived. Beautiful grounds, strong traditions, and the staff clearly love the place.

camp-lake-hubert.author: Camper, age 12
camp-lake-hubert.text: Waterskiing every day! The food is fine but the bakery smell in the morning is elite.

camp-kamaji.author: Kamaji alum and camp mom
camp-kamaji.text: Kamaji girls are Kamaji girls forever — the songs, the final banquet, the letters we still write. My daughter picked her own activities from day one and owned her summer.

camp-thunderbird-mn.author: Parent of a brother and sister
camp-thunderbird-mn.text: One drop-off, two camps — the kids get single-gender cabins but share big event nights. Thunderbird's ski show and council fires are highlights of their year.

camp-mishawaka.author: Third-generation camp family
camp-mishawaka.text: Mishawaka is timeless — same lodge, same lake, same values my grandfather knew. The wilderness trips into the BWCA are the crown jewel.

waldsee-concordia.author: Parent of a two-week villager
waldsee-concordia.text: Waldsee is total immersion done playfully — my daughter came home dreaming in German. Four-week credit sessions equal a year of high school language.

waldsee-concordia.author: Former villager
waldsee-concordia.text: The fake border crossing, the bakery, singing in the Gasthof — it's a tiny Germany in the northwoods. I'm a German major because of it.

lac-du-bois-concordia.author: Parent of a returning villager
lac-du-bois-concordia.text: Our son went from classroom French to actually chatting at dinner in one month. Cabin life is standard camp; the language magic is what you pay for.

skogfjorden-concordia.author: Parent with Norwegian roots
skogfjorden-concordia.text: Our kids connected with their great-grandparents' language and came home making lefse. The community feels like a family reunion every summer.

el-lago-del-bosque-concordia.author: Parent of a middle schooler
el-lago-del-bosque-concordia.text: Two weeks in and our daughter's Spanish confidence outpaced two years of school. Dance nights were her favorite thing on earth.

camp-birchwood-for-girls.author: Parent of a horse lover
camp-birchwood-for-girls.text: Riding every single day if she wants it, and she wants it. Small enough that the directors emailed us personal updates.

camp-voyageur.author: Dad of a 13-year-old
camp-voyageur.text: Voyageur is the anti-resort — small, rugged, and centered on real tripping. My son portaged his own canoe and hasn't stopped talking about it.

camp-kooch-i-ching.author: Alum of five summers
camp-kooch-i-ching.text: Kooch trips are legend — 30 days in the Canadian bush by 16. The island, the sauna, the brotherhood. It asks a lot and gives more.

camp-kooch-i-ching.author: Parent of a first-year camper
camp-kooch-i-ching.text: Incredible program, but know what you're signing up for: canvas tents, big water, real wilderness. Our son loved it; his cousin lasted one summer.

camp-ogichi-daa-kwe.author: Mom of a two-summer camper
camp-ogichi-daa-kwe.text: Ogichi girls do the same big trips the boys do, full stop. My daughter came home strong, muddy, and sure of herself.

camp-chippewa-mn.author: Parent of a quiet kid
camp-chippewa-mn.text: Chippewa is small and intentional — no cliques, lots of canoeing, real conversation at meals. The director knows every boy deeply.

camp-olson-ymca.author: Parent of two campers
camp-olson-ymca.text: Olson has that unfussy Y warmth — kids from every background, counselors who show up big, and a gorgeous lake. Great value.

ymca-camp-miller.author: Duluth parent
ymca-camp-miller.text: Camp Miller is where every Duluth kid seems to go, and there's a reason: sweet staff, simple cabins, and a week that feels like a month of childhood.

many-point-scout-camp.author: Troop leader
many-point-scout-camp.text: Many Point runs like clockwork for a camp its size — strong program areas, gorgeous lake, and the staff arena shows are a hoot. Book your troop's site a year out.

camp-ajawah.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-ajawah.text: Ajawah is a time capsule — tents, bugle calls, flag ceremonies — and astonishingly affordable. Kids learn to build fires and look people in the eye.

camp-omega-mn.author: Parent from a partner congregation
camp-omega-mn.text: Omega's counselors are college kids who genuinely love the campers. Solid Lutheran teaching, s'mores every night, very fair price.

camp-shamineau.author: Parent of three campers
camp-shamineau.text: Shamineau packs an unbelievable amount into a week — horses, blob, zipline — and the chapel times are heartfelt, not heavy-handed.

trout-lake-camps.author: Parent of elementary campers
trout-lake-camps.text: The separate campuses by age are genius — our 8-year-old wasn't swallowed by teenagers. Production value is high; kids call it the best week of summer.

camp-lebanon-mn.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-lebanon-mn.text: Lebanon feels like family — same staff faces every year, great food for camp, and my kids' counselors sent them birthday cards in the offseason.

luther-crest-bible-camp.author: Parent from a rural congregation
luther-crest-bible-camp.text: Luther Crest is where our whole church's kids grow up. Simple cabins, big-hearted counselors, and worship on the lake that sticks with you.

camp-courage-true-friends.author: Parent of a camper with cerebral palsy
camp-courage-true-friends.text: Camp Courage is the one week a year my son is fully independent of us — and he owns it. Nurses on site, adaptive everything, zero pity.

camp-knutson.author: Parent of an autistic camper
camp-knutson.text: Camp Knutson was built for kids like mine — sensory-aware staff, gorgeous lakefront, and a week where he was celebrated, not managed.

camp-buckskin.author: Parent of an ADHD camper
camp-buckskin.text: Buckskin's structure is the secret — predictable days, patient staff trained for kids like ours, and real camp fun. First summer ever he wasn't 'the problem kid.'

camp-buckskin.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-buckskin.text: Life-changing for social skills. It's a long session and not cheap, but the growth we saw justified it.

laketrails-base-camp.author: Parent of a teen sailor
laketrails-base-camp.text: Laketrails is barely findable on a map and that's the magic. Small crews, big water, real responsibility. Rustic doesn't begin to cover it.

wolf-ridge-summer-camp.author: Parent of a science kid
wolf-ridge-summer-camp.text: Wolf Ridge is summer camp taught by actual naturalists — banding birds, testing lakes, climbing real cliffs. Dorm-style lodging, spectacular setting.

castaway-club-young-life.author: Parent of a skeptical teenager
castaway-club-young-life.text: Our son went for the jet skis and came home talking about the speaker's message. Facilities are honestly nicer than most resorts we've stayed at.

camp-butwin.author: Twin Cities parent
camp-butwin.text: Butwin feels like overnight camp that lets them sleep at home — real lake, real campfires, Friday Shabbat is adorable. Buses make it easy.

blue-lake-fine-arts-camp.author: Band parent
blue-lake-fine-arts-camp.text: Two weeks of real ensemble playing with excellent conductors, and the final concert made me tear up. Cabins are rustic and the schedule is intense — kids who love music thrive.

blue-lake-fine-arts-camp.author: Alum, three summers
blue-lake-fine-arts-camp.text: Blue Lake made me a serious musician. Lots of rehearsal, less free time than regular camp — know what you're signing up for.

camp-miniwanca.author: Parent of two campers
camp-miniwanca.text: Miniwanca's setting is unreal — dunes, big lake, driftwood councils. The 'my own self at my very best' ethos genuinely shapes how our kids talk about themselves.

lake-of-the-woods-camp.author: Chicago-area parent
lake-of-the-woods-camp.text: LWC is camp with the volume turned up — endless activity choices, gorgeous facilities, and Olympics at the end. Communication and photos were constant.

greenwoods-camp-for-boys.author: Parent of a first-time camper
greenwoods-camp-for-boys.text: Our son tried 20 activities in two weeks — go-karts won. Staff were attentive and the brother-sister setup was perfect for our twins.

camp-walden-mi.author: Parent of a theater kid
camp-walden-mi.text: Walden is where kids get to be fully themselves — my son does musicals AND sailing and nobody blinks. The culture of acceptance is real.

camp-al-gon-quian.author: Ann Arbor parent
camp-al-gon-quian.text: Al-Gon-Quian is wonderfully un-fancy — cold lake mornings, campfire songs, kids running the flag line. Scholarships keep it open to everyone.

camp-hayo-went-ha.author: Dad of a two-summer camper
camp-hayo-went-ha.text: Torch Lake is absurdly beautiful and the tripping program is the real deal — my 14-year-old backpacked Isle Royale. Old camp, old values, in the best way.

camp-arbutus-hayo-went-ha.author: Mom of an adventurous daughter
camp-arbutus-hayo-went-ha.text: Arbutus balances sweet in-camp traditions with legitimately rugged trips. My daughter came home with trail miles and a new spine.

tamarack-camps-camp-maas.author: Detroit-area parent and alum
tamarack-camps-camp-maas.text: Everyone Jewish in metro Detroit has a Tamarack story — it's a rite of passage. My kids' friend groups and identity were forged there like mine were.

tamarack-camps-camp-maas.author: Parent of a first-timer
tamarack-camps-camp-maas.text: Big camp, big energy, great value with scholarship help. Our quiet kid needed a session to find her footing, then never looked back.

camp-sea-gull-mi.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-sea-gull-mi.text: Lake Charlevoix is the star — sailing, skiing, and beach nights. Small enough that the directors greet every kid by name at drop-off.

camp-daggett.author: Northern Michigan parent
camp-daggett.text: Daggett is what camp should be — kind, simple, on water Hemingway wrote about, and priced so real families can go. Local kids grow up counting the days.

camp-leelanau.author: Parent of a camper
camp-leelanau.text: The Leelanau peninsula setting is jaw-dropping and the character emphasis is genuine. Faith basis is gentle; boys of all backgrounds attend.

camp-kohahna.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-kohahna.text: Kohahna's riding program and dune hikes made my daughter's summer. Small, nurturing, and the counselors model real grace.

camp-westminster.author: Detroit-area parent
camp-westminster.text: Higgins Lake water is Caribbean-clear and the staff pastor kids without preaching at them. A humble camp that quietly does everything right.

camp-barakel.author: Parent of three campers
camp-barakel.text: Barakel is half the price of comparable camps because supporters believe in it. Solid Bible teaching, wonderful staff, and my kids' favorite week all year.

springhill-camps-michigan.author: Parent of two campers
springhill-camps-michigan.text: SpringHill is Disney-level camp — our kids slept in a treehouse village and rode ziplines daily. Faith content is enthusiastic and age-appropriate. Popular weeks sell out fast.

springhill-camps-michigan.author: First-time camp parent
springhill-camps-michigan.text: Amazing energy, though it's a big machine — 900 kids a week. Great for outgoing kids; ours took a few days to settle.

camp-geneva-mi.author: West Michigan parent
camp-geneva-mi.text: Camp on an actual Lake Michigan beach — the nightly sunset worship is something kids remember forever. Registration opens and closes in a blink.

camp-roger.author: Grand Rapids parent
camp-roger.text: Camp Roger is muddy-boots Christianity — less hype, more pond dipping and quiet campfires. Exactly what our overscheduled kid needed.

camp-henry-mi.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-henry-mi.text: Henry's counselors write campers letters after the summer ends — that's the kind of place it is. Waterfront is a kid paradise.

ymca-camp-copneconic.author: Mid-Michigan parent
ymca-camp-copneconic.text: Copneconic has been our family's camp for three generations. The ropes course and lake keep kids busy dawn to dusk; scholarships keep it accessible.

ymca-camp-ohiyesa.author: Detroit-area parent
ymca-camp-ohiyesa.text: Ohiyesa is the classic Detroit Y camp — diverse cabins, big traditions, counselors who grew up there. Our daughter left shy and came home singing.

ymca-camp-pendalouan.author: Muskegon-area parent
ymca-camp-pendalouan.text: Pendalouan is small, woodsy, and personal — the director stands at the gate greeting each kid. Solid value for a full-on overnight week.

camp-echo-mi.author: Evanston parent
camp-echo-mi.text: Echo's bus from Evanston is a rite of passage. Wonderfully diverse camper community and the Sunday candlelight ceremony gets everyone misty.

camp-tanuga.author: Detroit-area parent and alum
camp-tanuga.text: Tanuga is a Detroit institution — I went, my kids go, half their bunkmates are my camp friends' kids. Unfussy and completely beloved.

camp-tavor.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-tavor.text: Tavor treats kids as capable people — they run activities, cook, vote on camp issues. My kid found her ideological home. Facilities are humble; spirit is enormous.

camp-cavell.author: Parent of two campers
camp-cavell.text: Lake Huron mornings at Cavell are pure magic. It's an old camp with old cabins and a huge heart — scholarship support is generous.

bay-cliff-health-camp.author: Parent of a camper with hearing loss
bay-cliff-health-camp.text: Bay Cliff gave our son seven weeks of therapy disguised as the best summer of his life. UP community support keeps it essentially free. A Michigan treasure.

fortune-lake-lutheran-camp.author: UP parent
fortune-lake-lutheran-camp.text: Fortune Lake is the UP in camp form — humble, warm, and genuine. Every Yooper Lutheran kid I know grew up going.

michigan-tech-summer-youth.author: Parent of a robotics kid
michigan-tech-summer-youth.text: Actual university labs, actual professors, plus Superior beach nights. Our son came home set on engineering. Dorm life was a fun preview of college.

owasippe-scout-reservation.author: Chicago scoutmaster
owasippe-scout-reservation.text: Over a century of Chicago troops have made the trip and the traditions run deep. Huge property, strong aquatics, and history you can feel at the council ring.

ymca-camp-eberhart.author: Parent of two campers
ymca-camp-eberhart.text: Eberhart nails the classics — cabin skits, horseback, cannonballs off the dock. Multi-generation families everywhere you look.

sherman-lake-ymca-day-camp.author: Kalamazoo parent
sherman-lake-ymca-day-camp.text: Feels like overnight camp packed into a day — lake swims, archery, campfires on Fridays. The character values talk actually sticks at home.

camp-chief-ouray.author: Parent of a first-time camper
camp-chief-ouray.text: Counselors called us mid-session when our son was homesick and handled it beautifully. The Snow Mountain Ranch setting is stunning and the price is fair for what you get.

camp-chief-ouray.author: Returning camper
camp-chief-ouray.text: Loved the overnight campouts and gaga ball. Cabins are pretty basic and it gets cold at night, but that's part of it.

sanborn-western-camps.author: Alum who attended in the 2000s
sanborn-western-camps.text: Sanborn made me love the outdoors for life. Climbing a fourteener at 12 years old with your cabin is something you never forget.

sanborn-western-camps.author: Parent of a High Trails camper
sanborn-western-camps.text: Staff are unusually mature and the trip options are endless. It's pricey and popular sessions fill early, so plan ahead.

sanborn-western-camps.author: Former counselor
sanborn-western-camps.text: Wonderful philosophy and genuinely kind kids. Rustic living isn't for everyone, and the schedule keeps staff running hard.

geneva-glen-camp.author: Third-generation camp parent
geneva-glen-camp.text: There is a reason families come back for generations. The Knighthood ceremony still gives me chills as an adult. Enrollment is genuinely hard to get — set an alarm for registration day.

geneva-glen-camp.author: Teen camper
geneva-glen-camp.text: GG is my favorite place on earth. The traditions sound cheesy until you're in them, then they mean everything.

geneva-glen-camp.author: Parent of a younger camper
geneva-glen-camp.text: Magical program and caring staff. Only gripes are the waitlist and that cabins are close quarters in busy sessions.

colvig-silver-camps.author: Parent of two campers
colvig-silver-camps.text: The trip program is the real deal — our kids came home talking about mesa hikes and river days, not screens. Communication from staff was great.

colvig-silver-camps.author: Alum
colvig-silver-camps.text: Wonderfully unpolished in the best way. Food was hit or miss, but the wilderness trips shaped who I am.

anderson-camps.author: Parent of a horse-crazy camper
anderson-camps.text: Our daughter basically lived at the barn and loved every minute. Real ranch chores and real riding, not pony rides.

anderson-camps.author: Camper
anderson-camps.text: Rafting and the ropes course were awesome. Some of the cabins are old and the days start early, but it grows on you.

camp-granite-lake.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-granite-lake.text: Directors know every kid by name and the choice-based schedule kept our picky kid engaged all session. Facilities are noticeably nicer than most Colorado camps.

camp-granite-lake.author: First-year camper
camp-granite-lake.text: The lake and paddleboarding were the best part. Wish sessions were a little longer honestly.

camp-timberline-co.author: Parent from Texas
camp-timberline-co.text: Our kids beg to go back every summer. Counselors are joyful and intentional, and the mountain setting seals the deal.

camp-timberline-co.author: Teen camper
camp-timberline-co.text: Climbing a fourteener with my cabin was unreal. Worship nights are a big part of it, so know that going in.

eagle-lake-camps.author: Parent of two boys
eagle-lake-camps.text: The counselors invest in kids beyond the activities — ours still writes his counselor letters. The zipline over the lake is legendary.

eagle-lake-camps.author: Camper
eagle-lake-camps.text: So many activities you can't do them all in a week. Cabin time and Bible study are part of every day.

camp-shady-brook.author: Parent using camp scholarships
camp-shady-brook.text: The Y made it affordable for both our kids to go, and they were treated like family. Not fancy, but the staff heart is real.

camp-shady-brook.author: Camper
camp-shady-brook.text: Horseback riding and the mud hike were my favorites. Bathrooms could use an update.

ymca-camp-jackson.author: Pueblo-area parent
ymca-camp-jackson.text: Great value and my daughter felt safe and included. Facilities show their age, but the counselors more than make up for it.

ymca-camp-jackson.author: Alum
ymca-camp-jackson.text: Camp Jackson summers were the highlight of my childhood in Pueblo. Campfire nights under the Spanish Peaks are magic.

jcc-ranch-camp.author: Parent of a returning camper
jcc-ranch-camp.text: Shabbat at Ranch Camp is something special — our kid found her people there. Staff handled her food allergy without a hitch.

jcc-ranch-camp.author: Alum turned counselor
jcc-ranch-camp.text: The horse program and farm make it unlike any other Jewish camp. Dusty and hot some weeks, but that's ranch life.

shwayder-camp.author: Denver parent
shwayder-camp.text: A haven — our kids come home singing the songs for weeks. The altitude takes a day or two of adjustment for flatlander cousins.

shwayder-camp.author: Alum
shwayder-camp.text: Shwayder friendships last decades. Cabins are simple and it snowed one July, which we loved and parents did not.

ramah-in-the-rockies.author: Parent of a camper from the Midwest
ramah-in-the-rockies.text: The only place our kid gets real wilderness and real Jewish life at once. The masa trips build serious confidence.

ramah-in-the-rockies.author: Teen camper
ramah-in-the-rockies.text: Backpacking with tefillot on a mountain is a core memory. Tent living and camp chores aren't for everyone.

camp-idrahaje.author: Parent of three campers
camp-idrahaje.text: Affordable, faith-filled, and our kids count down the days each year. Registration fills fast for the popular weeks.

camp-idrahaje.author: Camper
camp-idrahaje.text: The blob and horse trails are great. Chapel is twice a day, and cabins get crowded in peak weeks.

camp-elim.author: Church youth leader
camp-elim.text: Our kids come back from Elim changed every year. Staff keep the gospel central without being heavy-handed.

camp-elim.author: Camper
camp-elim.text: Loved capture the flag across the whole camp. Food was fine, showers were cold sometimes.

rocky-mountain-mennonite-camp.author: Parent of a quiet kid
rocky-mountain-mennonite-camp.text: Gentle, non-competitive, and genuinely kind culture. Our introvert thrived here in a way he hasn't at bigger camps.

rocky-mountain-mennonite-camp.author: Alum
rocky-mountain-mennonite-camp.text: Beautiful aspen groves and real conversations. Don't expect ziplines and light shows — that's kind of the charm.

highlands-presbyterian-camp.author: Parent
highlands-presbyterian-camp.text: Staff met our anxious first-timer exactly where she was. The setting below Longs Peak is unbeatable for the price.

highlands-presbyterian-camp.author: Camper
highlands-presbyterian-camp.text: The creek hikes and s'mores were great. Rest hour is long and Wi-Fi does not exist, which is the point I guess.

camp-santa-maria.author: Denver parent
camp-santa-maria.text: Scholarships made camp possible for us, and the staff treated our kids with so much dignity. A hidden gem in Grant.

camp-santa-maria.author: Alum
camp-santa-maria.text: Generations of Denver families know Santa Maria. Buildings are old but the traditions and the view are timeless.

sonlight-christian-camp.author: Parent
sonlight-christian-camp.text: Small enough that the directors greeted our son by name at drop-off. The high-country hikes are legitimately stunning.

sonlight-christian-camp.author: Teen camper
sonlight-christian-camp.text: Best week of my year, every year. It's remote — the drive is long — but so worth it.

camp-redcloud.author: Parent
camp-redcloud.text: Staff are outdoorsmen and mentors in equal measure. The setting at 9,000 feet is jaw-dropping.

camp-redcloud.author: Camper
camp-redcloud.text: Horseback in the mountains was incredible. It's far from everything and phones stay home, fair warning.

sky-ranch-ute-trail.author: Parent from Texas
sky-ranch-ute-trail.text: Half our Dallas church sends kids up each June. Well-run, safe, and the mountain views beat any Texas camp.

sky-ranch-ute-trail.author: Camper
sky-ranch-ute-trail.text: The trail rides and cold river jump were highlights. Long bus ride from Texas though.

covenant-heights.author: Parent
covenant-heights.text: Lovely staff and an unbeatable location minutes from RMNP. Program is more traditional than flashy, which suited us.

covenant-heights.author: Camper
covenant-heights.text: We saw elk walk through camp during breakfast. Enough said.

rainbow-trail-lutheran-camp.author: Parent of three RT campers
rainbow-trail-lutheran-camp.text: Counselors radiate joy — our kids sing camp songs all year. Registration opens early and popular weeks go quickly.

rainbow-trail-lutheran-camp.author: Alum
rainbow-trail-lutheran-camp.text: Rainbow Trail shaped my faith and my friendships for life. The Sangres backdrop doesn't hurt either.

rainbow-trail-lutheran-camp.author: Camper
rainbow-trail-lutheran-camp.text: Loved my counselors and the hike day. Cabins are rustic and the road in is washboard forever.

sky-ranch-lutheran-camp.author: Parent
sky-ranch-lutheran-camp.text: Small enough that every counselor knew our daughter within a day. The mountain setting feels genuinely wild.

sky-ranch-lutheran-camp.author: Camper
sky-ranch-lutheran-camp.text: Beautiful and fun, but pack warm — it hailed in July and the cabins have no heat.

frontier-ranch-young-life.author: High school camper
frontier-ranch-young-life.text: Best week of my life, and everyone says that because it's true. The Mount Princeton hike at dawn wrecked me in a good way.

frontier-ranch-young-life.author: Young Life leader
