frontier-ranch-young-life.text: Incredibly well-run program and food. It's a packed schedule with little downtime — kids sleep hard on the bus home.

crooked-creek-ranch-young-life.author: Teen camper
crooked-creek-ranch-young-life.text: The food is better than most restaurants and the ropes course is huge. Club every night got me thinking about big questions.

crooked-creek-ranch-young-life.author: Parent
crooked-creek-ranch-young-life.text: Our skeptical teen came home glowing. You do go through your YL chapter, so plan around their trip dates.

wilderness-ranch-young-life.author: Teen backpacker
wilderness-ranch-young-life.text: Hardest and best thing I've done. Summiting with my crew after four days on trail felt earned in a way normal camp doesn't.

wilderness-ranch-young-life.author: YL leader
wilderness-ranch-young-life.text: Guides are competent and the terrain is spectacular. Kids should actually break in their boots first — some don't.

au-ranches.author: Multi-generation A/U parent
au-ranches.text: Three generations of our family have ridden at the Ranches. The horsemanship program is as good as any in Colorado.

au-ranches.author: Camper
au-ranches.text: Rafting week was amazing. It's a tight community — most campers are Christian Scientists, which shapes the culture.

tomahawk-ranch.author: Parent of a first-year camper
tomahawk-ranch.text: Financial aid was generous and my daughter came home confident and horse-obsessed. Sweet, encouraging staff.

tomahawk-ranch.author: Camper
tomahawk-ranch.text: Riding and the farm animals were the best. Some units sleep in platform tents, so be ready for spiders.

sky-high-ranch.author: Parent
sky-high-ranch.text: My daughter loved the horses and her counselors. Communication before camp could be smoother, but the week itself was great.

sky-high-ranch.author: Camper
sky-high-ranch.text: The stars up there are unreal and my unit felt like sisters by day two.

camp-alexander.author: Scoutmaster
camp-alexander.text: Solid merit badge program and a helpful staff. Dining hall food is standard scout camp fare — kids survive.

camp-alexander.author: Scout
camp-alexander.text: Climbing merit badge on real granite was awesome, and the river is right there.

peaceful-valley-scout-ranch.author: Scout parent
peaceful-valley-scout-ranch.text: Huge property and lots of program options. Afternoon thunderstorms are a given — pack rain gear.

peaceful-valley-scout-ranch.author: Scout
peaceful-valley-scout-ranch.text: Horseback and shotgun were highlights. Long walks between program areas in the sun.

ben-delatour-scout-ranch.author: Scoutmaster
ben-delatour-scout-ranch.text: More rustic and more real than the big front-country camps. Our troop returns every year for the horse trek.

ben-delatour-scout-ranch.author: Scout
ben-delatour-scout-ranch.text: Loved the outpost night. Showers were freezing but the campfires were the best I've had in scouting.

keystone-science-school.author: Parent of a science kid
keystone-science-school.text: Finally a camp where being into science is cool. The staff are basically field biologists who love kids.

keystone-science-school.author: Camper
keystone-science-school.text: Macroinvertebrate day in the river was surprisingly awesome. Altitude headaches the first two days were not.

perry-mansfield-camp.author: Parent of a dancer
perry-mansfield-camp.text: Faculty are working professionals and the training is real, but kids still ride horses and hike. A rare combination.

perry-mansfield-camp.author: Alum
perry-mansfield-camp.text: Dancing in an open-air studio with aspens behind the barre changed my life. Notable alumni history is no accident.

perry-mansfield-camp.author: Camper
perry-mansfield-camp.text: Intense schedule if you're in the performance track — come ready to work.

colorado-mountain-ranch.author: Boulder parent
colorado-mountain-ranch.text: The owning family has run it for generations and it shows. My kids learned to ride and to belong.

colorado-mountain-ranch.author: Alum
colorado-mountain-ranch.text: Pure mountain nostalgia — war canoes, powwows, dusty trails. Facilities are vintage, in both good and creaky ways.

deer-hill-expeditions.author: Parent of a two-time participant
deer-hill-expeditions.text: The service partnership work is genuine, not voluntourism. Our son came home more grounded and grateful.

deer-hill-expeditions.author: Teen participant
deer-hill-expeditions.text: Rafting the San Juan and working alongside Hopi hosts was unforgettable. You will be dirty for weeks and love it.

roundup-river-ranch.author: Parent of a camper with a chronic illness
roundup-river-ranch.text: The first place our son could just be a kid. On-site medical team meant we actually relaxed for a week too.

roundup-river-ranch.author: Camper
roundup-river-ranch.text: Nobody stares at your port or your meds here. Everyone just plays. I count down the days all year.

rocky-mountain-village.author: Parent of a camper who uses a wheelchair
rocky-mountain-village.text: Every single activity was adapted so my daughter could do it all, including the zipline. Staff ratios are incredible.

rocky-mountain-village.author: Counselor
rocky-mountain-village.text: The most meaningful summer job I've had. It's demanding work and the week turnover is fast, but campers' joy is everything.

colorado-lions-camp.author: Parent of a camper with low vision
colorado-lions-camp.text: The Lions subsidize most of the cost and the staff adapt everything. My son feels independent there in a way school never allows.

colorado-lions-camp.author: Counselor
colorado-lions-camp.text: Small camp, huge heart. Facilities are modest but the program is thoughtful and personal.

avid4-adventure-boulder.author: Boulder parent
avid4-adventure-boulder.text: My 8-year-old actually learned to mountain bike, not just ride around a parking lot. Worth the premium price for us.

avid4-adventure-boulder.author: Parent of two
avid4-adventure-boulder.text: Great instructors and real skills. It books up by February and drop-off lines can be chaotic.

thorne-nature-camp.author: Parent of a nature kid
thorne-nature-camp.text: Thorne turned our screen-loving kid into someone who flips rocks looking for salamanders. Gentle, knowledgeable staff.

thorne-nature-camp.author: Parent
thorne-nature-camp.text: Wonderful mission and counselors. Popular weeks sell out fast and there's no camp bus, so carpool up.

renaissance-adventures.author: Parent of a D&D-loving kid
renaissance-adventures.text: The only camp my son has ever begged to extend. Quest leaders are phenomenal with quirky, creative kids.

renaissance-adventures.author: Parent
renaissance-adventures.text: Inclusive and imaginative. Kids are outside all day in summer sun, so sunscreen diligence matters.

camp-shai-denver.author: Denver parent
camp-shai-denver.text: Shabbat sing-alongs on Fridays are adorable and the pool time is generous. Inclusion team supported our son's IEP needs well.

camp-shai-denver.author: Parent
camp-shai-denver.text: Great counselors, easy logistics, and camp spirit for days. Wish the summer-long option cost a bit less.

woodward-copper.author: Parent of a snowboarder
woodward-copper.text: Snowboarding in July plus foam pits — our kid's dream week. Coaching genuinely improved her riding.

woodward-copper.author: Teen camper
woodward-copper.text: The Barn is insane and the coaches are pro. Dorm-style lodging is more ski resort than campfire, if you care.

philmont-scout-ranch.author: Alum of two treks
philmont-scout-ranch.text: Nothing in Scouting compares. The Tooth of Time at sunrise after 11 days on trail is a memory for life.

philmont-scout-ranch.author: Crew advisor
philmont-scout-ranch.text: Impeccably organized for its scale. Treks book out years ahead and the physical requirements are no joke — train first.

philmont-scout-ranch.author: Scout
philmont-scout-ranch.text: Amazing but hard. Trail food gets old and afternoon storms are constant. Still want to go back.

cottonwood-gulch-expeditions.author: Parent of a returning trekker
cottonwood-gulch-expeditions.text: The Gulch is quirky, intellectual, and utterly free of camp cliché. Our daughter came home knowing bird calls and feeling capable.

cottonwood-gulch-expeditions.author: Alum
cottonwood-gulch-expeditions.text: Sleeping under Southwest stars on trek shaped my whole career in science. Bathrooms are outhouses. Worth it.

hummingbird-music-camp.author: Albuquerque band parent
hummingbird-music-camp.text: Half the musicians in our school district grew up going to Hummingbird. The Friday concert in the mountains makes everyone cry a little.

hummingbird-music-camp.author: Alum
hummingbird-music-camp.text: Practicing scales with the Jemez River in the background is a core memory. Cabins are old-school bunkhouses.

camp-stoney.author: Parent
camp-stoney.text: Small and personal — the chaplain knew every kid's name by Tuesday. A gem for families wanting a quieter camp.

camp-stoney.author: Camper
camp-stoney.text: Loved the campfires and the piñon hills. Not a ton of flashy activities, more about the people.

glorieta-adventure-camps.author: Texas parent
glorieta-adventure-camps.text: The facilities and bike trails are top-tier and the staff clearly love kids. Our whole church sends a crew each summer.

glorieta-adventure-camps.author: Teen camper
glorieta-adventure-camps.text: Downhill biking and the blob were awesome. It's big, so you have to put yourself out there to make friends.

camp-mary-white.author: Parent and former camper
camp-mary-white.text: I went in the 90s and my daughter goes now — same starry skies, same sweet traditions. Delightfully unplugged.

camp-mary-white.author: Camper
camp-mary-white.text: Horses and the mountain air are the best. The cabins are really open-air, so you hear every owl.

rancho-del-chaparral.author: New Mexico parent
rancho-del-chaparral.text: Affordable, welcoming, and my daughter adored the wranglers. Some maintenance rough edges, but the program shines.

rancho-del-chaparral.author: Camper
rancho-del-chaparral.text: Riding through the Jemez with my unit was the best week of my summer. The stars go on forever.

friendly-pines-camp.author: Phoenix parent
friendly-pines-camp.text: The escape from Phoenix heat alone is worth it, but the staff are what bring us back. Gentle, organized, and communicative.

friendly-pines-camp.author: Alum
friendly-pines-camp.text: Three generations of my family have gone. The traditions and the pines feel exactly the same, in the best way.

friendly-pines-camp.author: Camper
friendly-pines-camp.text: Horseback and crafts were great. I wished older teens had more options — it skews younger.

orme-summer-camp.author: Parent of a rider
orme-summer-camp.text: Real ranch riding — my daughter helped move cattle at 12. The Orme campus gives it great facilities for a small camp.

orme-summer-camp.author: Camper
orme-summer-camp.text: The horses are the whole point and they deliver. It's hot and dusty midsummer, bring twice the socks.

ymca-camp-sky-y.author: Phoenix parent
ymca-camp-sky-y.text: Sky-Y is where half of Phoenix learned to be away from home. Y scholarships made it work for our budget.

ymca-camp-sky-y.author: Camper
ymca-camp-sky-y.text: Campfire skits and the challenge course were the best. Cabins are old but that's camp.

shadow-rim-ranch.author: Parent
shadow-rim-ranch.text: My shy daughter came home a rider and a leader. Great value compared to private camps.

shadow-rim-ranch.author: Camper
shadow-rim-ranch.text: The horses were sweet and the Rim views are gorgeous. Monsoon storms canceled a ride, which was a bummer.

camp-maripai.author: Parent and Maripai alum
camp-maripai.text: Same songs I sang in the 90s, same magic. My daughter's counselors were patient and fun.

camp-maripai.author: Camper
camp-maripai.text: Loved riding and the craft lodge. Food was fine, the s'mores were better.

prescott-pines-camp.author: Youth pastor
prescott-pines-camp.text: Our group of 40 was handled seamlessly, and the staff's faith is genuine. Kids talk about it all year.

prescott-pines-camp.author: Camper
prescott-pines-camp.text: Zipline and night games were the best. Cabins pack a lot of bunks in.

ucyc-prescott.author: Parent
ucyc-prescott.text: Extremely well-run for its size — check-in, food, safety all dialed. Our kids' favorite week of summer.

ucyc-prescott.author: Teen camper
ucyc-prescott.text: The waterslides and rec games are elite. It's big and loud, which I love and my sister doesn't.

montlure-camp.author: Tucson parent
montlure-camp.text: Greer in July is heaven, and Montlure's small size means real relationships. My kids feel deeply known there.

montlure-camp.author: Alum
montlure-camp.text: The mountain meadows and lodge singing are unforgettable. Simple accommodations — it's about the place and people.

lost-canyon-young-life.author: High schooler
lost-canyon-young-life.text: Felt like a five-star resort built for teenagers. The talks at club hit deeper than I expected.

lost-canyon-young-life.author: YL leader
lost-canyon-young-life.text: Excellence in every detail. The schedule is packed — bring kids ready to go hard all week.

camp-geronimo.author: Scoutmaster
camp-geronimo.text: The Rim setting is the best in Arizona scouting. Staff spirit at campfires is outstanding.

camp-geronimo.author: Scout
camp-geronimo.text: Earned four badges and swam every day. The hike up to camp with full packs is rough in June heat.

camp-raymond.author: Scout parent
camp-raymond.text: Solid classic scout camp at a fair price. Program areas are spread out, so kids get their steps in.

camp-raymond.author: Scout
camp-raymond.text: Climbing and rifle were great. Nights get legitimately cold even in July.

lions-camp-tatiyee.author: Parent of a camper with special needs
lions-camp-tatiyee.text: Free, safe, and full of dignity. Tatiyee is the one week a year my daughter is fully independent, and she glows for months.

lions-camp-tatiyee.author: Counselor
lions-camp-tatiyee.text: The mission is real and the training is thorough. Hardest, best summer of my life.

camp-colley.author: Phoenix parent
camp-colley.text: My son had never seen a forest before Camp Colley. The city subsidy made it possible and the staff made it wonderful.

camp-colley.author: Camper
camp-colley.text: Cool nights, tall trees, new friends. The bus from Phoenix is long but worth it.

camp-wamatochick.author: Parent and alum
camp-wamatochick.text: Wamatochick is old-school camp in the best sense. Small units, big traditions, kind counselors.

camp-wamatochick.author: Camper
camp-wamatochick.text: Council fire night was my favorite. Wish it was two weeks instead of one.

camp-cloud-rim.author: Salt Lake parent
camp-cloud-rim.text: A lake at 9,000 feet is pure magic. My daughter came home confident on a paddleboard and full of camp songs.

camp-cloud-rim.author: Camper
camp-cloud-rim.text: Canoeing every day was awesome. It gets cold at night even in July — bring the warm sleeping bag they tell you to.

trefoil-ranch.author: Parent
trefoil-ranch.text: The wranglers taught real horsemanship, not just trail-following. Great value for a horse camp.

trefoil-ranch.author: Camper
trefoil-ranch.text: I loved my horse and my unit. The hill up to the cabins is a workout.

camp-tuttle.author: Parent
camp-tuttle.text: Tuttle welcomed our kid exactly as they are. Small enough that nobody slips through the cracks.

camp-tuttle.author: Alum
camp-tuttle.text: Wildflower hikes and lodge singing are the memories that stick. Rustic cabins, cold mornings, warm people.

camp-kostopulos.author: Parent of a camper with autism
camp-kostopulos.text: Camp K's staff understand our son in a way few programs do. He rides horses there — something we never thought possible.

camp-kostopulos.author: Counselor
camp-kostopulos.text: Meaningful work and strong training. The canyon site is beautiful, though summer heat can be intense midday.

camp-hobe.author: Parent of a childhood cancer survivor
camp-hobe.text: Hobé lets siblings come too, which healed something in our family. Nurses handle everything so kids just get to play.

camp-hobe.author: Camper
camp-hobe.text: At Camp Hobé being bald is normal and nobody asks questions. Just friends and fun.

camp-steiner.author: Scoutmaster
camp-steiner.text: The altitude and lakes make it unlike any other camp in the state. Cold plunges are a rite of passage.

camp-steiner.author: Scout
camp-steiner.text: Caught my first fish there. Freezing at night, even in July — they are not kidding about the elevation.

woodward-park-city.author: Parent of a skater
woodward-park-city.text: Coaches broke skills down safely and the indoor facility means no wasted rain days. Our son progressed fast.

woodward-park-city.author: Camper
woodward-park-city.text: Foam pits all day. More like a training facility than a campfire camp, which is exactly what I wanted.

canyonlands-field-institute.author: Parent
canyonlands-field-institute.text: Guides are naturalists first, and the river trip felt safe and genuinely educational. Small groups make it personal.

canyonlands-field-institute.author: Teen camper
canyonlands-field-institute.text: Rafting and sleeping on a sandbar under the stars beat any cabin camp I've done. Hot, though. Very hot.

camp-galilee-nv.author: Reno parent
camp-galilee-nv.text: Beachfront on Tahoe at a nonprofit price — unbeatable. The chaplains create a genuinely kind community.

camp-galilee-nv.author: Camper
camp-galilee-nv.text: Swimming in Tahoe every day was amazing, even if the water turns your legs numb.

nevada-state-4h-camp.author: Rural Nevada parent
nevada-state-4h-camp.text: Kids from tiny towns across the state meet at Tahoe — my daughter's 4-H friends now span 300 miles. Great value.

nevada-state-4h-camp.author: Camper
nevada-state-4h-camp.text: The lake days and camp dances were the best. Bunkhouses are basic but who cares.

camp-cartwheel.author: Parent of a camper in treatment
camp-cartwheel.text: Nurses on-site, zero cost, and my son's sister got to come too. Cartwheel gave our family a week of just being kids.

camp-cartwheel.author: Sibling camper
camp-cartwheel.text: It's the one place where everyone gets what our family is going through. Also the talent show is hilarious.

teton-valley-ranch-camp.author: Parent of a two-summer camper
teton-valley-ranch-camp.text: TVRC turned our teenager into a competent horseman and a hard worker. The backcountry pack trips are the real thing.

teton-valley-ranch-camp.author: Alum
teton-valley-ranch-camp.text: Sixty years of family history at the ranch. The mail-order lifestyle-free month in the Winds is a gift few kids get anymore.

teton-valley-ranch-camp.author: Camper
teton-valley-ranch-camp.text: Loved my horse and the hiking crew. It's a month with no phone, which was harder for my parents than me.

teton-science-schools-camps.author: Parent of a wildlife-obsessed kid
teton-science-schools-camps.text: Our son watched a wolf through a spotting scope with a biologist narrating. School science never stood a chance after that.

teton-science-schools-camps.author: Teen camper
teton-science-schools-camps.text: Backpacking in the Tetons was epic. There's journaling and data collection too — it is a science school, after all.

wilderness-adventures.author: Parent of a trip alum
wilderness-adventures.text: The leaders were exceptional communicators and our daughter came home more capable and kinder. Worth the steep price for us.

wilderness-adventures.author: Teen participant
wilderness-adventures.text: Rafting and summit days were unforgettable. You're with the same 14 people for weeks, so group vibes matter a lot.

soar-wyoming.author: Parent of an ADHD camper
soar-wyoming.text: First camp that didn't call us to pick him up early. SOAR's staff understand ADHD brains and build on strengths.

soar-wyoming.author: Camper
soar-wyoming.text: Llama trekking sounds weird but it's the best. Nobody treats you like the problem kid here.

camp-buffalo-bill.author: Scoutmaster
camp-buffalo-bill.text: The wildest setting our troop has camped in — moose walked through camp twice. Staff run tight bear protocols.

camp-buffalo-bill.author: Scout
camp-buffalo-bill.text: Fly fishing merit badge on the North Fork was great. It's remote, so bring everything you need.

camp-sacajawea-wy.author: Casper parent
camp-sacajawea-wy.text: A Casper Mountain classic — my mom went, I went, now my daughter goes. Simple and wonderful.

camp-sacajawea-wy.author: Camper
camp-sacajawea-wy.text: The forest is right there and the songs are the best part. Gets chilly at night on the mountain.

camp-loll.author: Utah scoutmaster
camp-loll.text: Worth every mile of washboard road. Staff spirit is legendary and the hikes to Union Falls are world-class.

camp-loll.author: Scout
camp-loll.text: Swimming below a waterfall in Yellowstone country — no other camp tops it. No showers all week though.

ymca-camp-colman.author: Parent of two campers
ymca-camp-colman.text: The beachfront is the whole show — my kids came home talking about low-tide walks and jumping off the dock. Counselors were young but clearly well trained. Sign-up day is chaotic though; sessions fill fast.

ymca-camp-colman.author: Former camper turned counselor
ymca-camp-colman.text: Colman has real history and it shows in the traditions. Cabins are rustic and the weather can be soggy, but that's the Northwest. Financial aid is genuinely accessible here.

ymca-camp-seymour.author: Parent of a first-time camper
ymca-camp-seymour.text: They handled my nervous 8-year-old beautifully — she was homesick day one and beaming by pickup. The marine science area is a standout. Communication home could be a bit better mid-week.

ymca-camp-seymour.author: Alum from the 2000s
ymca-camp-seymour.text: Classic Puget Sound camp: salty beach, big dining hall songs, campfires. It's gotten bigger and a little more polished than my era, but the heart is intact.

camp-sealth.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-sealth.text: Sealth is wonderfully unfussy — kids get dirty, sing loud, and belong. Staff handled my son's dietary needs without drama. The all-gender cabin options were handled thoughtfully.

camp-sealth.author: Longtime alum
camp-sealth.text: A hundred-plus years of tradition and it still feels like the same warm, slightly scrappy place. The boat ride to camp is pure magic for a kid.

camp-sealth.author: Parent
camp-sealth.text: Great value and great values. Facilities are aging in spots and the sign-up rush in January is stressful, but the counselors are the best we've encountered.

camp-killoqua.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-killoqua.text: Killoqua is the definition of a sweet local camp — small, affordable, and staffed by people who remember your kid's name from last year. Nothing flashy, everything solid.

camp-killoqua.author: Camper, age 12
camp-killoqua.text: The lake is awesome and counselors do funny skits every night. Food is just okay and the cabins get cold, but I want to go back.

camp-kirby.author: Parent
camp-kirby.text: Beach on both sides of camp and kids roam it at low tide like explorers. Very rustic — think platform shelters, not cabins — which our kid loved and some might not.

camp-kirby.author: Alum
camp-kirby.text: Decades later I still think about the smell of the salt flats. Small camp, big feelings. Facilities were basic then and I hear they still are.

camp-zanika-lache.author: Parent from Wenatchee
camp-zanika-lache.text: The setting on Lake Wenatchee is unbeatable and the price is fair for central Washington families. It's genuinely rustic — pack warm layers, the lake is cold even in July.

camp-zanika-lache.author: Camper, age 13
camp-zanika-lache.text: Canoeing at sunrise was my favorite thing ever. Bathrooms are a walk from the cabins and the mosquitoes mean business.

camp-roganunda.author: Parent from Yakima
camp-roganunda.text: A humble little mountain camp that's been serving valley kids forever. Facilities are old but the staff heart is real, and the price makes camp possible for a lot of families here.

camp-norwester.author: Third-generation camp family parent
camp-norwester.text: Nor'wester is a family religion for us. Teepee living, the big canoe, no screens for a month — kids come back changed. It's expensive and enrollment opens with a scramble.

camp-norwester.author: Alum
camp-norwester.text: The most formative place of my childhood. Traditions are taken seriously without being stuffy. Cold saltwater dips at dawn are non-negotiable, be warned.

camp-norwester.author: Parent
camp-norwester.text: Beautiful, thoughtful program. The island logistics are a real production and the tuition stings, but our daughter counts the days all year.

four-winds-westward-ho.author: Parent of two campers
four-winds-westward-ho.text: It feels like camp from a storybook — middies and shorts, letters home, sailing in the San Juans. Old-fashioned in a deliberate, lovely way. Not cheap, and the waitlist is real.

four-winds-westward-ho.author: Alum from the 90s
four-winds-westward-ho.text: The uniforms erase the status games and the island does the rest. I still row because of Four Winds. Some kids find the pace slow at first; that's the point.

canoe-island-french-camp.author: Parent of a returning camper
canoe-island-french-camp.text: Where else does your kid eat crepes on a private island and kayak with seals? French is woven in gently — no prior language needed. Getting there requires a boat shuttle and planning.

canoe-island-french-camp.author: Camper, age 14
canoe-island-french-camp.text: Fencing, tide pools, and baguette mornings. It's small so everyone knows everyone by day two. The composting toilets take getting used to.

hidden-valley-camp-wa.author: Parent of a first-time camper
hidden-valley-camp-wa.text: Gentle, joyful, and superbly organized. The lake with the big slide is every kid's highlight. Sessions sell out absurdly fast — set an alarm for registration day.

hidden-valley-camp-wa.author: Alum and now camp parent
hidden-valley-camp-wa.text: I went in the 80s and it's still recognizably the same kind place. Less wilderness-y than some NW camps; more classic bunk-and-lake fun.

camp-solomon-schechter.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-solomon-schechter.text: Joyful Judaism without pressure — my kids ask for the Shabbat songs at home. The community is small enough that the directors actually know every camper.

camp-solomon-schechter.author: Alum
camp-solomon-schechter.text: Lifelong friends and a genuine Jewish identity came out of my summers there. Facilities were dated for years, though they've been steadily upgrading.

urj-camp-kalsman.author: Parent of two campers
urj-camp-kalsman.text: Newer facilities than any Jewish camp we toured, and the song sessions are electric. Inclusion staff worked closely with us on our son's anxiety plan.

urj-camp-kalsman.author: Camper, age 15
urj-camp-kalsman.text: The farm goats, the pool, Shabbat under the trees — Kalsman is my favorite place on earth. Wi-fi-free is hard for a day, then great.

camp-parsons.author: Scoutmaster
camp-parsons.text: Parsons runs like a well-oiled machine after a century of practice — strong waterfront, legendary staff spirit. The dining hall is loud and the hills are steep; scouts leave tired and proud.

camp-parsons.author: Alum
camp-parsons.text: Sunrise over Hood Canal from the parade ground is burned into my memory. Traditions here mean something. Weather can drench a whole week, pack accordingly.

camp-pigott.author: Parent of a scout
camp-pigott.text: Solid, well-run scout week — my son knocked out three merit badges and came home filthy and happy. Food gets mixed reviews from the boys.

camp-cowles.author: Scout, age 14
camp-cowles.text: The waterfront is the best part — sailing merit badge was actually fun. Cabins are basic and it gets hot in July, but staff campfires are hilarious.

camp-robbinswold.author: Parent of a Girl Scout
camp-robbinswold.text: My daughter shucked her own oysters at 10 years old and hasn't stopped talking about it. Rustic in the true sense — tents, unit fires, cold canal swims.

camp-robbinswold.author: Alum
camp-robbinswold.text: Robbinswold made me an outdoors person for life. Council budget cuts always loom over these camps, so go while you can.

camp-river-ranch.author: Parent of a horse-obsessed camper
camp-river-ranch.text: The riding program is the draw and it delivers — patient wranglers, real skill-building. Horse sessions cost more and fill within days of registration opening.

camp-river-ranch.author: Camper, age 11
camp-river-ranch.text: I got to ride a horse named after a mountain and sleep in a treehouse unit. Showers are cold-ish sometimes. Best week of my summer.

camp-st-albans.author: Parent
camp-st-albans.text: Counselors were warm and the tide-flat exploring was a hit. Girl Scout camps are a bargain compared to private camps, and it shows a little in the aging facilities.

camp-don-bosco.author: Catholic school parent
camp-don-bosco.text: Faith is present but never heavy-handed — Mass outdoors, kind counselors, muddy happy kids. Registration through the archdiocese site is clunky.

camp-don-bosco.author: Alum
camp-don-bosco.text: Don Bosco summers shaped half the Catholic kids I know in Seattle. Cabins are no-frills and the river is cold, which is half the fun.

camp-hamilton.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-hamilton.text: Private lake, big woods, and a genuinely caring staff culture. Our non-Catholic friends' kids have gone too and felt fully welcome.

camp-gallagher.author: Parent of a teen sailor
camp-gallagher.text: By week's end my 14-year-old was actually skippering a boat on the Sound. It's rustic and physically demanding — kids who want a resort should look elsewhere.

camp-gallagher.author: Alum
camp-gallagher.text: Gallagher taught me sailing, weather, and grit. Overnight voyages to islands were the highlight of my teens. Bring rain gear you trust.

camp-huston.author: Episcopal parish parent
camp-huston.text: Huston is church camp for kids who ask big questions — open-hearted, LGBTQ-affirming, zero fire-and-brimstone. Small enough that shy kids blossom.

warm-beach-camp.author: Parent of two campers
warm-beach-camp.text: The horse program is the real deal and the grounds are immaculate. Faith content is front and center — exactly what we wanted; know that going in.

warm-beach-camp.author: Camper, age 12
warm-beach-camp.text: Zip line and horses were awesome. Chapel every day is a lot, but the counselors make it fun. Food is better than school food by a mile.

camp-firwood.author: Parent of a teen
camp-firwood.text: Firwood counselors invest hard in these kids — my son still texts his from two summers ago. Waterfront toys are top-tier. Evening worship is central to the program.

camp-firwood.author: Camper, age 15
camp-firwood.text: Wakeboarding every day and the wildest capture-the-flag games. Cabins are tight quarters but you barely spend time in them.

camp-lutherwood-washington.author: ELCA parent
camp-lutherwood-washington.text: Exactly the kind of open-armed Lutheran camp we hoped for — every kid belongs, grace at meals, mud on knees. Small camp means individual attention.

island-lake-camp.author: Parent
island-lake-camp.text: Skate camp plus lake time was the perfect combo for our 12-year-old. Gospel message is explicit and enthusiastic — that's the mission. Well-organized drop-off and pickup.

island-lake-camp.author: Camper, age 13
island-lake-camp.text: Go-karts and the blob are elite. Some activities have long lines when camp is full. Counselors were super chill.

miracle-ranch.author: Parent of a horse kid
miracle-ranch.text: Daily riding, real barn chores, and wranglers who teach horsemanship, not just pony rides. Faith programming is strong throughout. Horse track costs extra but is worth it.

sambica.author: Eastside parent
sambica.text: Hard to beat a day camp with an actual lakefront and boat time. Counselors are high-energy college kids. It's popular, so weeks sell out by early spring.

sambica.author: Parent
sambica.text: Our secular family was comfortable, though there's daily chapel and worship music — know your fit. Pickup traffic on West Lake Sammamish Parkway is its own adventure.

black-lake-bible-camp.author: Parent
black-lake-bible-camp.text: Great price for a full week on a lake. The gospel focus is strong every evening. Facilities are older but clean, and the waterfront staff were sharp.

lake-retreat-camp.author: Parent of a returning camper
lake-retreat-camp.text: Our kids love the lake and the staff clearly love the kids. Cabins are dated and the schedule is chapel-heavy, but it's a dependable, warm week every summer.

sunset-lake-camp.author: Adventist parent
sunset-lake-camp.text: Wonderful staff-to-camper ratio and the vegetarian food is honestly great. Non-Adventist friends' kids have attended happily too. Mount Rainier views on clear days.

camp-arnold.author: Parent
camp-arnold.text: They make camp possible for families like ours — scholarships without stigma. My daughter rode a horse for the first time and talked about it for months.

camp-arnold.author: Counselor
camp-arnold.text: Kids arrive from every background imaginable and the staff meets them all with patience. Resources are stretched thin some weeks; the heart never is.

camp-gifford.author: Spokane parent
camp-gifford.text: An absolute lifeline in summer — real camp at a price working families can manage. The lakefront is lovely and the counselors treat every kid with dignity.

camp-spalding.author: Spokane parent
camp-spalding.text: Spalding runs an unusually tight ship — great food, great staff, thoughtful faith conversations. Our kids rank it above pricier camps we've tried.

camp-spalding.author: Camper, age 16
camp-spalding.text: The wake week for teens is unreal. Counselors actually talk with you like a person. Cell phones stay home and honestly it's better that way.

tall-timber-ranch.author: Parent
tall-timber-ranch.text: The drive in tells you everything — river, peaks, no cell service for miles. Wonderful, humble staff. Our kid did a real backpacking trip at 12.

cascades-camp-yelm.author: Parent of a returning camper
cascades-camp-yelm.text: Facilities are nicer than most church camps — good food, clean lodges, well-run barn. The week flew by and my kids signed up again before we left the parking lot.

sound-view-camp.author: Parent
sound-view-camp.text: Kids fed goats, dug clams, and slept in yurts — very sweet, very rustic. Program polish varies year to year with the small staff, but the place itself is special.

ymca-camp-reed.author: Third-generation Camp Reed parent
ymca-camp-reed.text: Reed is a Spokane rite of passage — same songs I sang in the 80s, same lake, same magic. Registration morning is a bloodsport; be online early.

ymca-camp-reed.author: Camper, age 12
ymca-camp-reed.text: Cliff jumping (the safe kind), crazy theme days, counselors with the best nicknames. The outhouses at the tepee village are gross but it's tradition.

ymca-camp-bishop.author: Parent from Aberdeen
ymca-camp-bishop.text: Small-town camp in the best sense — affordable, kind, and low-key. Facilities are simple and some activities depend on which staff they land each year.

ymca-camp-dudley-wa.author: Yakima parent
ymca-camp-dudley-wa.text: The mountain setting is stunning and the price is right. Our kids came home with friendship bracelets and zero interest in their phones for a full day. Bring warm sleeping bags.

camp-ghormley.author: Parent
camp-ghormley.text: Honest mountain church camp — nothing fancy, everything sincere. My kids love the creek and the camp store. Dust gets everywhere by Friday.

lazy-f-camp.author: Methodist parent
lazy-f-camp.text: Progressive, welcoming, and set in a gorgeous canyon. The horse wranglers were patient with total beginners. Watch for wasps in late summer.

camp-korey.author: Parent of a camper with a chronic condition
camp-korey.text: The first place our son has ever been just a kid, not a patient. Medical team handles everything invisibly. Free of charge, which still amazes me.

camp-korey.author: Volunteer counselor
camp-korey.text: The most intentional camp programming I've ever seen — every activity has an adaptive plan. Sessions are short but families say the confidence lasts all year.

camp-stand-by-me.author: Parent of a camper with autism
camp-stand-by-me.text: Staff met my son exactly where he is — visual schedules, sensory breaks, real patience. He did a zip line. I cried at pickup.

camp-ten-trees.author: Parent of a queer teen
camp-ten-trees.text: The first camp where my kid didn't have to explain themselves to anyone. Sliding scale made it possible. Sessions are short and demand far exceeds spots.

camp-ten-trees.author: Camper, age 16
camp-ten-trees.text: A week of being completely myself, with talent shows and campfires and zero side-eye. The borrowed site means facilities vary, but nobody cares.

camp-burton.author: Parent
camp-burton.text: Lovely harbor beach and a kind, low-key staff. The ferry trip makes drop-off an event. Programming is simpler than the big-name camps.

flying-horseshoe-ranch.author: Parent of a horse-crazy kid
flying-horseshoe-ranch.text: Real ranch, real riding, real chores — my daughter progressed more in a week than a year of lessons. It's dusty and rough around the edges, exactly as a ranch should be.

naturebridge-olympic.author: Parent of a science kid
naturebridge-olympic.text: Sleeping steps from Lake Crescent and doing actual field research — our daughter came home planning a biology degree. More educational and less rah-rah than traditional camp.

pacific-science-center-camps.author: Seattle parent
pacific-science-center-camps.text: Curriculum actually taught my kid something — she explained polymers at dinner. Extended care makes it workable for two-career families. Popular themes sell out by March.

pacific-science-center-camps.author: Parent
pacific-science-center-camps.text: Well-organized and genuinely science-forward, though quality varies a bit by instructor. The Seattle Center location means great field trips steps away.

woodland-park-zoo-camp.author: Parent of an animal lover
woodland-park-zoo-camp.text: Behind-the-scenes keeper talks made my 8-year-old's summer. Staff handled our epi-pen kid carefully. Registration opens in winter and the good weeks vanish fast.

seattle-childrens-theatre-camps.author: Parent of a theater kid
seattle-childrens-theatre-camps.text: Professional teaching artists who treat kids like actors, not cargo. The final showcase was genuinely good. Pricier than rec-center drama camp, and worth it.

coyote-central.author: Parent of a maker kid
coyote-central.text: My 12-year-old welded a steel table. Actually welded it. Coyote trusts kids with real tools and they rise to it. Sliding scale means every kid in Seattle can go.

coyote-central.author: Camper, age 13
coyote-central.text: Way better than normal camp — you pick what you make and the teachers do that job in real life. Wish sessions were longer.

sail-sand-point.author: Parent
sail-sand-point.text: By Friday my 10-year-old could rig and sail a dinghy solo. Instructors are patient teen racers themselves. Light-wind days can drag for antsy kids.

wilderness-awareness-school.author: Parent of a nature kid
wilderness-awareness-school.text: This is the anti-screen camp — my son learned to move silently through the forest and identify hawks by their alarm calls. Kids come home muddy and calm.

wilderness-awareness-school.author: Parent
wilderness-awareness-school.text: Wonderful mentors and philosophy, though drop-off in Duvall is a haul from Seattle. Less structured than sports camps; dreamy kids thrive, checklist kids adjust.

mountaineers-summer-camps.author: Climbing parent
mountaineers-summer-camps.text: Real belay instruction, real trail days, staff who summit actual mountains. My cautious kid was rappelling by Thursday. Spots for popular weeks go quickly.

salish-sea-expeditions.author: Parent of a marine-science teen
salish-sea-expeditions.text: A week living aboard, standing watches, and doing actual science — transformative for our 15-year-old. Tight quarters and no showers; pack low expectations for hygiene.

camp-dart-lo.author: Spokane parent
camp-dart-lo.text: Affordable, outdoorsy, and sweetly old-fashioned — my kids splash in the river and come home singing. Bus pickup around town is a lifesaver.

rain-city-rock-camp.author: Parent of a first-time bassist
rain-city-rock-camp.text: Zero music experience required — by Saturday my daughter played bass on a real stage. The empowerment focus is genuine, not a slogan. Earplugs recommended for the showcase.

girls-rock-math.author: Parent of a math-shy kid
girls-rock-math.text: My daughter went from 'I hate math' to explaining tessellations at dinner. Clever curriculum and warm teachers. It's school-building-based, so not an outdoorsy vibe.

camp-westwind.author: Portland parent
camp-westwind.text: Crossing the estuary by boat to a roadless beach camp — it's pure magic and my kids guard their Westwind weeks fiercely. Registration is a frenzy every winter.

camp-westwind.author: Alum
camp-westwind.text: Fog, sand, spruce forest, and traditions that haven't changed in decades. Cold ocean wind is part of the deal; pack layers and embrace it.

camp-namanu.author: Second-generation Namanu parent
camp-namanu.text: The same songs, the same Uncle Toby stories, a hundred years running. Staff inclusivity training shows — every kind of kid belongs here. Cabins are old; nobody minds.

camp-namanu.author: Camper, age 11
camp-namanu.text: Blue Wing beach on the Sandy River is the best place on earth. The dining hall gets so loud during song wars my ears rang. Ten out of ten.

camp-howard.author: Catholic school parent
camp-howard.text: Wholesome in the unironic sense — sports, s'mores, and gentle faith. Counselors are largely alumni who love the place. Cabin bathrooms could use a remodel.

ymca-camp-collins.author: Parent of two campers
ymca-camp-collins.text: Close to Portland but feels deep-woods. Great counselors, easy logistics, real financial aid. The river beach is the highlight of every session.

ymca-camp-collins.author: Camper, age 10
ymca-camp-collins.text: Horses and the giant swing were the best. Some cabins are kind of creaky and the food is just fine. I made three best friends.

bnai-brith-camp.author: Portland Jewish parent
bnai-brith-camp.text: A hundred years of ruach on Devils Lake — song sessions, water skiing, Havdalah on the dock. Inclusion staff supported our ADHD kiddo beautifully.

bnai-brith-camp.author: Alum
bnai-brith-camp.text: BB is where Northwest Jewish kids find their people. Coastal weather means some gray mornings, but nobody remembers those — they remember the friendships.

camp-meriwether-or.author: Scoutmaster
camp-meriwether-or.text: An ocean-beach scout camp is a rare thing and Meriwether makes the most of it — surf wading, sand games, great staff spirit. Fog and drizzle are always on the packing list.

camp-baldwin.author: Parent of a scout
camp-baldwin.text: Sunny, dusty, and full of things that go fast — bikes, arrows, rifles. My scout loved it. It's remote, so forgotten gear stays forgotten.

camp-pioneer-or.author: Scout, age 13
camp-pioneer-or.text: The lake is freezing and awesome. Fishing merit badge guy was a legend. Long drive up but worth it when you see the mountains.

camp-baker-or.author: Scoutmaster
camp-baker-or.text: Strong waterfront program and the dunes trip is a highlight every year. Dining hall food is scout-camp standard, which is to say survivable.

camp-cleawox.author: Parent of a Girl Scout
camp-cleawox.text: Canoeing across the lake to climb giant dunes — it's like camp on another planet. Rustic tents and coastal chill; pack the warm pajamas the list demands.

camp-whispering-winds.author: Parent
camp-whispering-winds.text: My daughter's riding progressed every summer and the counselors remember her year to year. Girl Scout pricing makes multi-week summers actually feasible.

camp-kilowan.author: Alum and camp parent
camp-kilowan.text: Kilowan is tiny and timeless — same creek dams, same songs, same feeling of belonging I had in the 90s. Facilities are old, hearts are not.

twin-rocks-friends-camp.author: Parent
twin-rocks-friends-camp.text: Lake on one side, ocean on the other — location does half the work and the sweet staff does the rest. Coastal July can be 58 and gray; kids don't care.

camp-magruder.author: Methodist parent
camp-magruder.text: Falling asleep to surf and waking to lake mist — the setting is a sermon in itself. Inclusive theology, tired-but-happy kids. Sand in everything forever.

suttle-lake-camp.author: Parent from Bend
suttle-lake-camp.text: Gorgeous lake, kind theology, s'mores every night. Simple cabins and cold mornings — it's the mountains, that's the charm.

big-lake-youth-camp.author: Parent
big-lake-youth-camp.text: Staff energy is off the charts and the lake program rivals private ski camps. Vegetarian food surprised our skeptical son by being great. Sabbath rhythm is part of the week.

big-lake-youth-camp.author: Camper, age 14
big-lake-youth-camp.text: Wakeboarding under a volcano. That's it, that's the review. Also the banana bread slaps.

camp-tamarack-or.author: Parent
camp-tamarack-or.text: Feels like camp from a Wes Anderson film — small lake, wooden canoes, kids free-ranging happily. Modest size means it books up quietly and early.

trackers-earth-portland.author: Portland parent
trackers-earth-portland.text: The themed tracks are genius — my kid did Ranger camp and learned real knife skills and fire-building inside a story. Buses from close-in neighborhoods make logistics painless.

trackers-earth-portland.author: Parent
trackers-earth-portland.text: Wonderful outdoor skills and imagination, though the operation has gotten big and some weeks feel more polished than others. Kids universally come home filthy and thrilled.

omsi-hancock-field-station.author: Parent of a paleontology kid
omsi-hancock-field-station.text: Our dinosaur-obsessed 11-year-old held a 40-million-year-old fossil she excavated herself. Rustic desert quarters, huge intellectual payoff. It's hot and remote — that's the point.

omsi-hancock-field-station.author: Alum
omsi-hancock-field-station.text: Hancock summers are why I became a geologist. The night sky out there rearranges your brain. Bring double the water bottles you think you need.

camp-lutherhaven-id.author: Spokane-area parent
camp-lutherhaven-id.text: Lutherhaven counselors are famous around here for a reason — silly, kind, and completely present. The lakefront at sunset is postcard material.

camp-lutherhaven-id.author: Camper, age 12
camp-lutherhaven-id.text: The zip line goes toward the lake and it's the best feeling ever. Songs at closing campfire made half my cabin cry happy tears.

camp-sweyolakan.author: Third-generation camper family
camp-sweyolakan.text: Boat-in only, no roads — Sweyolakan feels out of time in the best way. My mother sang me the same songs my daughter now sings. Rustic shelters, big memories.

camp-sweyolakan.author: Camper, age 13
camp-sweyolakan.text: Sleeping with open walls means chipmunks visit, which is either great or terrifying. Swimming in the lake all day is unbeatable.

camp-four-echoes.author: Parent of a Girl Scout
camp-four-echoes.text: My daughter earned her paddle-craft progression and a truckload of confidence. Classic Girl Scout rusticity — she loved it, her phone-attached friend did not.

camp-easton.author: Scoutmaster
camp-easton.text: One of the prettiest scout waterfronts in the country — sailing, kayaking, the works. The hillside hike between camp areas keeps everyone honest.

twinlow-camp.author: Parent
twinlow-camp.text: Small enough that the director greeted my son by name on day two. Sweet lakeside setting, open-hearted theology, very fair price.

camp-mivoden.author: Parent
camp-mivoden.text: Waterski instruction that actually gets kids up on skis, and staff who radiate patience. The vegetarian cafeteria won over my carnivore twins.

sitka-fine-arts-camp.author: Parent of a young cellist
sitka-fine-arts-camp.text: Conservatory-level instruction in the most improbable, beautiful place. The final performances brought the whole town out. Getting to Sitka is an expedition — plan flights early.

sitka-fine-arts-camp.author: Alum
sitka-fine-arts-camp.text: SFAC rebuilt that campus with volunteer labor and you can feel the love in every studio. Rain every day, art every hour, friends for life.

camp-togowoods.author: Anchorage parent
camp-togowoods.text: Real Alaska camping — my daughter came home able to build a fire in drizzle and prouder than I've ever seen her. Mosquito repellent is not optional.

trailside-discovery-camp.author: Anchorage parent
trailside-discovery-camp.text: Field trips most Lower 48 camps could only dream of — glacier hikes, salmon streams, mudflats. Gear lists are serious because the weather is. Worth every rain jacket.

solid-rock-bible-camp.author: Kenai parent
solid-rock-bible-camp.text: Kids fish, ride, and hear the gospel from counselors who clearly love Alaska. Rustic and mosquito-rich in June, glorious in July.

victory-bible-camp.author: Mat-Su Valley parent
victory-bible-camp.text: Three generations of our family have gone to Victory. The mountain lake polar plunge is a badge of honor. Cabins are basic; the views are not.

echo-ranch-bible-camp.author: Juneau parent
echo-ranch-bible-camp.text: Horseback riding on a wild Alaskan beach — my kids talk about it all winter. Rain gear is life. Staff are humble, hardworking, and kind.

camp-li-wa.author: Fairbanks parent
camp-li-wa.text: Horses, midnight sun, and counselors who know every kid's name by Tuesday. It's the camp everyone in Fairbanks grew up with. Dust and mosquitoes included free.

camp-mokuleia.author: Oahu parent
camp-mokuleia.text: Falling asleep to waves on the North Shore — mainland camps can't compete. Staff blend Hawaiian culture and Episcopal warmth naturally. Book early; locals know.

ymca-camp-erdman.author: Honolulu parent
ymca-camp-erdman.text: A century-old Hawaii institution — kids surf-play in the morning and do ropes course under the Waianae cliffs by afternoon. Sessions fill with kama'aina families fast.

ymca-camp-erdman.author: Camper, age 11
ymca-camp-erdman.text: The beach bonfire night was the best night of my whole life. Cabins get sandy and nobody cares.

camp-paumalu.author: Parent of a Girl Scout
camp-paumalu.text: Lei-making, mo'olelo storytelling, and muddy trail hikes above the sea — a beautifully local take on Girl Scout camp. Facilities are simple and the ocean breeze is constant.

camp-pupukea.author: Scout parent
camp-pupukea.text: Merit badges in the morning, tide pools after dinner — you can't beat the geography. Camp itself is rustic and red-dirt dusty. Scouts love it.

camp-maluhia.author: Maui scout parent
camp-maluhia.text: Windy ridgeline camp with views to the ocean on both sides. Facilities are weathered but the volunteer spirit keeps it running strong.

ymca-camp-keanae.author: Maui parent
ymca-camp-keanae.text: The setting on the Ke'anae Peninsula is jaw-dropping — waves crashing on lava rock beside the field. Program is simple and sweet; the place itself is the teacher.

hawaii-nature-center-camp.author: Honolulu parent
hawaii-nature-center-camp.text: My kids came home naming native plants and begging to hike on weekends. Small groups, real naturalists. Prepare for spectacular amounts of red mud in the laundry.

kanakuk-k-1-mo.author: Parent of two Kampers
kanakuk-k-1-mo.text: Counselors pour into the kids and the energy is unreal. My son counts down the days all year. Faith is woven into everything, which is exactly what we wanted.

kanakuk-k-1-mo.author: Former camper, now a parent
kanakuk-k-1-mo.text: The program itself is fantastic, but the organization's past abuse scandal still gives some families pause. Ask hard questions about current safeguards before enrolling.

kanakuk-k-2-mo.author: Mom of a three-year returner
kanakuk-k-2-mo.text: K-2 turned my shy teen into a leader. The staff-to-camper ratio and intentional discipleship are worth the steep price.

kanakuk-k-2-mo.author: Teen camper
kanakuk-k-2-mo.text: Best two weeks of my summer, but the days are long and there's not much downtime. You will be exhausted in the best way.

kanakuk-k-kountry-mo.author: First-time camp dad
kanakuk-k-kountry-mo.text: Perfect on-ramp to overnight camp. Counselors sent photos and my six-year-old came home singing camp songs for weeks.

kanakuk-k-west-mo.author: Parent of a teen girl
kanakuk-k-west-mo.text: The counselors model real faith and confidence. My daughter's friend group from K-West texts all year long.

kanakuk-k-west-mo.author: Alum counselor
kanakuk-k-west-mo.text: Wonderful sisterhood culture. It is intense and scheduled, so girls who need quiet time have to carve it out.

kids-across-america-mo.author: Youth leader who brings a group
kids-across-america-mo.text: KAA treats our city kids like royalty. Scholarships make it possible, and the impact lasts all school year.

camp-barnabas-mo.author: Parent of a camper with special needs
camp-barnabas-mo.text: The only place my son is just a camper, not a diagnosis. One-on-one buddies, nurses on site, and pure joy.

camp-barnabas-mo.author: Volunteer buddy
camp-barnabas-mo.text: Exhausting, holy work. Barnabas changes the volunteers as much as the campers.

camp-war-eagle-ar.author: Parent paying full fare
camp-war-eagle-ar.text: The income-based tuition model means real diversity in the cabins. Program quality rivals camps triple the price.

camp-siloam-ar.author: Church youth leader
camp-siloam-ar.text: Affordable, well-run, and the staff serve our group like family. Cabins are basic but the ministry is the point.

camp-siloam-ar.author: Parent
camp-siloam-ar.text: My kids love it, though summer heat in the older cabins is no joke.

ferncliff-camp-ar.author: Parent of a sensitive kid
ferncliff-camp-ar.text: Ferncliff meets kids where they are. Less rah-rah, more kindness, gardens, and canoes. Our daughter thrived.

new-life-ranch-ok.author: Tulsa parent
new-life-ranch-ok.text: Three generations of our family have gone. Great value, great counselors, and the creek is the star of the show.

new-life-ranch-ok.author: Camper
new-life-ranch-ok.text: The blob and horses are awesome. Food is just okay, but nobody goes for the food.

falls-creek-ok.author: Oklahoma alum
falls-creek-ok.text: A rite of passage for Oklahoma teens. The tabernacle worship with thousands of students is unforgettable.

falls-creek-ok.author: Youth pastor
falls-creek-ok.text: Massive and hot in July, and cabin quality depends on your church's building. But the week consistently changes kids' lives.

carol-joy-holling-camp-ne.author: Omaha parent
carol-joy-holling-camp-ne.text: Counselors are genuinely kind, and the horse program is a highlight. Faith is present without being heavy-handed.

pine-cove-tyler-tx.author: Houston mom
pine-cove-tyler-tx.text: Staff culture is the secret sauce — silly, safe, and sincere. Registration sells out in minutes, so set an alarm.

pine-cove-tyler-tx.author: Camper
pine-cove-tyler-tx.text: Counselors have goofy nicknames and make everyone feel included. The week flies by too fast.

pine-cove-crier-creek-tx.author: Family camp regular
pine-cove-crier-creek-tx.text: The rare vacation where parents actually rest while kids are loved on all day. We rebook for next year before we leave.

sky-ranch-van-tx.author: Dallas parent
sky-ranch-van-tx.text: Facilities are resort-level and the horsemanship program is legit. Strong communication all week.

sky-ranch-van-tx.author: Parent of a first-timer
sky-ranch-van-tx.text: Wonderful week, though it's pricier than church camps and add-ons stack up.

t-bar-m-camps-tx.author: San Antonio dad
t-bar-m-camps-tx.text: Coaches actually teach skills, and the river time is classic Texas summer. Staff remember returning kids by name.

camp-eagle-tx.author: Adventure-loving camper
camp-eagle-tx.text: Clearest river water in Texas and real mountain bike trails. It feels wild and far from everything — because it is.

camp-eagle-tx.author: Parent
camp-eagle-tx.text: Incredible program; just know it's a long drive from any city and cell service is nil.

laity-lodge-youth-camp-tx.author: Multi-generation LLYC family
laity-lodge-youth-camp-tx.text: The Frio canyon is magic and the camp culture is unusually kind — no cliques, no yelling coaches. Getting a spot is the hard part.

camp-cho-yeh-tx.author: Houston parent
camp-cho-yeh-tx.text: Well-organized from drop-off to photos. My kids came home more confident and asking to go longer next year.

camp-cho-yeh-tx.author: Camper
camp-cho-yeh-tx.text: Horses and the lake are great. Cabins get hot in July even with AC working overtime.

carolina-creek-tx.author: Parent
carolina-creek-tx.text: Staff enthusiasm is contagious and the waterfront is a kid's dream. Registration fills quickly for peak weeks.

camp-allen-tx.author: Episcopal parent
camp-allen-tx.text: Faith with room to ask questions. Beautiful grounds, kind counselors, and compline under the pines is special.

camp-capers-tx.author: Alum sending her own kids
camp-capers-tx.text: Small enough that everyone is known. River swims and evening chapel feel timeless.

mo-ranch-tx.author: Presbyterian parent
mo-ranch-tx.text: The river slide alone is worth it. Programming is warm and never pushy, and the canyon views are stunning.

camp-lone-star-tx.author: Lutheran parent
camp-lone-star-tx.text: Affordable, faithful, and friendly. Counselors handle homesickness with a lot of grace.

camp-blue-haven-nm.author: West Texas parent
camp-blue-haven-nm.text: Old-fashioned in the best sense — devotionals, mountain hikes, and singing that gives you chills.

glorieta-camps-nm.author: Colorado parent
glorieta-camps-nm.text: Adventure programming is genuinely challenging and the mountain setting beats the summer heat. Staff share their faith naturally.

glorieta-camps-nm.author: Teen camper
glorieta-camps-nm.text: Bike trails are amazing. Altitude wiped me out the first two days, so hydrate.

camp-kivu-co.author: Parent of a skeptical teen
camp-kivu-co.text: Kivu lets teens wrestle with faith instead of forcing it. The Durango setting and trips are spectacular.

annunciation-heights-co.author: Catholic parent
annunciation-heights-co.text: Rare find: faithful Catholic formation plus genuinely fun outdoor programming. Counselors are joyful young adults.

camp-wojtyla-co.author: Parent of a teen boy
camp-wojtyla-co.text: Tents, summit hikes, daily Mass on a mountainside. Demanding and deeply formative — not for kids wanting cushy cabins.

young-life-frontier-ranch-co.author: High school camper
young-life-frontier-ranch-co.text: They call it the best week of your life and honestly it was. The mountain hike and club talks hit different.

young-life-frontier-ranch-co.author: YL volunteer leader
young-life-frontier-ranch-co.text: Everything is engineered so leaders can focus on kids. Food and facilities are shockingly good.

young-life-crooked-creek-ranch-co.author: Teen camper
young-life-crooked-creek-ranch-co.text: Felt like a mountain resort built for teenagers. Leaders actually listen to you.

christikon-mt.author: Montana parent
christikon-mt.text: Wilderness trips led with care, and theology that takes kids seriously. No cell service for miles — wonderful.

flathead-lutheran-bible-camp-mt.author: Parent
flathead-lutheran-bible-camp-mt.text: The lakefront is world-class and the counselors are gems. Great sliding-scale pricing.

hume-lake-ca.author: California youth pastor
hume-lake-ca.text: The gold standard for church camps out West. Production quality is unreal and the setting among the sequoias can't be beat.

hume-lake-ca.author: Teen camper
hume-lake-ca.text: Best week of summer, though it's crowded and you're mostly with your own church group.

forest-home-ca.author: SoCal parent
forest-home-ca.text: Deep history and a beautiful canyon. Our church has camped here for decades.

redwood-camp-mount-hermon-ca.author: Bay Area parent
redwood-camp-mount-hermon-ca.text: Camp under actual redwoods, with a century of tradition behind it. Staff are superb with first-timers.

hartland-christian-camp-ca.author: Fresno-area parent
hartland-christian-camp-ca.text: Solid, affordable mountain camp our church trusts. Kids rave about paintball and the snack bar.

jh-ranch-ca.author: Dad who did the father-son week
jh-ranch-ca.text: The parent-teen format rebuilt our relationship. Long travel to get there, but worth every mile.

camp-stevens-ca.author: San Diego parent
camp-stevens-ca.text: Progressive, welcoming, and grounded in nature. Farm-to-table meals at a kids' camp — remarkable.

westminster-woods-ca.author: Sonoma County parent
westminster-woods-ca.text: Redwoods, ropes course, and counselors who let kids be themselves. Faith is an invitation, not a lecture.

young-life-woodleaf-ca.author: First-time YL camper
young-life-woodleaf-ca.text: I'm not religious and still had the best week ever. Nobody pressured me; the talks just made me think.

young-life-lost-canyon-az.author: Phoenix teen
young-life-lost-canyon-az.text: Looks like a resort out of a movie. Leaders were hilarious and the last-night talk got real quiet, in a good way.

young-life-washington-family-ranch-or.author: Portland-area leader
young-life-washington-family-ranch-or.text: An oasis in the middle of nowhere — kids can't believe it when the bus comes over the hill. Superbly run.

young-life-washington-family-ranch-or.author: Camper
young-life-washington-family-ranch-or.text: The water slides and zip line are insane. Long, hot bus ride to get there though.

cyo-camp-howard-or.author: Portland Catholic parent
cyo-camp-howard-or.text: Affordable, sweet, and genuinely Catholic without being stuffy. Counselors grew up there themselves.

cyo-camp-howard-or.author: Camper
cyo-camp-howard-or.text: The forest games are great. Cabins are old but that's part of the charm.

springhill-michigan-mi.author: Michigan parent
springhill-michigan-mi.text: Kids sleep in tree houses and talk about Jesus around campfires. Organization is impressive for the size.

springhill-michigan-mi.author: Parent of a camper with ADHD
springhill-michigan-mi.text: Staff handled my son's needs well. It's big and stimulating, which is great for some kids, a lot for others.

springhill-indiana-in.author: Indianapolis parent
springhill-indiana-in.text: Our kids count down to SpringHill week all year. Counselors write real letters home.

lake-ann-camp-mi.author: Grand Rapids parent
lake-ann-camp-mi.text: Northern Michigan setting is stunning and the staff genuinely disciple kids. Book early for July.

camp-arcadia-mi.author: Fourth-generation Arcadian
camp-arcadia-mi.text: Same families, same beach, a hundred summers. Getting a reservation is the only hard part.

young-life-timber-wolf-lake-mi.author: Chicago-area teen
young-life-timber-wolf-lake-mi.text: Nicest camp I've ever seen. My leader paid attention to me all week, not just at club.

honeyrock-wi.author: Wheaton alum parent
honeyrock-wi.text: The counselor training is a cut above — these are college students being formed as leaders, and it shows.

lutherdale-wi.author: Lutheran parent
lutherdale-wi.text: Kind counselors and a gorgeous lake. Grace-filled in every sense.

camp-ondessonk-il.author: St. Louis parent
camp-ondessonk-il.text: Open-air cabins, waterfall hikes, real adventure. It's rustic — kids shower less than you'd like — and they love it.

camp-ondessonk-il.author: Alum
camp-ondessonk-il.text: Ondessonk made me who I am. The night hikes and horse trails are unforgettable.

camp-wartburg-il.author: Metro-east parent
camp-wartburg-il.text: Close to St. Louis, easy on the budget, and the staff treat every kid with patience.

camp-rancho-framasa-in.author: Indianapolis parent
camp-rancho-framasa-in.text: They welcomed my son's IEP needs without blinking. Beautiful Brown County woods.

catholic-youth-camp-mn.author: Twin Cities Catholic parent
catholic-youth-camp-mn.text: Sweet mix of classic lake camp and gentle faith. Staff alumni come back year after year.

young-life-castaway-club-mn.author: Fargo teen
young-life-castaway-club-mn.text: Parasailing at church camp?? Leaders made everyone feel like the favorite.

camp-ewalu-ia.author: Iowa parent
camp-ewalu-ia.text: Unplugged, muddy, happy kids. The creek hikes are legendary.

hidden-acres-ia.author: Des Moines parent
hidden-acres-ia.text: Go-karts and horses sealed the deal for my kids. Staff communicate well all week.

camp-metigoshe-nd.author: North Dakota parent
camp-metigoshe-nd.text: Half our town's kids grew up here. Music and campfires you remember for decades.

outlaw-ranch-sd.author: South Dakota parent
outlaw-ranch-sd.text: Horses in the Black Hills plus campfire worship — a South Dakota classic.

camp-patmos-oh.author: Cleveland parent
camp-patmos-oh.text: The ferry ride alone makes it feel like an adventure. Simple, sincere, and fun.

skyview-ranch-oh.author: Akron parent
skyview-ranch-oh.text: Great horse program for the price and counselors with servant hearts.

camp-ridgecrest-for-boys-nc.author: Charlotte dad
camp-ridgecrest-for-boys-nc.text: The tribe traditions give boys something to grow into. Counselors are strong role models.

camp-crestridge-for-girls-nc.author: Atlanta mom
camp-crestridge-for-girls-nc.text: My daughter's counselors wrote her notes she still keeps. Wholesome in the best way.

camp-rockmont-nc.author: Nashville parent
camp-rockmont-nc.text: Rockmont takes boyhood seriously — real skills, real challenge, real faith. Lake Eden is gorgeous.

camp-rockmont-nc.author: Camper
camp-rockmont-nc.text: Blacksmithing was the coolest thing I've ever done. Wish sessions were longer.

camp-merri-mac-nc.author: Mom of a returning camper
camp-merri-mac-nc.text: Joyful, Christ-centered, and surprisingly adventurous — my daughter got real whitewater time.

camp-timberlake-nc.author: Raleigh dad
camp-timberlake-nc.text: Small enough that every boy matters. The kayaking progression is excellent.

camp-kahdalea-nc.author: Parent of an outdoorsy daughter
camp-kahdalea-nc.text: Real wilderness trips for girls, run by a family that knows every camper. A hidden gem.

the-wilds-nc.author: Greenville parent
the-wilds-nc.text: Impeccably run and the counselors are earnest. Know going in that it's conservative and preaching-heavy.

the-wilds-nc.author: Teen camper
the-wilds-nc.text: The zip line and giant swing are elite. Schedule is packed to the minute.

camp-kanuga-nc.author: Episcopal mom
camp-kanuga-nc.text: Open-hearted faith and hundred-year-old traditions. My anxious kid was gently folded right in.

camp-don-lee-nc.author: Raleigh parent
camp-don-lee-nc.text: My daughter can actually sail now. Salt air, kind staff, gentle faith.

lutheridge-nc.author: Lutheran choir parent
lutheridge-nc.text: Music camp week is pure magic — the closing hymn sing packs the outdoor chapel.

young-life-windy-gap-nc.author: High schooler from Knoxville
young-life-windy-gap-nc.text: I came for my friends and the giant swing; the last night around the cross wrecked me in the best way.

young-life-carolina-point-nc.author: YL leader
young-life-carolina-point-nc.text: Facilities rival a resort and the staff serve leaders incredibly well. Kids talk about it all year.

look-up-lodge-sc.author: Greenville youth leader
look-up-lodge-sc.text: They serve church groups better than anywhere we've been. Kids beg to return.

asbury-hills-sc.author: Columbia parent
asbury-hills-sc.text: Creek stomping and vespers under the trees — simple and lovely. Staff are gems.

camp-st-christopher-sc.author: Charleston parent
camp-st-christopher-sc.text: Beach camp with a soul. Marsh ecology classes plus chapel by the ocean.

nacome-camp-tn-tn.author: Presbyterian parent
nacome-camp-tn-tn.text: Feels like summer at your grandparents' farm, with worship. Kids adore the creek.

lake-forest-ranch-ms.author: Tupelo parent
lake-forest-ranch-ms.text: Staff invest in kids beyond the summer — ours gets birthday cards from counselors.

camp-abbey-la.author: New Orleans Catholic parent
camp-abbey-la.text: Where NOLA Catholic kids have always gone. Humble cabins, river swims, lifelong friends.

camp-hanover-va.author: Richmond parent
camp-hanover-va.text: Small groups mean no kid gets lost in the shuffle. Wonderfully welcoming to nervous first-timers.

camp-sandy-cove-wv.author: DC-area parent
camp-sandy-cove-wv.text: Close enough to DC, but a world away. Horse program and staff warmth stand out.

camp-of-the-woods-ny.author: Multi-decade returning family
camp-of-the-woods-ny.text: Same week, same families, every August for 20 years. The lakefront and speakers keep us coming back.

word-of-life-island-ny.author: New Jersey parent
word-of-life-island-ny.text: An island camp — the boat ride in sets the tone. Solid Bible teaching and my teen loved the tournaments.

word-of-life-island-ny.author: Former camper
word-of-life-island-ny.text: Great waterfront and friends. Schedule is intense and preaching is long if you're not used to it.

deerfoot-lodge-ny.author: Father of two Deerfoot boys
deerfoot-lodge-ny.text: My sons chop wood, read maps, and lead devotions now. Deerfoot makes men the slow, real way.

young-life-saranac-village-ny.author: Long Island teen
young-life-saranac-village-ny.text: The lake at sunset, the ridiculous skits, leaders who actually care — best week of my life, no contest.

young-life-lake-champion-ny.author: NYC youth leader
young-life-lake-champion-ny.text: They handle busloads from the city with excellence. Kids who've never left their borough feel like VIPs.

camp-johnsonburg-nj.author: NJ parent
camp-johnsonburg-nj.text: Warm, goofy, and safe for every kind of kid. The staff loyalty tells you everything.

spruce-lake-pa.author: Philadelphia parent
spruce-lake-pa.text: Gentle faith, great trails, and staff with genuine patience. A calm alternative to hype-heavy camps.

camp-hebron-pa.author: Central PA parent
camp-hebron-pa.text: Humble and kind from the director on down. The horsemanship weeks are excellent value.

camp-nawakwa-pa.author: Lutheran parent
camp-nawakwa-pa.text: Simple, sweet, and steeped in tradition. My kids love the apple orchard hikes.

camp-pecometh-md.author: Annapolis parent
camp-pecometh-md.text: River kayaking and campfires with genuinely kind staff. Classic Eastern Shore summer.

camp-st-charles-md.author: DC Catholic parent
camp-st-charles-md.text: Generations of DC families send kids here. Riverfront sunsets and rosaries — timeless.

summit-bechtel-reserve.author: Camper from a Virginia troop
summit-bechtel-reserve.text: Zip lines, mountain biking, rafting the New River — it's the most modern Scout facility I've seen. Feels more like an adventure park than a traditional camp, which is either a plus or minus.

summit-bechtel-reserve.author: Parent of two Scouts
summit-bechtel-reserve.text: Facilities are incredible since it hosted the Jamboree. It lacks the rustic soul of the older bases and the summer heat in the gorge is intense, but the kids had a blast.

florida-sea-base.author: Crew leader alum
florida-sea-base.text: A week living on a sailboat with your crew is unforgettable. Snorkeling the reef at Looe Key was the highlight of my Scouting career. Book two years out — slots vanish fast.

florida-sea-base.author: Parent of a scuba crew member
florida-sea-base.text: The scuba program was professionally run and my daughter earned her open-water cert. Pricey once you add airfare, and seasickness got a few kids on the sailing adventure.

northern-tier-high-adventure.author: Alum of two treks
northern-tier-high-adventure.text: Portaging a canoe through the Boundary Waters with everything you need on your back is the real deal. Our interpreter was outstanding. Mosquitoes in July are no joke — bring a head net.

northern-tier-high-adventure.author: Parent of a first-year trekker
northern-tier-high-adventure.text: Cheaper than Philmont and just as transformative. My son came back with sore shoulders and a hundred stories. The gear they provide is well maintained.

camp-emerald-bay.author: Scoutmaster of a returning troop
camp-emerald-bay.text: We cross the channel every summer. Snorkeling in the kelp with leopard sharks and the marine bio staff is something no mainland camp can touch. Book early; summer weeks sell out.

camp-emerald-bay.author: Camper, third summer
camp-emerald-bay.text: You wake up on the beach on an island. Scuba merit badge here was the best week of my life. Food is decent, showers are cold sometimes, nobody cares.

camp-emerald-bay.author: Parent of a first-timer
camp-emerald-bay.text: The boat ride out sets the tone — kids are off the grid all week. Communication home is limited, which was hard for us but great for him.

camp-cherry-valley.author: Camper from Orange County
camp-cherry-valley.text: Snorkeling right off the pier and the astronomy program at night were awesome. The hike to the island ridge is brutal in the sun but the view is worth it.

camp-cherry-valley.author: Troop committee parent
camp-cherry-valley.text: Classic island Scouting with friendly staff. Cabins are open-air and rustic; pack layers because the marine layer keeps mornings chilly even in July.

camp-whitsett.author: Assistant Scoutmaster
camp-whitsett.text: Giant sequoias in camp, a solid waterfront, and staff who clearly love the place. Merit badge program runs on rails. The drive up the mountain road is winding — plan for it.

camp-whitsett.author: Camper, second year
camp-whitsett.text: Campfires and the lake are great. It gets dusty by August and the nights are cold, but that's mountain camp for you.

camp-chawanakee.author: Scoutmaster of 20 years
camp-chawanakee.text: Big camp, big waterfront, big traditions — the Order of the Arrow callout on the lake is unforgettable. Because it's popular it can feel crowded at the dining hall in peak weeks.

camp-chawanakee.author: Camper from Fresno
camp-chawanakee.text: Sailing merit badge on Shaver Lake was the best. Some campsites are a long dusty walk from everything.

camp-wolfeboro.author: Alum and former staffer
camp-wolfeboro.text: Wolfeboro is a time capsule in the best way — river swims, patrol cooking, real backcountry feel. Not the camp for kids who need hot showers and Wi-Fi.

camp-wolfeboro.author: Parent of a new Scout
camp-wolfeboro.text: Beautiful spot on the river and staff were welcoming to first-years. It's genuinely rustic; pack like you're camping, because you are.

camp-royaneh.author: Third-generation alum
camp-royaneh.text: My grandfather camped under the same redwoods. The hillside campsites and the pool keep the old summer-camp feel. Fog rolls in at night, so bring a warm bag.

camp-royaneh.author: Camper from San Francisco
camp-royaneh.text: Cool trees and good campfires. The hike up to some campsites is steep and the food was just okay.

camp-oljato.author: Scoutmaster from the Peninsula
camp-oljato.text: Arriving by boat with no road access makes the whole week feel like an expedition. Sailing program is top-notch. Cell service is nonexistent, which is the point.

camp-oljato.author: Camper, age 13
camp-oljato.text: You take a boat to camp! Sailing and the polar bear swim were my favorites. It's cold in the mornings even in summer.

camp-marin-sierra.author: Parent volunteer
camp-marin-sierra.text: Unpretentious mountain camp with a friendly staff and a pretty little lake. Facilities are showing their age in spots, but the setting makes up for it.

camp-marin-sierra.author: Camper from Marin
camp-marin-sierra.text: Loved the mountain man program and swimming in the lake. Nights get really cold.

camp-winton.author: Scoutmaster of a small troop
camp-winton.text: Small camp, huge heart. Gear goes across the reservoir by barge and the staff-to-Scout ratio is the best we've seen. Limited program compared to mega-camps, but we prefer it.

camp-lassen.author: Parent of a Webelos scout
camp-lassen.text: Great starter camp — low-key, affordable, and the staff were patient with the younger kids. Amenities are basic and the pool is small.

camp-fleischmann.author: Scout from Reno
camp-fleischmann.text: Climbing program and the lake day were the highlights. Smoke from wildfires cut one afternoon short the week we went — that's just the Sierra now.

camp-fleischmann.author: Troop parent
camp-fleischmann.text: Well-run week with genuine outdoor skills taught. Kids came home dirty and happy.

camp-bartlett.author: Scoutmaster from Ogden
camp-bartlett.text: Affordable, well-staffed, and the horse program is a rare treat at this price. Dust and afternoon wind are the only complaints.

camp-bartlett.author: Camper, age 13
camp-bartlett.text: Rifle range and the lake trip to Bear Lake were awesome. Showers were freezing.

camp-loll-id.author: Alum and Eagle Scout
camp-loll-id.text: The hike to Union Falls is the stuff of legend, and the staff spirit is the strongest I've encountered anywhere. It's primitive on purpose — that's the magic.

camp-loll-id.author: Parent of two Scouts
camp-loll-id.text: Kids came back talking about mudslides at the falls and campfire skits. Rough roads getting in, rustic everything, completely worth it.

camp-morrison.author: Boise troop parent
camp-morrison.text: Beautiful lakefront location you'd pay resort prices for anywhere else. Program was solid if not flashy; the kids loved the cold-water polar plunge.

camp-tahosa.author: Venturing crew advisor
camp-tahosa.text: Perfect base for Indian Peaks treks — small, quiet, and genuinely alpine. Not the camp for a first-year wanting a big dining hall scene.

melita-island.author: Scoutmaster from Missoula
melita-island.text: An entire island to yourselves on Flathead Lake — sailing program is superb and the sunsets are absurd. Weather can strand the boat shuttle occasionally; build in slack.

melita-island.author: Camper, age 14
melita-island.text: Living on an island for a week was the coolest thing I've done in Scouts. Water is COLD.

camp-gorsuch.author: Anchorage parent
camp-gorsuch.text: Real Alaskan camp experience without leaving the road system. Endless daylight means kids come home exhausted in the best way. Bring bug spray by the gallon.

gorham-scout-ranch.author: Santa Fe parent
gorham-scout-ranch.text: A friendly, small ranch camp with big scenery. Program depends heavily on that summer's staff, and this year's crew was great.

camp-phillips-wi.author: Eau Claire parent
camp-phillips-wi.text: Unfussy, affordable northwoods camp where new Scouts get real attention. Nothing extravagant, everything dependable.

gardner-dam-scout-camp.author: Green Bay Scoutmaster
gardner-dam-scout-camp.text: Rafting the Wolf River rapids as part of a standard camp week is a huge draw. River program staff take safety seriously. Campsites are pleasantly spread out.

camp-napowan.author: Chicago suburbs alum
camp-napowan.text: Generations of our troop grew up at Napowan. Two lakes means the waterfront never feels mobbed. Chapel point at sunset sticks with you.

camp-napowan.author: Camper, age 12
camp-napowan.text: Sailing was great and staff were funny. Some tents had holes and the mosquitoes found them.

d-bar-a-scout-ranch.author: Metro Detroit parent
d-bar-a-scout-ranch.text: The horse program makes it — my daughter lived at the corral all week. The ranch is huge, so bikes help getting around.

d-bar-a-scout-ranch.author: Camper, age 11
d-bar-a-scout-ranch.text: I rode a horse named Biscuit and shot a shotgun. Best week ever.
