cole-canoe-base.author: Scoutmaster from Saginaw
cole-canoe-base.text: River treks with camp support are a perfect step before the big high-adventure bases. Riverfront sites are the ones to request.

cole-canoe-base.author: Camper, second year
cole-canoe-base.text: Canoeing all week was awesome. Tubes down the Rifle River on Friday is tradition. Food was meh.

camp-manatoc.author: Akron-area alum
camp-manatoc.text: The chapel in the pines and the old lodge halls are pure 1930s craftsmanship. Being wrapped inside a national park means real trails out the back of every campsite.

camp-manatoc.author: Parent of a first-year
camp-manatoc.text: Tradition-rich without being stuffy. The staff clearly grew up here. Summer weeks book out — reserve early.

camp-friedlander.author: Cincinnati Scoutmaster
camp-friedlander.text: Named for Dan Beard country and runs like a machine — organized merit badge program, solid food, good ranges. Less rustic than some prefer, which suits plenty of families fine.

camp-friedlander.author: Camper, age 13
camp-friedlander.text: The lake tower jump is the best thing there. Lines for the climbing wall were long.

seven-ranges-scout-reservation.author: Canton-area parent
seven-ranges-scout-reservation.text: Dependable Ohio Scouting — kids earned badges, sang too loud, slept hard. Hills between program areas earn their reputation.

ransburg-scout-reservation.author: Scoutmaster from Indianapolis
ransburg-scout-reservation.text: Ransburg's waterfront on Lake Monroe rivals anything in the country — skiing, sailing, the works. Staff spirit at campfires is unmatched. Book early; it fills with out-of-state troops.

ransburg-scout-reservation.author: Camper, age 14
ransburg-scout-reservation.text: Water skiing and the blob. Enough said. Hilly walks and hot Indiana humidity are the only downside.

camp-krietenstein.author: Terre Haute parent
camp-krietenstein.text: Small and personal — staff knew our son by name day one. Program list is shorter than the mega-camps, but everything offered was done well.

canyon-camp.author: Rockford Scoutmaster
canyon-camp.text: People don't believe Illinois has canyons until they camp here. Waterfall hikes, great staff, honest patrol cooking. Pool instead of a lake is the one trade-off.

ingersoll-scout-reservation.author: Peoria-area parent
ingersoll-scout-reservation.text: Good value and a caring staff. It's humble compared to the famous camps, but our new Scouts came home wanting to return.

h-roe-bartle-scout-reservation.author: Mic-O-Say tribesman and dad
h-roe-bartle-scout-reservation.text: Bartle is a way of life in Kansas City — ten-day sessions, the Tribe of Mic-O-Say, paint and eagle claws passed down from grandfathers. Nowhere else in Scouting is quite like it.

h-roe-bartle-scout-reservation.author: First-time parent
h-roe-bartle-scout-reservation.text: The tradition and scale are extraordinary, though the ten-day session and Missouri heat are a real commitment for a first-year camper.

theodore-naish-scout-reservation.author: Kansas City parent
theodore-naish-scout-reservation.text: Convenient, well-kept, and welcoming for younger Scouts working up to Bartle. July on the Kansas bluffs is hot; the pool earns its keep.

s-bar-f-scout-ranch.author: St. Louis Scoutmaster
s-bar-f-scout-ranch.text: Famous Eagle and Gamble give troops two distinct camps on one gorgeous lake. Sailing and the mile swim are rites of passage. Ozark heat in late July is formidable.

s-bar-f-scout-ranch.author: Camper, age 12
s-bar-f-scout-ranch.text: The lake is huge and the staff were great. Ticks. So many ticks.

beaumont-scout-reservation.author: St. Louis County parent
beaumont-scout-reservation.text: Twenty minutes from home and our Cub had a full, happy week. It's more suburban-adjacent than wild, which honestly made drop-off easy.

camp-geiger.author: Three-generation Geiger family
camp-geiger.text: Small camp, giant tradition — Mic-O-Say ceremonies here shaped my whole family. Everyone is welcomed like kin.

camp-mitigwa.author: Des Moines parent
camp-mitigwa.text: Solid Iowa Scouting — kind staff, good ranges, honest food. The pool area could use updating but nobody seemed to mind.

little-sioux-scout-ranch.author: Omaha-area Scoutmaster
little-sioux-scout-ranch.text: The Loess Hills terrain is unlike anywhere else in the Midwest, and the camp's story of rebuilding gives the place real heart. Steep trails — bring broken-in boots.

camp-cedars.author: Omaha parent
camp-cedars.text: Well-organized week and an easy drive from Omaha. Nebraska summer humidity and mosquitos come free of charge.

medicine-mountain-scout-ranch.author: Scoutmaster from Sioux Falls
medicine-mountain-scout-ranch.text: Camping in the Black Hills with Rushmore day trips built in — troops drive from ten states for this. Program is simple but the setting does the heavy lifting.

camp-wilderness-mn.author: Fargo troop parent
camp-wilderness-mn.text: Classic Minnesota lake camp — fishing off the dock, cool nights, no frills. Worth the long drive from Fargo.

camp-tom-hale.author: Tulsa Scoutmaster
camp-tom-hale.text: The Ouachitas surprise people — real ridgeline hikes and great views. Program facilities are modest; the mountains are the draw.

slippery-falls-scout-ranch.author: Camper from Ardmore
slippery-falls-scout-ranch.text: Sliding down the actual slippery falls is the best thing at any camp anywhere. Hot as an oven by afternoon.

buffalo-trail-scout-ranch.author: El Paso Scoutmaster
buffalo-trail-scout-ranch.text: The Davis Mountains are Texas's best-kept secret — mile-high mornings, horse treks, and stargazing that rivals the McDonald Observatory next door. It's far from everywhere; that's the point.

buffalo-trail-scout-ranch.author: Camper, age 15
buffalo-trail-scout-ranch.text: Summited Baldy and saw the Milky Way like a photo. Long, long bus ride to get there.

worth-ranch.author: Fort Worth alum
worth-ranch.text: The Brazos, the bluffs, and CCC-era stonework — Worth Ranch feels carved out of Texas history. July heat is brutal; drink water like it's your job.

sid-richardson-scout-ranch.author: Dallas-area parent
sid-richardson-scout-ranch.text: Lake program is the star — sailing, skiing, the works. Texas sun is relentless, and shade in some campsites is thin.

camp-constantin.author: Camper from Plano
camp-constantin.text: Possum Kingdom is the clearest lake in Texas and camp sits right on it. Ski boats all afternoon. The walk up from the waterfront in 100 degrees builds character.

camp-constantin.author: Troop dad
camp-constantin.text: First-rate aquatics staff. Non-water program is thinner, so pick this camp for the lake.

trevor-rees-jones-scout-camp.author: Dallas parent
trevor-rees-jones-scout-camp.text: The nicest Scout camp facilities we've seen in Texas — modern, clean, thoughtfully laid out. Purists may miss the rustic feel; my first-year camper did not.

camp-strake.author: Houston Scoutmaster
camp-strake.text: The new Strake is state-of-the-art — great aquatics center, dining hall with actual AC. It's still growing its traditions after the move, but the bones are exceptional.

camp-strake.author: Camper, age 12
camp-strake.text: Everything is new and clean. Swimming was great. It rained and everything turned to red mud.

lost-pines-scout-reservation.author: Austin parent
lost-pines-scout-reservation.text: Close to Austin and welcoming to new Scouts. You can still see fire scars in the forest, which the nature staff turn into a great ecology lesson.

camp-orr.author: Scoutmaster from Fayetteville
camp-orr.text: Floating the Buffalo under 400-foot bluffs from your own campsite is extraordinary. Rustic camp, rough road in, zero complaints from the boys.

camp-orr.author: Camper, age 13
camp-orr.text: Canoed past cliffs and saw an elk. The swimming hole beats any pool.

camp-avondale.author: Baton Rouge parent
camp-avondale.text: Our troop's home camp for years — dependable staff and a nice lake. Louisiana humidity means everything stays damp all week.

hood-scout-reservation.author: Jackson-area Scoutmaster
hood-scout-reservation.text: Honest, affordable Scouting with a good-sized lake. Facilities are dated but the staff heart is genuine.

camp-la-no-che.author: Orlando Scoutmaster
camp-la-no-che.text: One of the best-equipped camps in the Southeast — the aquatics center and COPE course are top shelf. Florida in July is a sauna; program wisely front-loads mornings.

camp-la-no-che.author: Camper, age 12
camp-la-no-che.text: Zipline into the week, swimming every day. Watch for fire ants at the campsites.

camp-shands.author: Jacksonville parent
camp-shands.text: No-frills Florida Scouting done by people who care. The lake and shooting sports carried the week; the heat is what it is.

camp-tanah-keeta.author: Palm Beach County parent
camp-tanah-keeta.text: Paddling the wild Loxahatchee is special. Camp itself is compact and buggy in summer, but staff enthusiasm makes up for it.

camp-rainey-mountain.author: Northeast Georgia alum
camp-rainey-mountain.text: CRM does tradition right — the Friday campfire above the lake gives you chills. Rustic sites, cold creek, exactly as it should be.

camp-sequoyah-al.author: Birmingham Scoutmaster
camp-sequoyah-al.text: Being next to Cheaha gives older Scouts real hiking. Camp program is standard-issue but delivered warmly. August humidity is heavy.

camp-raven-knob.author: Scoutmaster from Winston-Salem
camp-raven-knob.text: The hike to the top of the Knob at sunrise, the lake, the sheer scale — Raven Knob earns its legend. Order of the Arrow ceremonies here are moving.

camp-raven-knob.author: Camper, age 13
camp-raven-knob.text: Best week of my summer, twice now. Dining hall songs go hard. Uphill walks everywhere though.

camp-daniel-boone.author: Troop leader from Charlotte
camp-daniel-boone.text: At 3,200 feet the nights are cool even in July. High-adventure options into Shining Rock are outstanding. The river through camp is the best feature.

camp-daniel-boone.author: Camper, age 12
camp-daniel-boone.text: Caught my first trout and hiked a waterfall. The mountain fog makes mornings freezing.

camp-grimes.author: Charlotte parent
camp-grimes.text: Our troop's home camp — reliable staff, pretty setting, good value. Facilities are simple; the traditions are the draw.

camp-ho-non-wah.author: Charleston-area parent
camp-ho-non-wah.text: Saltmarsh Scouting is its own world — kayaking tidal creeks, catching blue crabs off the dock. Pluff mud and no-see-ums included free.

camp-coker.author: Florence-area alum
camp-coker.text: Swimming in the tea-colored lake and campfires under the pines — Coker hasn't changed much, thankfully. Facilities could use some love.

skymont-scout-reservation.author: Chattanooga parent
skymont-scout-reservation.text: Bluff-top views and caving trips set Skymont apart. The camp itself is plain, but the plateau setting is quietly spectacular.

camp-powhatan.author: Roanoke Scoutmaster
camp-powhatan.text: The reservation is enormous — Powhatan for merit badges, Ottari for high adventure, Claytor Lake nearby. Strong staff and mountain evenings that need a hoodie.

camp-powhatan.author: Camper, age 13
camp-powhatan.text: Loved climbing and the lake. The hill up to our site was a daily workout.

camp-shenandoah.author: Valley troop parent
camp-shenandoah.text: A gem of a small camp — staff learn every kid's name and the valley views are lovely. Program list is shorter than the mega-camps.

camp-t-brady-saunders.author: Richmond Scoutmaster
camp-t-brady-saunders.text: Well-run week close to Richmond — high ropes and waterfront are the standouts. Humid lowland site, so pack accordingly.

goshen-scout-reservation.author: DC-area Scoutmaster
goshen-scout-reservation.text: Six camps around one big lake means every troop finds its fit, from patrol cooking at Bowman to dining hall life at Marriott. The drive from DC is worth it.

goshen-scout-reservation.author: Camper, age 11
goshen-scout-reservation.text: Sailing on Lake Merriweather was awesome. Our campsite was a serious hike from the dining hall.

pipsico-scout-reservation.author: Hampton Roads parent
pipsico-scout-reservation.text: Finding fossil shark teeth on the James River beach was the week's hit. Camp is rustic and humid, staff are wonderful.

camp-rodney.author: Delaware troop leader
camp-rodney.text: Sailing the upper Chesapeake from your own camp beach is hard to beat. Oldest traditions in the Del-Mar-Va council and a first-rate waterfront staff.

camp-rodney.author: Camper, age 12
camp-rodney.text: Sailing and jet ski demos were great. Jellyfish showed up in late July.

broad-creek-memorial-scout-reservation.author: Baltimore-area alum
broad-creek-memorial-scout-reservation.text: Broad Creek's stream valley is prettier than people expect an hour from Baltimore. Traditions run deep; some facilities run old.

horseshoe-scout-reservation.author: Chester County alum
horseshoe-scout-reservation.text: The Octoraro bend, the parade field, ninety-plus years of troop flags — Horseshoe is tradition incarnate. Swimming in the creek beats any pool.

camp-yawgoog.author: Rhode Island alum, 40 years on
camp-yawgoog.text: Three camps, one pond, a century of songs — the Sunday dress parade and waterfront rescue races feel timeless. Yawgoog is practically a Rhode Island institution.

camp-yawgoog.author: Camper, age 12
camp-yawgoog.text: The Bucklin memorial hike and the pond swims were my favorites. Food in the Sandy Beach mess hall was actually good.

camp-yawgoog.author: First-time parent
camp-yawgoog.text: Enormous but well-organized. Our shy first-year found his feet by Wednesday. Sites near the parade field are loud.

ten-mile-river-scout-camps.author: Brooklyn Scoutmaster
ten-mile-river-scout-camps.text: TMR gives NYC kids twelve thousand acres of forest — canoeing the Delaware, the museum of a century of city Scouting. Keowa and Aquehonga each have their own flavor.

ten-mile-river-scout-camps.author: Camper from Queens
ten-mile-river-scout-camps.text: First place I ever saw the Milky Way. Bathhouses are old but the lake makes up for everything.

camp-no-be-bo-sco.author: NJ Scoutmaster
camp-no-be-bo-sco.text: Yes, it's the Friday the 13th camp — the staff lean into it with good humor at the right moments. More importantly it's a tight, spirited camp with a beautiful lake.

camp-no-be-bo-sco.author: Camper, age 13
camp-no-be-bo-sco.text: Telling movie stories at the campfire was epic. Waterfront staff were strict but fair.

winnebago-scout-reservation-nj.author: Morris County parent
winnebago-scout-reservation-nj.text: Convenient and complete — good ranges, decent lake, caring staff. It's suburban-close, which cuts both ways.

citta-scout-reservation.author: Ocean County parent
citta-scout-reservation.text: Swimming in a tea-dark Pine Barrens lake is a Jersey rite of passage. Quiet, sandy, full of deer flies in July.

curtis-s-read-scout-reservation.author: Westchester Scoutmaster
curtis-s-read-scout-reservation.text: Adirondack camping with real mountains to climb and a gorgeous lake. Waubeeka's dining hall spirit versus Buckskin's patrol cooking — pick your culture.

massawepie-scout-camps.author: Rochester-area alum
massawepie-scout-camps.text: Massawepie is the Adirondacks distilled — misty ponds, canoe treks, no crowds. The eskers and bogs make the nature program genuinely special.

massawepie-scout-camps.author: Camper, age 14
massawepie-scout-camps.text: Canoeing between ponds all week was amazing. Cold rain two days straight was not.

onteora-scout-reservation.author: Suffolk County parent
onteora-scout-reservation.text: Worth the drive upstate — real mountains for Island kids, solid staff. Cabin-free rustic camping, so pack for rain.

june-norcross-webster-scout-reservation.author: Eastern CT parent
june-norcross-webster-scout-reservation.text: Small pond, small camp, big heart. Our first-years were never lost in a crowd.

horace-a-moses-scout-reservation.author: Springfield-area Scoutmaster
horace-a-moses-scout-reservation.text: Honest Berkshires camping — a pretty pond, decent ranges, staff who return year after year. Facilities are older but cared for.

camp-wah-tut-ca.author: Greater Boston Scoutmaster
camp-wah-tut-ca.text: One of the last true patrol-cooking camps in New England — kids learn more in a week here than a summer of dining halls. Not for troops that want convenience.

griswold-scout-reservation.author: NH troop parent
griswold-scout-reservation.text: Lakes Region setting with horses and a good climbing program. Blackflies in late June are the toll; July weeks are safer.

mount-norris-scout-reservation.author: Vermont Scoutmaster
mount-norris-scout-reservation.text: Sunrise from Mount Norris and a swim in Lake Eden before lunch — pure Vermont. Small camp where troops shape their own week.

resica-falls-scout-reservation.author: Philadelphia Scoutmaster
resica-falls-scout-reservation.text: Swimming below Resica Falls and fly fishing legendary trout water — few camps have a centerpiece like this. Program has sharpened in recent years.

resica-falls-scout-reservation.author: Camper, age 12
resica-falls-scout-reservation.text: The falls are awesome. The hills between campsites are less awesome.

camp-minsi.author: Lehigh Valley alum
camp-minsi.text: Stillwater Lake sunsets from the dining hall porch are the memory that sticks. Classic Pocono camp with a loyal staff family.

hawk-mountain-scout-reservation.author: Reading-area parent
hawk-mountain-scout-reservation.text: Solid Pennsylvania Scouting with the AT practically next door. Raptor-watching field trips are a unique touch. Facilities are workmanlike.

heritage-scout-reservation.author: Pittsburgh Scoutmaster
heritage-scout-reservation.text: Rafting the Yough at Ohiopyle as part of camp week is a huge draw. The Laurel Highlands stay cool when the city bakes.

camp-conestoga-pa.author: Johnstown-area alum
camp-conestoga-pa.text: Small mountain camp where the staff remembers your name from last summer. Cold nights, warm community.

camp-andree-clark.author: Troop leader from the Hudson Valley
camp-andree-clark.text: Camping where Girl Scouting's camp tradition literally began gives the place weight. Program is classic — fires, hikes, badges — and counselors are nurturing.

camp-andree-clark.author: Parent of a Brownie
camp-andree-clark.text: Sweet, historic camp so close to the city. Facilities are old; the spirit is not.

camp-kaufmann.author: Westchester parent
camp-kaufmann.text: My daughter went nervous and came home planning next year. Horseback add-on was worth it. Communication before camp could be smoother.

camp-comstock.author: Alum, camper then counselor
camp-comstock.text: Cayuga at dusk from the hill is the image I carry of my whole childhood. Rustic cabins, strong sisterhood, zero pretense.

camp-bonnie-brae.author: Alum of eight summers
camp-bonnie-brae.text: A hundred-plus years of girls have sung the same graces over dinner. The lake, the horses, the candlelight ceremonies — Bonnie Brae does tradition better than anywhere.

camp-bonnie-brae.author: First-time camp parent
camp-bonnie-brae.text: Wonderful staff and history everywhere. Cabins are rustic and the Berkshire nights get cold — pack the extra blanket they tell you to pack.

camp-lou-henry-hoover.author: North Jersey parent
camp-lou-henry-hoover.text: A hidden gem on Swartswood Lake — my daughters beg to go back. Platform-tent living is real camping, not a resort.

camp-sacajawea-nj.author: South Jersey parent
camp-sacajawea-nj.text: Friendly, affordable, and close to home. Pool and archery were the hits. Some cabins need updating.

camp-archbald.author: Scranton-area alum
camp-archbald.text: A century of northeast PA girls learned to swim in Ely Lake. When the council discussed selling it, hundreds of alums showed up — that tells you everything.

camp-mosey-wood.author: Philadelphia-area parent
camp-mosey-wood.text: Beautiful Pocono lake and a caring staff. Our first-timer loved boating; the food got mixed reviews from the tent.

camp-small-valley.author: Harrisburg-area parent
camp-small-valley.text: Sweet valley setting and genuinely kind counselors. It's rustic — heavy rain made the paths a mud run one day and the girls loved it.

camp-furnace-hills.author: Lancaster parent
camp-furnace-hills.text: Close, affordable, and horse-centered — exactly what my daughter wanted. Sessions are short and sweet.

camp-may-flather.author: DC-area alum
camp-may-flather.text: May Flather summers made me who I am — river tubing, mountain hikes, singing until hoarse. Stone lodges from the 1930s give it storybook charm.

camp-potomac-woods.author: Northern Virginia parent
camp-potomac-woods.text: Perfect first sleepaway — short drive, warm staff, pool and river program. It's more gentle woods than rugged wilderness.

camp-mary-atkinson.author: Raleigh parent
camp-mary-atkinson.text: Our troop's favorite — good mix of water time and crafts. July sessions are steamy; the pool schedule saves the day.

camp-dorothy-thomas.author: Tampa parent
camp-dorothy-thomas.text: Horse week was everything to my daughter. Florida summer means afternoon storms and serious humidity — the schedule works around both.

camp-whispering-pines-la.author: New Orleans parent
camp-whispering-pines-la.text: A sweet piney-woods camp with sincere staff. It's humble and hot, and my daughter counts down the days every spring.

camp-texlake.author: Austin parent
camp-texlake.text: Lake Travis is the star — sailing and paddleboards all week. Drought years shrink the shoreline, but the staff adapt cheerfully.

camp-misty-meadows.author: Houston parent
camp-misty-meadows.text: The horse program is the real deal — progressive skills, not just pony rides. Pool time balances the Texas heat.

camp-agnes-arnold.author: Houston alum
camp-agnes-arnold.text: Summers at Agnes Arnold taught me to paddle, lead, and belt camp songs without shame. The treehouse units are still the coolest place to sleep in Texas.

camp-la-jita.author: San Antonio parent
camp-la-jita.text: The Sabinal swimming hole under the cypresses is worth the drive alone. Rustic, joyful, and cooler by the river than anywhere else in a Texas July.

camp-bette-perot.author: Dallas parent
camp-bette-perot.text: Big, well-equipped camp — the barn staff especially impressed us. It's a haul from Dallas but the girls sleep the whole ride home.

camp-daisy-hindman.author: Topeka alum
camp-daisy-hindman.text: Daisy is ninety years of Kansas girls on the same prairie. Riding through the Flint Hills at golden hour — nothing better.

camp-cedarledge.author: St. Louis parent
camp-cedarledge.text: Cedarledge turned my cautious kid into a trail rider and a climber in two summers. Ticks and humidity are the Missouri tax.

camp-lakamaga.author: Twin Cities parent
camp-lakamaga.text: A hundred years of Minnesota girls have camped this shoreline. Staff handled homesickness beautifully. Mosquito repellent is mandatory equipment.

camp-ehawee.author: La Crosse parent
camp-ehawee.text: Small enough that every counselor knew every girl. The coulee valley is beautiful. Cabins are basic and that's part of the charm.

camp-butternut-springs.author: Northwest Indiana parent
camp-butternut-springs.text: Solid, caring camp an hour from Chicago. The lake and archery were hits; the week flew by too fast, per my camper.

camp-libbey.author: Toledo-area parent
camp-libbey.text: Historic, leafy, and friendly. My daughter loved the horses and the WPA-era lodge. Some facilities show their age.

camp-ledgewood.author: Cleveland parent
camp-ledgewood.text: Sitting inside a national park, the hiking is phenomenal. Staff run a tight, warm ship. Sessions with horseback fill first.

camp-elliott-barker.author: Albuquerque parent
camp-elliott-barker.text: Aspens, elk bugles, and a tight-knit camp family. It's remote and rustic, which is exactly why we chose it.

camp-scherman.author: Orange County parent
camp-scherman.text: Well-organized big camp with a great riding barn. The mountain setting stays pleasant when the lowlands roast. Bus from OC made logistics easy.

camp-lakota-ca.author: LA parent
camp-lakota-ca.text: Close enough for an easy drop-off, remote enough to feel like real mountains. Horse sessions book out the day registration opens.

camp-osito-rancho.author: Inland Empire alum
camp-osito-rancho.text: Osito's opening campfire above Big Bear made me a camp person for life. Cool air, tall pines, songs I still remember word for word.

camp-bothin.author: Bay Area parent
camp-bothin.text: Lovely Marin setting and thoughtful, inclusive staff. Foggy mornings burn off into perfect afternoons. Pricier than camps farther out.

skylark-ranch.author: Peninsula parent
skylark-ranch.text: Riding through coastal hills with the Pacific in view — Skylark's horse program is the best girls' program in the Bay Area. Bring layers; the fog is faithful.

camp-paumalu-hi.author: Honolulu parent
camp-paumalu-hi.text: Hawaiian culture woven through everything, from oli chants to malama aina service. The ridge view of the North Shore is unbeatable.

double-h-ranch.author: Parent of a returning camper
double-h-ranch.text: They got my wheelchair-using daughter up on adaptive waterskis. She talks about Double H all year. The staff are angels with lifeguard certifications.

the-painted-turtle.author: Parent of a camper with kidney disease
the-painted-turtle.text: Dialysis-compatible camp — I didn't believe it existed. He came home singing camp songs and planning next year. Zero cost to us.

flying-horse-farms.author: Parent of a heart patient
flying-horse-farms.text: The med team reviewed every detail before we arrived, then let him just be a camper. He caught his first fish there.

victory-junction.author: Parent of a camper with spina bifida
victory-junction.text: He drove a real kart, rode a horse, and never once heard 'you can't.' The Petty family built something sacred here.

camp-sunshine-maine.author: Parent of a child with cancer
camp-sunshine-maine.text: The only camp that took all of us — including his little sister, who needed it just as much. We left with a community that still checks on us.

camp-simcha.author: Parent of a camper in remission
camp-simcha.text: One counselor per camper, oncology nurses on site, and more joy per square foot than anywhere on earth. Simcha means joy for a reason.

camp-mak-a-dream.author: Teen camper
camp-mak-a-dream.text: Everyone here gets it — no explaining chemo brain or ports. Montana sunsets with people who understand. I count the days until summer.

happiness-is-camping.author: Parent of siblings who attend together
happiness-is-camping.text: They welcomed both my kids — the patient and the sibling who's sacrificed so much. Chemo schedules were handled without fuss.

camp-okizu.author: Parent and longtime supporter
camp-okizu.text: Okizu carried our family through treatment years. Even after the fire took the campus, the community refused to let the camp die.

camp-ronald-mcdonald-good-times.author: Sibling camper, age 14
camp-ronald-mcdonald-good-times.text: There's a whole session just for siblings like me. First place I could talk about my brother's cancer without everyone getting weird.

camp-nejeda.author: Parent of a newly diagnosed camper
camp-nejeda.text: The first week since diagnosis I actually slept. He came home doing his own site changes and proud of it.

camp-nejeda.author: Alum, now a counselor
camp-nejeda.text: Nejeda taught me T1D doesn't have to shrink your life. Now I get to teach the next kids the same thing.

camp-sweeney.author: Alum of ten summers
camp-sweeney.text: Sweeney isn't a diabetes camp, it's a camp that happens to handle diabetes better than any hospital. Three weeks that shaped my whole identity.

camp-sweeney.author: Parent of a 9-year-old
camp-sweeney.text: Incredible medical care and spirit. Three weeks is long for little ones and Texas summer is brutal, but she thrived.

camp-kudzu.author: Parent of a first-time camper
camp-kudzu.text: Doctors in the dining hall, nurses on the zipline platform — he did everything every other kid does, safely. He's already asking about teen week.

camp-ho-mita-koda.author: Parent of a camper
camp-ho-mita-koda.text: Nearly a century of T1D camping and it shows in every routine. Affordable, historic, and full of heart.

camp-conrad-chinnock.author: Parent of two T1D kids
camp-conrad-chinnock.text: Both my kids go and both come home more independent with their care. The mountain air and pine trees don't hurt either.

easterseals-camp-harmon.author: Parent of a camper with cerebral palsy
easterseals-camp-harmon.text: The first camp that said yes to everything — swimming, archery, campfire — with adaptations already figured out. Staff treat campers with real dignity.

camp-ascca.author: Parent of a wheelchair-using camper
camp-ascca.text: Everything — the treehouse, the zipline, the boats — is genuinely accessible, not an afterthought. He calls it the best week of his year.

camp-wawbeek-wi.author: Longtime camper, now adult session attendee
camp-wawbeek-wi.text: I've come here for fifteen years. Wawbeek is where I'm most myself — the staff see me, not my chair.

camp-courageous-iowa.author: Parent of an adult camper who started as a kid
camp-courageous-iowa.text: Twenty years of summers here. The staff turnover is low and the welcome is unconditional. Iowa generosity keeps it affordable.

talisman-camps.author: Parent of a camper with ADHD
talisman-camps.text: Staff understood his impulsivity without punishing it. He finished a backpacking trip we never thought possible.

talisman-camps.author: Parent of a teen with ASD
talisman-camps.text: Real skill-building and honest communication from staff. Rustic facilities and a serious price, but the growth was worth it for us.

soar-adventure-camp.author: Parent of a camper with ADHD and dyslexia
soar-adventure-camp.text: For once his energy was an asset. He led a climb, cared for a llama, and came home taller somehow.

camp-huntington.author: Parent of a young adult camper
camp-huntington.text: They work on his IEP-style goals without him realizing it's work. One of the few camps serving campers into their twenties.

camp-northwood.author: Parent of a twice-exceptional camper
camp-northwood.text: After two failed camp attempts elsewhere, Northwood's structure and patience cracked the code. He's been back three summers.

camp-nuhop.author: Parent of a camper with sensory needs
camp-nuhop.text: Six kids per group with two counselors who actually read his intake paperwork. Affordable compared to the famous East Coast options.

ramapo-for-children.author: Social worker who refers families
ramapo-for-children.text: I send my toughest cases to Ramapo and they come back with wins. The staff training there is the gold standard in the field.

camp-holiday-trails.author: Parent of a medically complex camper
camp-holiday-trails.text: They handled a feeding tube, meds, and homesickness with equal grace. Sliding-scale tuition meant we could actually say yes.

camp-john-marc.author: Parent of a burn survivor
camp-john-marc.text: A week with other kids who have scars like hers, staffed by her actual hospital team. She came home wearing shorts again.

camp-good-days.author: Parent of a bereaved camper
camp-good-days.text: After her brother died, this was the only place she could grieve among kids who understood. Free of charge and full of grace.

center-for-courageous-kids.author: Parent of a camper with sickle cell
center-for-courageous-kids.text: Every activity is adapted and the med staff never miss a beat. He calls it his happy place, and it costs us nothing.

camp-ta-kum-ta.author: Parent of a camper in remission
camp-ta-kum-ta.text: The volunteers pour their hearts into it. She waterskied for the first time since diagnosis and cried happy tears. Entirely free.

camp-fantastic-special-love.author: Alum, now a volunteer nurse
camp-fantastic-special-love.text: I went as a patient and now I staff it as an oncology nurse. The NCI doctors right there mean parents can exhale for a week.

sunrise-day-camp-pearl-river.author: Parent of a camper on treatment
sunrise-day-camp-pearl-river.text: A full day camp he could attend between chemo appointments, with nurses right there. Free, with a bus to our door. Unbelievable gift.

camp-royall-autism.author: Parent of a nonverbal camper
camp-royall-autism.text: Staff trained specifically in autism, predictable schedules, and no judgment. My son who can't do birthday parties thrived for a whole week.

camp-riley-bradford-woods.author: Parent of a wheelchair-using camper
camp-riley-bradford-woods.text: Accessible cabins, adaptive boats, and staff who've seen it all thanks to the Riley Hospital connection. He was independent for a week.

bearskin-meadow-camp.author: Parent of a T1D camper
bearskin-meadow-camp.text: High-altitude backcountry camp where the whole staff counts carbs. She came home confident dosing on her own.

camp-del-corazon.author: Parent of a heart-transplant recipient
camp-del-corazon.text: Cardiologists on an island camp — I actually relaxed. He met other kids with zipper scars and felt normal for once. Free of charge.

camp-aldersgate.author: Parent of a camper with muscular dystrophy
camp-aldersgate.text: There's a week here for nearly every diagnosis, all accessible. Affordable and warmly staffed by people who clearly love it.

camp-rap-a-hope.author: Parent of a camper
camp-rap-a-hope.text: For one week she wasn't the sick kid — she was just a camper making friends. Nurses handled her port without her missing a beat. And it was free.

camp-oakhurst.author: Parent of a camper with spina bifida
camp-oakhurst.text: Over a hundred years of doing this well. Everything is wheelchair-accessible and the staff make independence the goal.

camp-loyaltown.author: Parent of an adult camper
camp-loyaltown.text: My son has gone since he was a boy and still counts down to it as an adult. The staff know him and love him.

id-tech-camps.author: Parent of a 12-year-old
id-tech-camps.text: My son built his first playable game in a week and hasn't stopped coding since. The college campus setting made him feel very grown up.

id-tech-camps.author: Parent of two campers
id-tech-camps.text: Instructors were enthusiastic college kids, some better teachers than others. It's pricey for one week, and quality varies by location. Kids still had fun.

digital-media-academy.author: Camper, age 15
digital-media-academy.text: Learned real editing software, not kid stuff. Some days dragged with lectures, but my final film was something I was actually proud of.

astrocamp-idyllwild.author: Parent of a returning camper
astrocamp-idyllwild.text: The only place my science-loving kid has found other kids exactly like her. Launching rockets by day, stargazing by night — she counts down the days all year.

astrocamp-idyllwild.author: Camper, age 13
astrocamp-idyllwild.text: The labs and liquid nitrogen demos are awesome. Cabins are pretty basic and it gets cold at night in the mountains, but that's part of it.

emagination-stem-camps.author: Parent of an 11-year-old
emagination-stem-camps.text: Great mix of screen time and outside time, which is exactly what we wanted. Dorm living took some adjusting for our younger kid.

national-computer-camps.author: Alum who attended in high school
national-computer-camps.text: This place started my software career. No fancy facilities, just real programming and sports breaks. The founder's passion sets the tone.

national-computer-camps.author: Parent of a teen
national-computer-camps.text: Solid instruction at a fraction of what the famous tech camps charge. Website and dorms feel dated, but my son learned a ton.

camp-watonka.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-watonka.text: My son blows things up safely in a real chem lab and then goes waterskiing. Rustic cabins, no screens, and he loves every minute.

camp-watonka.author: Alum
camp-watonka.text: Watonka made science cool before STEM was a buzzword. Facilities are old-school and the camp is small, which is either charming or limiting depending on your kid.

camp-invention.author: Parent of a 7-year-old
camp-invention.text: Affordable, close to home, and my daughter came back every day with some contraption she'd built. Quality depends on the local teachers running it.

catalina-sea-camp.author: Parent of a returning camper
catalina-sea-camp.text: Swimming with leopard sharks off a private cove — it's summer camp in a postcard. Staff are warm and the science is legit.

catalina-sea-camp.author: Camper, age 12
catalina-sea-camp.text: Best week of my life. The night snorkel was scary at first and then amazing. Food was just okay.

camp-kennedy-space-center.author: Parent of a 10-year-old
camp-kennedy-space-center.text: Where else does your kid eat lunch under a Saturn V? Counselors kept the science fun. Drop-off lines get long, plan ahead.

brevard-music-center.author: Camper, age 17
brevard-music-center.text: Playing full symphonies in an open-air hall in the mountains while pros perform next door — nothing else like it. Practice expectations are intense.

brevard-music-center.author: Parent of a violinist
brevard-music-center.text: Superb faculty and real orchestral repertoire. Cabins are humid and buggy, and your kid needs self-discipline to thrive here.

stagedoor-manor.author: Parent of a theater kid
stagedoor-manor.text: For a kid who lives for musicals, this is Mecca. Everyone gets cast, the productions are astonishing, and she found her people instantly.

stagedoor-manor.author: Alum
stagedoor-manor.text: Magical and intense — rehearsals all day in a converted Borscht Belt hotel with zero athletic fields. Not for kids who want a traditional camp, and the price is steep.

usdan-summer-camp-arts.author: Parent of two campers
usdan-summer-camp-arts.text: A serious arts education disguised as camp — my kids came home humming their orchestra pieces. The wooded campus is gorgeous.

usdan-summer-camp-arts.author: Parent from Queens
usdan-summer-camp-arts.text: Terrific teachers, but the bus ride is long and hot days on a hilly campus wear younger kids out.

meadowmount-school-of-music.author: Alum
meadowmount-school-of-music.text: Practice, eat, practice, sleep. It sounds brutal and it is — and my playing improved more in seven weeks than in the previous two years.

meadowmount-school-of-music.author: Parent of a cellist
meadowmount-school-of-music.text: Transformative for the right kid, miserable for the wrong one. Spartan rooms, strict practice checks, zero camp fluff. Know what you're signing up for.

camp-ballibay.author: Parent of a nonconformist kid
camp-ballibay.text: The camp for kids who don't fit the sports-camp mold. Total creative freedom, zero pressure, and staff who genuinely get quirky kids.

camp-ballibay.author: Alum
camp-ballibay.text: I loved the freedom, though kids who need structure can drift. Facilities are charmingly shabby farmhouse, not shiny.

appel-farm-arts-camp.author: Parent of a returning camper
appel-farm-arts-camp.text: The most accepting community my kid has ever been part of. She tried filmmaking, pottery, and songwriting in one session.

appel-farm-arts-camp.author: Camper, age 15
appel-farm-arts-camp.text: Everyone can be themselves here, which is rare. Bunks are old and the pool is small, but the vibe makes up for it.

idyllwild-arts-summer.author: Parent of a painter
idyllwild-arts-summer.text: Real studios, real faculty, and a campus that smells like pine sap. My daughter's portfolio came home transformed.

idyllwild-arts-summer.author: Camper, age 16
idyllwild-arts-summer.text: Amazing teachers and facilities. Less traditional camp bonding since kids rotate weekly, but the work you make is worth it.

socapa.author: Parent of an aspiring filmmaker
socapa.text: My son wrote, shot, and edited a short film in two weeks and came home with a portfolio piece. City programs mean dorms and supervision, not campfires.

walden-school-young-musicians.author: Alum, now a composer
walden-school-young-musicians.text: Walden taught me to think in music. Hearing a piece I wrote premiered at age 13 rewired my brain. A nerdy, joyful, singular place.

sewanee-summer-music-festival.author: Camper, age 16
sewanee-summer-music-festival.text: Great conductors and tons of repertoire. Weekly auditions keep you sharp but stressed. The campus looks like Hogwarts on a mountain.

luzerne-music-center.author: Parent of a bassist
luzerne-music-center.text: Where else does a 13-year-old get coached by orchestra pros and then jump in a lake? Tight-knit and inspiring.

bucks-rock-camp-ct.author: Alum
bucks-rock-camp-ct.text: Total creative freedom with pro-grade studios. I learned silversmithing, played in a band, and never once lined up for an activity I hated.

bucks-rock-camp-ct.author: Parent of a first-year camper
bucks-rock-camp-ct.text: Freedom is the point — kids who need someone structuring their day can flounder for the first week. Once she found her studios, she thrived.

cazadero-music-camp.author: Alum and parent
cazadero-music-camp.text: I went in the 80s and now my kids go. Playing jazz under old-growth redwoods is pure magic. Rustic platform tents, cold mornings, zero complaints.

camp-winnarainbow.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-winnarainbow.text: Tipis, tie-dye, and trapeze. My kid came home juggling clubs and preaching kindness. It's gloriously weird and deeply sweet.

camp-winnarainbow.author: Camper, age 12
camp-winnarainbow.text: The circus show at the end is the best. Outdoor showers and dust everywhere, but who cares when you can walk on stilts.

circus-smirkus-camp.author: Parent of an aerialist
circus-smirkus-camp.text: Real circus training from pros who tour. My daughter went from cartwheels to trapeze routines in two weeks and now dreams of the troupe.

greenwood-music-camp.author: Alum, professional violinist
greenwood-music-camp.text: Greenwood is sacred ground for string players — Brahms in the barn, swimming in the pond, friendships that last decades.

saddlebrook-tennis-camp.author: Parent of a tournament player
saddlebrook-tennis-camp.text: Old-school volume hitting in Florida heat — kids leave fitter and tougher. Resort lodging is comfortable; the program is all business.

evert-tennis-academy-camp.author: Parent of a junior player
evert-tennis-academy-camp.text: Serious coaching with genuine attention to technique, and John Evert's staff know the junior pathway cold. Smaller and more personal than the mega-academies.

john-newcombe-tennis-ranch.author: Alum who returned as a counselor
john-newcombe-tennis-ranch.text: Newk's is tennis camp with a soul — team cheers, legends dropping by, and coaches who remember your name years later.

john-newcombe-tennis-ranch.author: Parent of a 12-year-old
john-newcombe-tennis-ranch.text: Great blend of serious tennis and actual summer camp. Texas heat is no joke; sessions start at dawn for a reason.

van-der-meer-tennis-camp.author: Parent of a high school player
van-der-meer-tennis-camp.text: Sound, methodical instruction — my son's serve was rebuilt in a week. Housing is simple villas and the island location is a big plus.

nike-sports-camps.author: Parent of a soccer player
nike-sports-camps.text: Convenient, well-organized, and my daughter loved training on a college field. It's a franchise model, so research the specific director.

nike-sports-camps.author: Parent of a 10-year-old
nike-sports-camps.text: Fine week of basketball but big groups and generic drills. You're partly paying for the swoosh.

kutshers-sports-academy.author: Parent of a returning camper
kutshers-sports-academy.text: Kids pick their own sports schedule, which kept my non-specialist happy. Storied name, decent facilities, food gets mixed reviews.

hoop-group-elite-camps.author: Camper, age 16
hoop-group-elite-camps.text: Games all day against real competition, and college coaches actually walk the gyms at elite weeks. Dorms are rough and days are long.

nbc-basketball-camps.author: Parent of a 14-year-old
nbc-basketball-camps.text: The most demanding week of my son's summer — conditioning, skills, and real talks about attitude. He came home a better player and kinder kid.

nbc-basketball-camps.author: Camper, age 15
nbc-basketball-camps.text: You will run. A lot. Coaches push hard but it works. The motivational talks got a little preachy by day five.

julian-krinsky-camps.author: Parent of two campers
julian-krinsky-camps.text: One kid did tennis, the other cooking, same campus and logistics — very convenient. Programs vary in intensity, so read the descriptions closely.

j-robinson-wrestling-camps.author: Alum, college wrestler
j-robinson-wrestling-camps.text: Hardest thing I did as a teenager and the most valuable. You find out exactly who you are around day nine of the 14-day.

j-robinson-wrestling-camps.author: Parent of a 15-year-old
j-robinson-wrestling-camps.text: He came home leaner, tougher, and quietly proud. Be honest about whether your kid actually wants this — it breaks the unwilling.

international-gymnastics-camp.author: Parent of a level 7 gymnast
international-gymnastics-camp.text: She trained next to an Olympian on Tuesday and did the camp lake jump on Wednesday. Coaching quality is unmatched for a summer camp.

international-gymnastics-camp.author: Camper, age 13
international-gymnastics-camp.text: Got my back handspring on beam in one week. The gyms are huge and the coaches actually spot you properly.

flip-fest-gymnastics-camp.author: Parent of a returning camper
flip-fest-gymnastics-camp.text: Perfect balance — real skill progress in the gym plus the lake blob and campfires. She begs to go back every June.

cooperstown-baseball-camp.author: Parent of an 11-year-old
cooperstown-baseball-camp.text: Playing ball all day in Cooperstown itself — the setting does half the work. Coaches were patient with my late-blooming kid.

heartland-hockey-camp.author: Parent of a bantam player
heartland-hockey-camp.text: Serious ice time with real correction, plus fishing off the dock at night. Minnesota hockey culture at its best.

heartland-hockey-camp.author: Camper, age 12
heartland-hockey-camp.text: My edges got way better. Bunks are basic and 6 a.m. ice is early, but that's hockey.

pro-ambitions-hockey-camps.author: Parent of a squirt player
pro-ambitions-hockey-camps.text: Every drill has a compete element — no standing in lines. Pricey per hour of ice, but the intensity is real.

gary-gilchrist-golf-academy-camp.author: Parent of a junior golfer
gary-gilchrist-golf-academy-camp.text: Coaching depth is exceptional — swing video, on-course strategy, even journaling. My son's tournament scoring dropped within a month.

bishops-gate-golf-academy-camp.author: Parent of a 15-year-old golfer
bishops-gate-golf-academy-camp.text: Very professional operation with real assessment tech. International crowd, high expectations, and a price tag to match.

chelsea-piers-summer-camp.author: Manhattan parent
chelsea-piers-summer-camp.text: Ice rink, climbing wall, and gymnastics under one roof in the city — my kids never got bored. Worth the eye-watering price for us.

chelsea-piers-summer-camp.author: Parent of a 7-year-old
chelsea-piers-summer-camp.text: Fantastic facilities, though it's indoors most of the day and drop-off logistics on the West Side Highway are a hassle.

roads-end-farm.author: Parent of a returning camper
roads-end-farm.text: It's about the horses, not competition — my daughter learned to care for a horse, not just sit on one. Waitlists are real; book early.

roads-end-farm.author: Alum
roads-end-farm.text: Twenty years later I still think about the barn mornings. Small, old-fashioned, and completely magical.

black-river-farm-and-ranch.author: Parent of a first-time camper
black-river-farm-and-ranch.text: She rode every single day and the counselors handled first-week homesickness like pros. Great value compared to Eastern horse camps.

black-river-farm-and-ranch.author: Alum
black-river-farm-and-ranch.text: Loved my summers there. It's horses, horses, horses — girls wanting lots of other activities may find it narrow.

camp-rim-rock.author: Parent of two campers
camp-rim-rock.text: The riding program is exceptional and the all-girls culture is genuinely confidence-building. Sessions sell out by winter.

camp-rim-rock.author: Camper, age 12
camp-rim-rock.text: Horses and the pool are the best. Cabins have no electricity, which I ended up loving even if my phone-charging dreams died.

culver-summer-schools-camps.author: Parent of a returning camper
culver-summer-schools-camps.text: Riding with the cavalry troop and sailing on a private lake — the traditions run deep and the leadership growth is visible.

culver-summer-schools-camps.author: Alum
culver-summer-schools-camps.text: Uniforms, inspections, and bugle calls aren't for everyone, but Culver gave me discipline and lifelong friends from around the world.

camp-friendship-va.author: Parent of an equestrian camper
camp-friendship-va.text: The riding-focused option gives real daily instruction, not pony rides. Very international bunkmates, which our daughter loved.

rawhide-ranch-camp.author: Parent of a 9-year-old
rawhide-ranch-camp.text: She learned to lope, groomed her horse every day, and even tried cattle sorting. The ranch atmosphere is authentic, right down to the dust.

high-cascade-snowboard-camp.author: Camper, age 15
high-cascade-snowboard-camp.text: Snowboarding in July with pros dropping in next to you. The private lane and park crew are unreal. Legs destroyed, worth it.

high-cascade-snowboard-camp.author: Parent of a returning camper
high-cascade-snowboard-camp.text: Superbly run and safety-conscious, though it's expensive and the mountain weather can eat a day or two of riding.

windells-camp.author: Alum
windells-camp.text: Skiing in the morning, concrete bowls all evening — I progressed more in a week than all winter. The campus is an action-sports playground.

windells-camp.author: Parent of a 13-year-old skier
windells-camp.text: He loved every second. Supervision is decent but it's a loose, skate-culture vibe — know your kid.

mt-hood-summer-ski-camps.author: Parent of a U14 racer
mt-hood-summer-ski-camps.text: Full-length GS courses in June and coaches who've worked national-team athletes. It's the summer training our race club can't provide.

paskowitz-surf-camp.author: Parent of a first-time surfer
paskowitz-surf-camp.text: By Wednesday she was trimming down the line at San-O. The family history makes it feel like joining a tribe, not buying lessons.

endless-summer-surf-camp.author: Parent of an 11-year-old
endless-summer-surf-camp.text: Camping on the beach and surfing at dawn — he came home sunburned, salty, and completely hooked. Instructors watched the water like hawks.

surf-diva-surf-camp.author: Parent of a 9-year-old girl
surf-diva-surf-camp.text: Patient, stoked instructors got my nervous kid standing on day one. La Jolla Shores is the perfect learner wave.

aloha-beach-camp.author: Valley parent
aloha-beach-camp.text: The pick-your-days scheduling saved our summer, and my kids adore the counselors. Long bus rides from the Valley are the only gripe.

ron-jon-surf-school-camp.author: Parent of a 8-year-old
ron-jon-surf-school-camp.text: Warm water, small waves, and instructors who kept every kid in sight. She was riding whitewater solo by Friday.

ymca-camp-surf.author: Parent of a returning camper
ymca-camp-surf.text: Tents on the sand, surfing every morning, and a fraction of private-camp prices. Registration fills within days of opening.

ymca-camp-surf.author: Camper, age 13
ymca-camp-surf.text: Surfing and beach games all day. The water is cold and the wetsuits are well-used, but the bonfires make up for it.

national-debate-forum-camps.author: Camper, age 16
national-debate-forum-camps.text: My cases got dramatically better in two weeks and I placed at nationals that fall. Long lab days — bring stamina and a laptop.

national-debate-forum-michigan.author: Alum, college policy debater
national-debate-forum-michigan.text: Seven weeks of Michigan changed my whole understanding of the activity. Brutal workload, elite coaching, lifelong debate friends.

national-debate-forum-michigan.author: Parent of a debater
national-debate-forum-michigan.text: Serious academic intensity, not a summer vacation. Dorm food and long research nights, but her results spoke for themselves.

concordia-language-villages.author: Parent of a returning camper
concordia-language-villages.text: She came home dreaming in French. The immersion is total — meals, cabins, songs, all in the language. A genuinely brilliant model.

concordia-language-villages.author: Alum
concordia-language-villages.text: El Lago del Bosque set me on the path to a Spanish major. First few days of only-target-language are disorienting, then it clicks.

middlebury-monterey-language-academy.author: Parent of a high schooler
middlebury-monterey-language-academy.text: The language pledge is strict and it works — he tested into a much higher level at school afterward. Pricey but transformative.

duke-tip-summer-studies.author: Parent of a gifted 8th grader
duke-tip-summer-studies.text: Three weeks of one subject at college depth, with peers who love learning as much as she does. More classroom than camp, which suited her.

national-student-leadership-conference.author: Camper, age 16
national-student-leadership-conference.text: The mock trial and hospital simulations were genuinely useful for figuring out my interests. It's a bit pricey for what's essentially a busy college-preview week.

kenyon-young-writers.author: Alum, now an English major
kenyon-young-writers.text: Two weeks that made me believe I could be a writer. Generative exercises every morning, real workshop feedback, and a tight-knit crew of word nerds.

kenyon-young-writers.author: Parent of a writer
kenyon-young-writers.text: She filled two notebooks and found her people. It's academically intense — not a place for a reluctant writer.

iowa-young-writers-studio.author: Alum
iowa-young-writers-studio.text: Being at Iowa, the writing capital, as a teenager felt surreal. Admission is competitive and the workshops are rigorous — exactly what a serious young writer wants.

camp-euphoria-mathpath.author: Parent of a math-loving 12-year-old
camp-euphoria-mathpath.text: Finally a place where being obsessed with math is normal. He learned real proofs and made friends who speak his language.

hampshire-college-summer-studies-math.author: Alum, math PhD student
hampshire-college-summer-studies-math.text: HCSSiM rewired how I think. It's not about being fastest — it's about falling in love with mathematical ideas. Six weeks I'll never forget.

center-for-talented-youth-jhu.author: Alum
center-for-talented-youth-jhu.text: CTY was where I found my people — nerdy, intense, and joyfully weird. The classes were college-level and the dances were legendary.

center-for-talented-youth-jhu.author: Parent of a first-time camper
center-for-talented-youth-jhu.text: Academically outstanding and socially warm for gifted kids. It's school-in-summer, so a kid wanting a traditional camp will be disappointed.

camp-tawonga.author: Parent of two campers
camp-tawonga.text: Tawonga is a Bay Area institution for a reason. The Yosemite backpacking trips and the emotionally intelligent staff are unmatched. Sessions fill within hours of registration opening, so set an alarm.

camp-tawonga.author: Alum turned counselor
camp-tawonga.text: The river, Shabbat under the pines, the friendships - it shaped who I am. It leans progressive and touchy-feely, which is exactly what most families there want.

camp-tawonga.author: First-time camp parent
camp-tawonga.text: Wonderful values and my kid came home glowing. Only gripes: the waitlist game is stressful and it is not cheap, though they do offer real financial aid.

camp-ramah-california.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-ramah-california.text: Ramah Ojai gave my kids Jewish confidence you cannot teach at home. Strong community, great Israeli staff. Ojai heat in July is real, though.

camp-ramah-california.author: Alum from the 90s
camp-ramah-california.text: Lifelong friends and a genuine love of Judaism. The structure and davening schedule is a lot for kids who are not used to it - know what you are signing up for.

camp-ramah-galim.author: Parent of a first-year camper
camp-ramah-galim.text: Ocean adventures plus Shabbat ruach - my daughter is hooked. Being a younger camp, some logistics are still finding their groove, but the heart is enormous.

camp-ramah-galim.author: Camper, age 13
camp-ramah-galim.text: Surfing at Jewish camp! Foggy mornings are cold and the site is more rustic than you would expect, but the counselors make it awesome.

urj-camp-newman.author: Parent of an alum
urj-camp-newman.text: The community rallied after the fire and the rebuilt camp is beautiful. Song sessions at Newman are legendary - my kids sing those melodies all year.

urj-camp-newman.author: Former songleader
urj-camp-newman.text: Newman ruach is real. Watching shy kids find their voice by week two never got old. Facilities are a mix of brand-new and still-rebuilding.

camp-jca-shalom.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-jca-shalom.text: Inclusive in the truest sense - kids of every background and ability belong here. The farm and animals are the highlight. Bunks are older but well loved.

camp-jca-shalom.author: Alum
camp-jca-shalom.text: Shabbat on the hill at sunset is something I still think about. Food was hit or miss, but the community was everything.

camp-alonim.author: Parent of two Alonim kids
camp-alonim.text: The Israeli dancing alone is worth it - hundreds of kids dancing at once gives you chills. Non-denominational feel welcomes everyone from secular to observant.

camp-alonim.author: Alum
camp-alonim.text: Grew up there and the ruach is unmatched. The Simi Valley sun is brutal midday, but nobody seems to care once music starts.

camp-hess-kramer.author: Multi-generation camp family
camp-hess-kramer.text: Three generations of our family went to Hess Kramer. The canyon-to-ocean setting and the traditions are irreplaceable - watching it come back after the fire has been emotional.

camp-hess-kramer.author: Alum
camp-hess-kramer.text: Beach days and campfire songs I will never forget. The rebuild means some facilities are in flux, so ask what will be open your summer.

camp-mountain-chai.author: San Diego parent
camp-mountain-chai.text: A gem in the mountains - great lake trips, sweet Shabbat traditions, staff who genuinely know each kid. Smaller than the LA camps, which we saw as a plus.

camp-mountain-chai.author: Camper, age 12
camp-mountain-chai.text: Loved the zipline and color war. Cabins get cold at night even in summer, pack layers.

camp-gilboa.author: Parent of a movement kid
camp-gilboa.text: Gilboa turns kids into thoughtful, capable young people - they genuinely run parts of camp themselves. It is rustic and ideological, which is the whole point.

camp-gilboa.author: Alum
camp-gilboa.text: The most formative place of my youth. Facilities are bare-bones and the youth-leadership model means occasional chaos, but that chaos builds character.

eden-village-west.author: Parent of a sensitive kid
eden-village-west.text: The kindest camp culture we have encountered - zero body talk, zero cliques, lots of mud and goats. My anxious kid thrived. Food is farm-fresh and kosher.

eden-village-west.author: Camper, age 11
eden-village-west.text: I milked a goat and swam in the river every day. The no-electronics thing was hard for two days and then I forgot about my phone.

maccabi-sports-camp.author: Parent of a soccer kid
maccabi-sports-camp.text: Real coaching, real games, and my son came home singing Hebrew songs he refused to learn in religious school. Dorm living is comfortable compared to rustic camps.

maccabi-sports-camp.author: Camper, age 13
maccabi-sports-camp.text: Basketball all day was amazing. If you are not that into sports there is not much else to do, so come for the right reason.

mount-hermon-camps.author: Bay Area parent
mount-hermon-camps.text: Kids come home talking about the redwoods, the zip lines, and their counselors in that order. A hundred-plus years of experience shows in how smoothly weeks run.

mount-hermon-camps.author: Alum
mount-hermon-camps.text: Ponderosa teen weeks shaped my faith. It is a big operation with lots of groups on site, so it can feel busy rather than remote.

jenness-park-christian-camp.author: Youth pastor
jenness-park-christian-camp.text: We bring our group every year - the staff serve like crazy and the mountain setting resets kids fast. Book early; summer weeks go quickly.

jenness-park-christian-camp.author: Parent
jenness-park-christian-camp.text: Great week for my daughter with her church. Communication runs through the church group rather than camp, which takes some getting used to.

mission-springs-camps.author: San Jose parent
mission-springs-camps.text: Frontier Ranch was the perfect first sleepaway - redwoods, kind staff, only 40 minutes from home. My son felt safe and came back taller somehow.

mission-springs-camps.author: Camper, age 10
mission-springs-camps.text: The creek hike and campfire skits were my favorite. The food was just okay.

redwood-christian-park.author: Church group parent
redwood-christian-park.text: The setting under the redwoods is stunning and the price is right. Facilities are dated - think creaky bunks and cool mornings - but the ministry heart is genuine.

thousand-pines-christian-camp.author: LA parent
thousand-pines-christian-camp.text: Easy drive from the city and my kids felt like they were a world away. Counselors send photo updates and the high ropes course was the summer highlight.

thousand-pines-christian-camp.author: Camper, age 11
thousand-pines-christian-camp.text: Zipline and gaga ball all week! The cabins have a lot of kids in them so it gets loud at night.

calvin-crest-camp.author: Central Valley parent
calvin-crest-camp.text: A humble, beautiful camp - my daughter did the horsemanship week and adored her wrangler counselor. Not flashy, and that is why we like it.

calvin-crest-camp.author: Alum
calvin-crest-camp.text: Vespers on the point at sunset stays with you. Some buildings are showing their age.

angeles-crest-christian-camp.author: Youth group volunteer
angeles-crest-christian-camp.text: Shockingly close to LA yet feels like deep wilderness. Staff run a tight, energetic program. The winding drive up is not for queasy stomachs.

angeles-crest-christian-camp.author: Camper, age 13
angeles-crest-christian-camp.text: The blob and night games were great. Cabins are packed and mornings are cold even in July.

mile-high-pines-camp.author: Inland Empire parent
mile-high-pines-camp.text: Our church week here was fantastic - horses, ropes course, attentive staff. Feels family-run in the best way.

idyllwild-pines-camp.author: Church volunteer
idyllwild-pines-camp.text: A hundred years of camp history in those cedars. Buildings are vintage and the town of Idyllwild is a charming bonus for pickup day.

camp-maranatha-idyllwild.author: Parent
camp-maranatha-idyllwild.text: Solid, sincere week for my son with his youth group. Nothing fancy, but the counselors were wonderful and Idyllwild itself is magic.

circle-v-ranch-camp.author: Santa Barbara parent
circle-v-ranch-camp.text: Old-school camp goodness - my kids loved the hikes to the creek and the closing campfire. The scholarship program makes it genuinely open to everyone.

circle-v-ranch-camp.author: Camper, age 10
circle-v-ranch-camp.text: Archery and the pool were the best. It gets really hot and there are lots of bugs.

pilgrim-pines-camp.author: Parent
pilgrim-pines-camp.text: An open-hearted, open-minded church camp - my nonbinary kid felt completely embraced. Facilities are simple; the community is the draw.

camp-wawona.author: Adventist parent
camp-wawona.text: Where else does your kid summit in Yosemite at church camp? Vegetarian meals, Sabbath rhythms, and unbeatable scenery. Spots go fast given the tiny size.

camp-wawona.author: Camper, age 14
camp-wawona.text: We hiked to a waterfall inside Yosemite. Best week of my life, even with no meat at meals.

leoni-meadows-camp.author: Sacramento-area parent
leoni-meadows-camp.text: Horsemanship weeks are outstanding and the staff feel like extended family. The vegetarian food is better than my kids expected.

camp-cedar-falls.author: Parent
camp-cedar-falls.text: A gentle mountain week rooted in Adventist values. Facilities are older but cared for; my daughter loved the horses.

kidder-creek-camp.author: Parent of a horse-crazy kid
kidder-creek-camp.text: This is the real deal - my daughter rode mountain trails daily and came home smelling like horse and talking about Jesus and river rapids. Remote location means a long drive.

kidder-creek-camp.author: Camper, age 15
kidder-creek-camp.text: Rafting week was insane in the best way. You are truly out there - no phone service for miles.

woodleaf-young-life.author: Young Life leader
woodleaf-young-life.text: Woodleaf is a machine built for joy - excellent food, wild rec, and space for real conversations. Kids who came reluctantly beg to return.

woodleaf-young-life.author: Camper, age 16
woodleaf-young-life.text: I went for the zipline and stayed for the people. Every minute is planned, which was honestly kind of great.

sugar-pine-christian-camps.author: Youth group parent
sugar-pine-christian-camps.text: Beautiful little lake and dedicated staff. Cabins are rustic and it books through churches, but the value is excellent.

camp-st-francis.author: Catholic school parent
camp-st-francis.text: Beach walks, boogie boards, and gentle Franciscan spirituality - a rare combination. The site is small, so it feels like a big family.

camp-st-francis.author: Camper, age 11
camp-st-francis.text: Beach every day! The fog in the morning is cold and mass was a little long, but I want to go back.

st-dorothys-rest.author: Sonoma County parent
st-dorothys-rest.text: A tiny camp with a giant soul - 120 years of kids under those redwoods. Staff-to-camper ratio is remarkable and the inclusivity is genuine.

st-dorothys-rest.author: Alum
st-dorothys-rest.text: The chapel in the trees is one of the most peaceful places I know. Rustic in the truest sense - bring warm pajamas.

cyo-camp-occidental.author: SF parent
cyo-camp-occidental.text: A true community camp - kids from every neighborhood and income level, held together by staff who clearly love the place. Strong financial aid.

cyo-camp-occidental.author: Camper, age 12
cyo-camp-occidental.text: The redwood hikes and pool time were great. Cabins are old and the showers are ancient, but campfire made up for it.

mount-cross-camp.author: Lutheran parent
mount-cross-camp.text: Kind, unhurried, and deeply welcoming - exactly what young campers need. The redwood campfire bowl is lovely. Facilities are modest.

camp-de-benneville-pines.author: UU parent
camp-de-benneville-pines.text: My theater kid found their people here - talent shows, deep circle talks, total acceptance. It is unapologetically progressive, which is why we chose it.

camp-de-benneville-pines.author: Teen camper
camp-de-benneville-pines.text: The one week a year I feel completely myself. Cabins are drafty and the altitude makes the hikes hard, worth it.

pali-adventures.author: Parent of a creative kid
pali-adventures.text: My son did the stunt specialty and literally got set on fire (safely!). Staff from all over the world, incredible energy. You pay a premium for the spectacle.

pali-adventures.author: Camper, age 13
pali-adventures.text: I DJ-ed a real dance party at 13. Best two weeks ever. Food court style dining beats normal camp food.

pali-adventures.author: Parent
pali-adventures.text: Extremely fun, well run, and expensive. It is more like a resort of activities than an old-fashioned bonding camp - depends what you want.

camp-augusta.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-augusta.text: The staff training here is legendary - counselors practice communication skills most adults lack. My kid designed her own overnight quest. Enrollment opens and closes in a blink.

camp-augusta.author: Camper, age 12
camp-augusta.text: I learned blacksmithing, archery on horseback, and how to make cheese. Nobody makes you do anything, which makes you want to do everything.

kennolyn-camps.author: Silicon Valley parent
kennolyn-camps.text: Beautifully run - great food, gorgeous redwood setting, and the Saturday beach trips are a hit. It is one of the pricier NorCal options and worth it for us.

kennolyn-camps.author: Alum
kennolyn-camps.text: Three generations of the same family running it and you feel that continuity. The hilltop dining hall view never gets old.

mountain-camp-tahoe.author: SF parent
mountain-camp-tahoe.text: The elective model was perfect for my strong-willed kid - she sailed every single morning. Lake is cold and stunning. Sessions sell out by winter.

mountain-camp-tahoe.author: Camper, age 14
mountain-camp-tahoe.text: Wakeboarding at 7000 feet with your friends cheering from the boat. Enough said.

mountain-meadow-ranch.author: Parent
mountain-meadow-ranch.text: Small enough that every kid is known. My son fed pigs, rode daily, and learned woodshop from the owner himself. Remote - the drive is a commitment.

mountain-meadow-ranch.author: Alum
mountain-meadow-ranch.text: Decades later I still think about the pancake rides and the smell of the barn. A hidden gem few Californians know about.

plantation-farm-camp.author: Parent of two farm kids now
plantation-farm-camp.text: My city kids came home knowing how to milk a cow and set a table for forty. The farm chores are real, and that is exactly the magic.

plantation-farm-camp.author: Camper, age 13
plantation-farm-camp.text: Cheese-making, bonfires at the ocean, and my favorite goat, Pretzel. I cried at pickup because I did not want to leave.

camp-jack-hazard.author: Modesto parent
camp-jack-hazard.text: Real wilderness for a fraction of what fancy camps charge - the nonprofit keeps fees low and scholarships flowing. My daughter summited her first peak at 11.

camp-jack-hazard.author: Alum
camp-jack-hazard.text: CJH backpacking trips made me who I am. No electricity in the cabins, river-cold swims, zero regrets.

waltons-grizzly-lodge.author: Third-generation camp parent
waltons-grizzly-lodge.text: My father went, I went, now my kids go. The owners greet every camper by name and the lakefront mornings are pure gold. Books out a year in advance.

waltons-grizzly-lodge.author: Camper, age 12
waltons-grizzly-lodge.text: Waterskiing before breakfast and campfires every night. The banana boat and the 4th of July carnival are the best.

shaffers-high-sierra-camp.author: Tahoe-area parent
shaffers-high-sierra-camp.text: Like a guided adventure vacation disguised as camp - real climbing, real rafting, tiny group sizes. The owners know every kid deeply within days.

skylake-yosemite-camp.author: Parent of a returning camper
skylake-yosemite-camp.text: Skylake nails the balance - real traditions and waterfront freedom without military scheduling. The lake at sunset during campfire is unforgettable.

skylake-yosemite-camp.author: Alum
skylake-yosemite-camp.text: Twenty years later my Skylake friends are still my best friends. Hot afternoons, cold lake, perfect summers.

river-way-ranch-camp.author: LA parent
river-way-ranch-camp.text: The activity list is bonkers - my son did water ski, paintball, and animal care in one day. Valley heat is intense; they schedule water time wisely.

river-way-ranch-camp.author: Camper, age 12
river-way-ranch-camp.text: Go-karts and the banana boat were awesome. It is a big camp so you have to speak up to get your favorite activities.

douglas-ranch-camps.author: Bay Area parent
douglas-ranch-camps.text: Daily riding instruction that actually builds skill, plus a sweet, unhurried culture. Many families have gone for generations and it shows in the traditions.

douglas-ranch-camps.author: Alum
douglas-ranch-camps.text: Learned to ride, swim, and shoot archery in the golden Carmel hills. The place smells like eucalyptus and horses - heaven.

santa-catalina-summer-camp.author: Parent of a first-time camper
santa-catalina-summer-camp.text: Beautiful campus, gracious staff, and a lovely mix of sports and arts. Dorm rooms beat cabins for my comfort-loving daughter. More school-like than woodsy.

bar-717-ranch.author: Parent
bar-717-ranch.text: The real thing - horses with actual responsibility, garden-to-table meals, creek swims instead of pools. Far from everywhere, and that is the point.

bar-717-ranch.author: Alum
bar-717-ranch.text: Bar 717 summers were the freest I have ever felt. Learned to shoe a horse and bake bread in the same week.

camp-unalayee.author: Palo Alto parent
camp-unalayee.text: The most authentic wilderness camp in California - kids carry real packs on real trails. Not for comfort-seekers; absolutely for future mountaineers.

camp-unalayee.author: Alum
camp-unalayee.text: Unalayee is dirt, alpine lakes, and lifelong friends. The bus ride up the mountain road is an initiation in itself.

deer-crossing-camp.author: Parent of a teen
deer-crossing-camp.text: Boat-in access, no electricity, actual skill certifications - my son came home a competent sailor and a more confident person. Not a hand-holding camp.

coppercreek-camp.author: Parent
coppercreek-camp.text: Coppercreek feels like a big family ranch - my daughter picked horses every morning and ceramics every afternoon. Their comeback after the Dixie Fire says everything about the people.

canyon-creek-sports-camp.author: LA parent
canyon-creek-sports-camp.text: Perfect starter camp - close to the city, one-week options, and enough sports variety that my picky athlete never got bored.

canyon-creek-sports-camp.author: Camper, age 11
canyon-creek-sports-camp.text: Flag football and the waterslide were great. It gets dusty and hot, and the cabins are pretty basic.

camp-ocean-pines.author: Central Coast parent
camp-ocean-pines.text: Watching whales from camp is normal here. Small cabins, kind staff, reasonable price - a Central Coast treasure. Fog can hang around till noon.

camp-ocean-pines.author: Camper, age 9
camp-ocean-pines.text: We saw otters at the tide pools and made swords in woodshop. I want to live there.

camp-mendocino.author: SF parent
camp-mendocino.text: An incredible gift to San Francisco families - sliding-scale pricing and a program as rich as camps triple the cost. My son found mentors he still talks about.

camp-mendocino.author: Former counselor
camp-mendocino.text: Kids from every SF neighborhood becoming one community under thousand-year-old trees. Facilities are old but the magic is not.

camp-concord.author: East Bay parent
camp-concord.text: A public camp with a Tahoe address - the value is unbeatable and the staff culture is surprisingly strong for a city program. Weeks sell out fast to Concord residents.

camp-bob-waldorf.author: LA parent
camp-bob-waldorf.text: They made camp possible for our family, full stop. Scholarships, warm staff, and my son felt like he belonged from day one.

camp-bob-waldorf.author: Former counselor
camp-bob-waldorf.text: Kids who have never left their neighborhood catching frogs above the LA skyline - this camp changes trajectories. Facilities are humble and mighty.

griffith-park-boys-camp.author: LA parent
griffith-park-boys-camp.text: City prices for a real sleepaway week - my son did archery and night hikes and we could see camp from the freeway. Registration is a scramble when it opens.

griffith-park-boys-camp.author: Camper, age 10
griffith-park-boys-camp.text: We heard coyotes at night and swam every day. The cabins are old but the counselors were really nice.

camp-hollywoodland.author: Parent
camp-hollywoodland.text: My daughter slept in a cabin under the Hollywood sign for less than a day of theme park tickets. A genuinely sweet, old-fashioned program run by the city.

campus-by-the-sea.author: Parent
campus-by-the-sea.text: You arrive by boat to a cove with no roads - kids snorkel with garibaldi before breakfast. Simple cabins, unforgettable setting.

seacamp-san-diego.author: Parent of a bio-obsessed teen
seacamp-san-diego.text: This is real field science - boat trips, dissections, night snorkels. My daughter came home certain about marine biology as a career.

seacamp-san-diego.author: Camper, age 14
seacamp-san-diego.text: Held a swell shark, saw dolphins from the research boat, and the instructors treat you like scientists. Dorm-style housing is basic but fine.

camp-sea-lab.author: Monterey parent
camp-sea-lab.text: Kayaking past sea otters with marine-science educators - this camp uses the bay as its classroom brilliantly. Housing is college dorms, which older kids loved.

camp-woolman.author: Parent
camp-woolman.text: Consensus circles, pond swims, and zero competitive pressure - my conflict-averse kid finally loved camp. Quaker practices are inclusive of all faiths and none.

ymca-camp-marston.author: San Diego parent
ymca-camp-marston.text: The classic first camp - a week in Julian, kind counselors, archery and pond canoeing. Y pricing and financial aid make it doable for regular families.

ymca-camp-marston.author: Camper, age 10
ymca-camp-marston.text: The zipline over the pond was awesome. Food was okay, the bug juice is legendary though.

ymca-raintree-ranch.author: Parent of a rider
ymca-raintree-ranch.text: Twice-daily rides at a YMCA price point is unheard of. My daughter came home mucking-stall proud and asking for boots.

ymca-camp-oakes.author: Long Beach parent
ymca-camp-oakes.text: Affordable mountain camp with a real observatory - my son saw the Milky Way for the first time. Cabins are basic Y-style bunkhouses.

ymca-camp-oakes.author: Camper, age 11
ymca-camp-oakes.text: Canoeing and the campout under the stars were the best. It gets freezing at night and the hikes are steep.

ymca-camp-whittle.author: Parent
ymca-camp-whittle.text: A solid, honest YMCA week - my kids came home dusty and happy. Nothing fancy, plenty of heart, and the Y makes aid easy to request.

ymca-camp-fox.author: Glendale parent
ymca-camp-fox.text: The boat pulls into the cove and kids just erupt. Snorkeling, paddleboards, and campfires on the beach - hard to believe the price for an island camp.

ymca-camp-fox.author: Camper, age 12
ymca-camp-fox.text: We kayaked to a sea cave and saw a leopard shark (they are harmless!). The saltwater showers take getting used to.

ymca-camp-jones-gulch.author: Peninsula parent
ymca-camp-jones-gulch.text: Redwoods, horses, and a program that has onboarded nervous first-timers for ninety years. Strong financial aid through the Y.

ymca-camp-jones-gulch.author: Camper, age 9
ymca-camp-jones-gulch.text: I licked a banana slug on a dare and rode a horse named Biscuit. The showers were cold sometimes.

ymca-camp-campbell.author: San Jose parent
ymca-camp-campbell.text: Dependable and warm - my kids love the river and the counselor skits. Cabins and dining hall show their decades, which never bothered the kids.

ymca-camp-loma-mar.author: SF parent
ymca-camp-loma-mar.text: Gentle, green, and close to home - the perfect training-wheels sleepaway. My daughter's counselor wrote her a postcard afterward, which melted me.

ymca-camp-arroyo.author: East Bay parent
ymca-camp-arroyo.text: The greenest camp facilities I have seen and a kitchen that handles allergies flawlessly - it doubles as a medical camp site, and that expertise shows.

camp-three-falls.author: Scout parent
