camp-lantern-creek.text: The director's philosophy is real: girls are encouraged to be loud, muddy, and themselves. My daughter wrote a song and shot a bow in the same day. Small camp, big heart.

prude-ranch-summer-camp.author: El Paso parent
prude-ranch-summer-camp.text: Real ranch riding — not nose-to-tail pony circles — and the mountain air is glorious. It's remote and rough around the edges, which is exactly its charm.

prude-ranch-summer-camp.author: Alum
prude-ranch-summer-camp.text: Horses every day and the darkest starry skies in Texas. Bunkhouses are old-school. The drive is far for most Texans but worth it.

camp-mitre-peak.author: West Texas Scout mom
camp-mitre-peak.text: The desert mountain setting is unforgettable and the price is right. It's rustic and the drive is long from anywhere, but my daughter counts stars there every summer.

camp-zephyr.author: Youth group alum
camp-zephyr.text: Hot, loud, and holy — every South Texas Baptist kid has Zephyr stories. The lake and the friendships carry you through the humidity and plain bunks.

carolina-creek-christian-camp.author: Houston-area parent
carolina-creek-christian-camp.text: Feels like a water park and a youth retreat had a very well-organized baby. My kids came home exhausted and asking bigger questions about faith. Popular weeks fill by spring.

ymca-camp-classen.author: Oklahoma City parent
ymca-camp-classen.text: Classen is the camp every Oklahoman seems to have a story about. The Arbuckles are gorgeous and the horse program is a steal at Y prices. Cabins are basic but clean.

ymca-camp-classen.author: Alum
ymca-camp-classen.text: Swimming under the stars in the Arbuckles never gets old. Big camp, so first-timers should ride the buddy system hard the first few days.

camp-loughridge.author: Tulsa parent
camp-loughridge.text: You forget you're inside Tulsa city limits — lake, woods, ziplines. Counselors are warm and the faith element is gentle. Register early; weeks sell out.

shepherds-fold-ranch.author: Parent of a returning camper
shepherds-fold-ranch.text: Affordable, sincere, and personal — the director knows every kid. Horses are the big draw for my daughter. Facilities are modest ranch buildings.

dry-gulch-usa.author: Parent of two campers
dry-gulch-usa.text: The western town and nightly shows make it feel like a theme park with a message. Strongly charismatic worship style — know that going in. Kids beg to return.

camp-egan.author: Methodist parent
camp-egan.text: Peaceful creekside camp with kind staff and a real welcome for every kind of kid. Simple lodging, wonderful spirit.

camp-dakani.author: OKC parent
camp-dakani.text: Old-fashioned outdoor play ten minutes from downtown — fort building, creek stomping, campfire cooking. Staff handle my ADHD kiddo with real skill.

cross-point-camp.author: Youth group alum
cross-point-camp.text: Texoma sunsets from the bluff are unbeatable and the tubing is a blast. Buildings are older church-camp style, but nobody goes for the architecture.

ymca-camp-takatoka.author: Tulsa parent
ymca-camp-takatoka.text: Takatoka gave my son his first week away and he came back taller somehow. Great waterfront staff. Cabins and food are Y-standard — fine, not fancy.

camp-waluhili.author: Grandparent and Camp Fire alum
camp-waluhili.text: I went in the 60s and it still smells like the same campfire — wonderful continuity. My granddaughter loves the horses. Bring bug spray and low expectations for plumbing.

hale-scout-reservation.author: Scoutmaster
hale-scout-reservation.text: Beautiful mountain setting and a solid waterfront. It's a haul from anywhere, and July is steamy, but the trails and lake make it worth the drive.

shepherd-of-the-ozarks.author: Youth leader
shepherd-of-the-ozarks.text: The canyon property is stunning — waterfalls, bluffs, wild horses on the hills. Staff serve our group like family. Cell service is nonexistent, gloriously.

camp-bear-track.author: Little Rock parent
camp-bear-track.text: The lake is crystal clear and the brother-sister setup means one drive for both kids. Counselors write actual letters home. Feels personal in a way big camps don't.

camp-mitchell-ar.author: Episcopal parent
camp-mitchell-ar.text: Watching my kids watch the sunset off Petit Jean at vespers — that's the whole review. Gentle, welcoming camp for kids who aren't the sporty type.

camp-tanako.author: Methodist parent
camp-tanako.text: Sweet, unhurried lake camp — my daughter loved canoeing at sunrise. Cabins are older but the staff warmth more than compensates.

camp-wyldewood.author: Searcy parent
camp-wyldewood.text: The college-age counselors are the draw — they genuinely mentor. Simple facilities, strong values, easy price. Our whole congregation's kids go.

camp-grant-walker.author: 4-H parent
camp-grant-walker.text: Wonderfully affordable and well-chaperoned through our parish 4-H. My son loved the fishing and archery. It's an older facility and the July heat is fierce.

camp-hardtner.author: Alum and parent
camp-hardtner.text: Hardtner people get misty just saying the name. It's where every kid — quirky, quiet, loud — belongs completely. The pool and pines are simple; the community is extraordinary.

camp-istrouma.author: Baton Rouge parent
camp-istrouma.text: Close to home, gentle on the wallet, and my kids come back singing camp songs for weeks. Facilities are simple church-camp style but the counselors shine.

dry-creek-baptist-camp.author: Youth group alum
dry-creek-baptist-camp.text: Dry Creek summers shaped half the deacons in southwest Louisiana. Old wooden tabernacle, sno-cones, lifelong friends. Rustic in every sense.

camp-marydale.author: Baton Rouge Scout mom
camp-marydale.text: The equestrian weeks are the best value riding instruction in the region. Pretty, hilly property — rare for Louisiana. Cabins are camp-basic.

camp-bon-coeur.author: Heart mom
camp-bon-coeur.text: The only week of the year I don't hover. Cardiac nurses at every activity, and my daughter met other kids with zipper scars just like hers. Life-changing.

louisiana-lions-camp.author: Parent of a camper with special needs
louisiana-lions-camp.text: Completely free, completely loving. The staff ratio is incredible and my son came home with a trophy he shows everyone. Louisiana at its best.

kanakuk-kamps.author: Longtime camp family
kanakuk-kamps.text: Our kids count the days to K-1 every year — elite sports coaching, wild spirit, and counselors who disciple them all year by text. The community is like nothing else.

kanakuk-kamps.author: Parent who chose to leave
kanakuk-kamps.text: The program itself is superb, but the organization's handling of past abuse cases gave us pause, and we ultimately moved camps. Do your homework and ask hard questions.

kanakuk-kamps.author: Alum
kanakuk-kamps.text: Thirteen summers there shaped my faith and my jump shot. It's intense, polished, and very Southern-evangelical — kids either love the energy or find it a lot.

sherwood-forest-camp.author: St. Louis parent
sherwood-forest-camp.text: They invest in the same kids year after year, through high school leadership programs. My daughter learned to swim, then to lead. The Black River is beautiful.

ymca-camp-lakewood.author: St. Louis parent
ymca-camp-lakewood.text: Gorgeous private lake, solid values programming, generous financial aid. Cabins are rustic and the food is standard camp fare, but our kids never notice.

ymca-camp-lakewood.author: Alum
ymca-camp-lakewood.text: Learned to sail, ride, and lead at Lakewood over ten summers. Big camp energy with genuinely kind traditions like the Friday campfires.

camp-taum-sauk.author: Parent of an outdoorsy kid
camp-taum-sauk.text: Actual adventure — caves, bluffs, shut-ins, overnight floats. My son came home filthy and transformed. Don't expect air conditioning or fancy food.

camp-sabra.author: St. Louis Jewish parent
camp-sabra.text: Sabra's peninsula setting is unreal — water on three sides. Our kids live for Maccabiah and Friday night Shabbat on the point. Strong inclusion staff too.

camp-sabra.author: Alum
camp-sabra.text: The trans-lake swim is still one of my proudest moments. Jewish identity there felt joyful, never forced. Worth every penny, though it is a lot of pennies.

camp-wyman.author: St. Louis parent
camp-wyman.text: A 125-year-old camp that still puts underserved kids first — scholarships made it possible for us. The teen leadership pipeline is the real gem.

windermere-baptist-camp.author: Youth pastor
windermere-baptist-camp.text: The lakefront is spectacular and the staff host hundreds of kids smoothly. It's a conference-center feel — dorms and meeting halls — more than a woodsy camp.

camp-moval.author: UCC parent
camp-moval.text: Radically welcoming — my nonbinary kid felt safe and celebrated. Small, sweet, screen-free week with counselors who listen. Facilities are humble.

wonderland-camp.author: Parent of an adult camper who started as a kid
wonderland-camp.text: Wonderland has grown with our daughter for fifteen years. Accessible pontoon rides, patient staff, real joy. The waitlist for summer weeks is long — apply in winter.

camp-mihaska.author: St. Louis grandparent
camp-mihaska.text: The Salvation Army made camp possible for our family, period. Clean grounds, caring staff, kids come home singing. Bless them.

camp-fiddlecreek.author: Scout parent
camp-fiddlecreek.text: Well-run council camp with a great climbing tower. My daughter's unit leaders were fantastic with homesickness. Rustic units, standard camp chow.

camp-david-of-the-ozarks.author: Guardian of two campers
camp-david-of-the-ozarks.text: They love on kids the system forgets — letters all year, not just a week in July. My nephews came home gentler and prouder. Small camp doing big work.

ymca-camp-belknap.author: Parent of two Belknap boys
ymca-camp-belknap.text: The older-camper leadership model is real — my sons came home more confident and kinder. Getting a spot is the hard part; the waitlist is no joke.

ymca-camp-belknap.author: Alum from the 2000s
ymca-camp-belknap.text: Rustic in the best way. No screens, cold lake swims, songs at dinner. Twenty years later my closest friends are still Belknap guys.

ymca-camp-belknap.author: First-time camp parent
ymca-camp-belknap.text: Wonderful traditions and staff, though the cabins are genuinely bare-bones. If your kid needs creature comforts this isn't the place.

ymca-camp-foss.author: Parent of a shy first-timer
ymca-camp-foss.text: They eased my nervous daughter in beautifully. She came home singing camp songs and asking for two weeks next year.

ymca-camp-foss.author: Alum turned counselor
ymca-camp-foss.text: Classic no-frills New Hampshire camp. The magic is the people; just pack extra socks because everything stays a little damp.

ymca-camp-frank-a-day.author: Newton parent
ymca-camp-frank-a-day.text: A lot of Boston-area families grow up with this place. Communication from the directors was excellent all session.

ymca-camp-frank-a-day.author: Former counselor
ymca-camp-frank-a-day.text: Tight-knit and traditional. Facilities are showing their age in spots, but the lake program and camper spirit carry it.

ymca-camp-burgess.author: Parent of a 9-year-old
ymca-camp-burgess.text: The farm animals and pond swimming won my son over immediately. Brother-sister setup with Hayward worked great for our family.

ymca-camp-burgess.author: Camper, age 12
ymca-camp-burgess.text: Kayaking and the giant swing are the best. Cabins get hot in July and the food repeats a lot, but I'm going back.

ymca-camp-hayward.author: Cape Cod parent
ymca-camp-hayward.text: My daughter loved the horses and the garden. Counselors were nurturing with homesickness — she called it her happy place.

ymca-camp-hayward.author: Former counselor
ymca-camp-hayward.text: Sweet community and beautiful ponds. Like most Y camps it runs lean, so don't expect resort polish.

ymca-camp-becket.author: Parent of a three-summer camper
ymca-camp-becket.text: Becket shapes character — my son talks about honesty and 'the Becket way' unprompted. The month flies by for them, slower for us.

ymca-camp-becket.author: Alum from the 90s
ymca-camp-becket.text: Grey Torch night still gives me chills. It costs more than typical Y camps, but financial aid is generous.

ymca-camp-sloane.author: Parent from Westchester
ymca-camp-sloane.text: Tent living sounded intense but my kids loved it. Really diverse community — campers from all over the world at dinner.

ymca-camp-sloane.author: Former camper
ymca-camp-sloane.text: The tents and lake make it feel like real camp. Some program periods felt understaffed on busy weeks, but counselors cared.

ymca-camp-hazen.author: Parent of an anxious camper
ymca-camp-hazen.text: The choice-based schedule was perfect for my kid who hates being herded. Staff sent a reassuring update when she struggled early on.

ymca-camp-hazen.author: Camper, age 13
ymca-camp-hazen.text: You actually pick what you do all day. The lake trampoline and climbing tower are elite.

ymca-camp-ockanickon.author: South Jersey parent
ymca-camp-ockanickon.text: The cedar lake turns everyone's swimsuit brown and the boys love it. Great value for overnight camp this close to Philly.

ymca-camp-ockanickon.author: Camper, age 12
ymca-camp-ockanickon.text: Boating and the mud pit are awesome. Cabins are open-air so bring bug spray, seriously.

ymca-camp-matollionequay.author: Parent of a returning camper
ymca-camp-matollionequay.text: Matolly girls come back year after year — the counselors were campers themselves and it shows.

ymca-camp-matollionequay.author: Former counselor
ymca-camp-matollionequay.text: Sweet, traditional program with real sisterhood. Facilities are rustic Pine Barrens standard: sandy, buggy, beloved.

ymca-camp-mason-nj.author: NJ parent
ymca-camp-mason-nj.text: The skate park sold my son; the campfires kept him. Very welcoming to kids who've never camped before.

ymca-camp-mason-nj.author: Camper, age 14
ymca-camp-mason-nj.text: Horseback and the Water Gap hikes are great. The dining hall gets chaotic when it's full.

camp-speers-ymca.author: Parent of a camper on the spectrum
camp-speers-ymca.text: One of the few camps where my son is simply included, not accommodated as an afterthought. Staff training is evident.

camp-speers-ymca.author: Camper, age 12
camp-speers-ymca.text: The lake is huge and boating is every day if you want. Long walk from the far cabins to everything though.

ymca-camp-shand.author: Lancaster parent
ymca-camp-shand.text: They made camp possible for us with a scholarship, and never made it feel like charity. My kids felt like they belonged from hour one.

ymca-camp-shand.author: Former counselor
ymca-camp-shand.text: Mission-first camp with big hearts. Equipment is older and the pool schedule gets tight, but the kids barely notice.

ymca-camp-conrad-weiser.author: Berks County parent
ymca-camp-conrad-weiser.text: Counselors from a dozen countries — my daughter still writes to hers in Spain. Solid value and lots of choices.

ymca-camp-conrad-weiser.author: Camper, age 12
ymca-camp-conrad-weiser.text: The pool and hilltop campfires are great. It's a hike between activity areas, so your legs get strong fast.

ymca-camp-letts.author: DC-area parent
ymca-camp-letts.text: Sailing on the Chesapeake forty minutes from the Beltway. My kids came home salty, sunburned, and beaming.

ymca-camp-letts.author: Camper, age 13
ymca-camp-letts.text: Wakeboarding is the best thing ever. Jellyfish show up in late July, so time your session.

ymca-camp-tockwogh.author: Wilmington parent
ymca-camp-tockwogh.text: The sailing fleet rivals private sailing camps at half the cost. Counselor spirit at drop-off was off the charts.

ymca-camp-tockwogh.author: Alum and former counselor
ymca-camp-tockwogh.text: Tockwogh waterfront staff are the real deal. Bay breezes, bonfires, lifelong friends — the mosquitoes are the only villains.

ymca-camp-horseshoe-wv.author: Morgantown parent
ymca-camp-horseshoe-wv.text: River days and real mountain air. The staff treat leadership development seriously, not as a buzzword.

ymca-camp-horseshoe-wv.author: Alum from the 90s
ymca-camp-horseshoe-wv.text: The Cheat River valley is God's country. Rustic cabins, zero pretense, lifelong friends.

camp-sea-gull-nc.author: Charlotte parent
camp-sea-gull-nc.text: The seamanship program is unmatched — my son can pilot a motorboat and read the wind. Sign-ups fill a year out.

camp-sea-gull-nc.author: Alum, 10 summers
camp-sea-gull-nc.text: Sea Gull ranks taught me discipline before I knew the word. Half of eastern NC's sailors started here.

camp-sea-gull-nc.author: First-year parent
camp-sea-gull-nc.text: Impressive operation, though it's big — quieter kids can get lost in the shuffle the first week.

camp-seafarer-nc.author: Mother and Seafarer alum
camp-seafarer-nc.text: Watching my daughter earn her Skipper rank on the same river I did was everything. The traditions endure.

camp-seafarer-nc.author: Camper, age 14
camp-seafarer-nc.text: Driving a motorboat by yourself is the coolest feeling. Salty hair all summer.

ymca-storer-camps.author: Toledo parent
ymca-storer-camps.text: The horsemanship program is the best value in the Midwest. Storer's traditions run deep — my dad sang me the same grace songs.

ymca-storer-camps.author: Camper, age 13
ymca-storer-camps.text: Riding every day and the tree houses are amazing. Lake has weeds near the shore, swim out past them.

ymca-camp-nissokone.author: Metro Detroit parent
ymca-camp-nissokone.text: Real up-north camp — Au Sable canoe trips and beach days on Huron. Worth the three-hour drive.

ymca-camp-nissokone.author: Camper, age 12
ymca-camp-nissokone.text: The sandbar and tubing are awesome. Cabins are old and the walk to the showers is far.

ymca-camp-manitou-lin.author: Grand Rapids parent
ymca-camp-manitou-lin.text: My nervous 7-year-old did a mini session and demanded two weeks the next summer. Gentle, patient staff.

ymca-camp-manitou-lin.author: Camper, age 12
ymca-camp-manitou-lin.text: The horses and lake are great. Wish sessions were longer honestly.

ymca-camp-al-gon-quian.author: Ann Arbor parent
ymca-camp-al-gon-quian.text: Small enough that the director greeted my daughter by name in week one. Burt Lake evenings are pure Michigan magic.

ymca-camp-al-gon-quian.author: Alum from the 2010s
ymca-camp-al-gon-quian.text: Sailing at sunset and cabin overnights on the beach — the place is a time capsule in the best way.

ymca-camp-tecumseh-in.author: Indianapolis parent
ymca-camp-tecumseh-in.text: Tecumseh is a machine in the best sense — organized, joyful, values-forward. Chapel is gentle but definitely present.

ymca-camp-tecumseh-in.author: Camper, age 12
ymca-camp-tecumseh-in.text: River rides, the alpine tower, and the best counselors alive. I've gone four years straight.

ymca-camp-tecumseh-in.author: First-time parent
ymca-camp-tecumseh-in.text: Fantastic program, though secular families should know devotions and chapel are part of the rhythm.

ymca-camp-matawa.author: Fond du Lac parent
ymca-camp-matawa.text: Small and nurturing — the right first camp. Staff sent us a happy photo by day two and homesickness never landed.

ymca-camp-matawa.author: Camper, age 9
ymca-camp-matawa.text: I liked the frog pond and s'mores. The hills make your legs tired.

ymca-camp-u-nah-li-ya.author: Green Bay parent
ymca-camp-u-nah-li-ya.text: Exactly what camp should be — muddy shoes, campfire smell, new best friends. Great value.

ymca-camp-u-nah-li-ya.author: Camper, age 10
ymca-camp-u-nah-li-ya.text: Fishing and the water mat are the best. The loons are cool but LOUD at night.

ymca-camp-edwards.author: Chicago parent
ymca-camp-edwards.text: They welcomed my city kid who'd never seen a lake and gave him the full camp experience without breaking us.

ymca-camp-edwards.author: Former camper
ymca-camp-edwards.text: Small, sweet, old-school. The waterfront is modest but the counselors made everything an event.

ymca-camp-maclean.author: Chicagoland parent
ymca-camp-maclean.text: MacLean was my camp and now my kids'. Same lake smell, same songs, same happy exhaustion at pickup.

ymca-camp-maclean.author: Camper, age 12
ymca-camp-maclean.text: Sailing and horses in one week is great. The cabins are cozy which is a nice word for small.

ymca-camp-olson.author: Brainerd-area parent
ymca-camp-olson.text: Genuine Northwoods camp that works hard so any kid can attend. The sailing program surprised us with its depth.

ymca-camp-olson.author: Camper, age 13
ymca-camp-olson.text: The lake is beautiful and the counselors are hilarious. Mosquito hour at dusk is a real thing, bring spray.

ymca-camp-cormorant.author: Fargo parent
ymca-camp-cormorant.text: Every kid in Fargo seems to pass through Cormorant. Waterskiing instruction was patient and my daughter got up on skis day three.

ymca-camp-cormorant.author: Camper, age 11
ymca-camp-cormorant.text: Tubing and the candy store are the best. The wind off the lake can cancel boating some days.

des-moines-y-camp-ia.author: Des Moines parent
des-moines-y-camp-ia.text: Y Camp is an institution — my dad, me, and now my kids. The river valley setting surprises people who think Iowa is flat.

des-moines-y-camp-ia.author: Camper, age 12
des-moines-y-camp-ia.text: Paintball and horses in the same week! The hike back up from the river is a beast.

camp-ramah-in-the-rockies.author: Parent of an outdoorsy kid
camp-ramah-in-the-rockies.text: Perfect for a kid who'd rather summit a peak than play color war. Rustic means rustic though - platform tents and real weather.

camp-ramah-in-the-rockies.author: Camper, age 14
camp-ramah-in-the-rockies.text: The masa backpacking trips are the highlight. Davening on a mountain hits different.

camp-ramah-galim-ca.author: Bay Area parent
camp-ramah-galim-ca.text: Surfing and Shabbat in one camp - our kids love it. Still a young camp, so some programs feel like they're finding their footing.

ramah-day-camp-nyack.author: Manhattan parent
ramah-day-camp-nyack.text: Wonderful Jewish energy and great swim instruction. The bus commute makes for a long day for younger kids.

ramah-day-camp-philadelphia.author: Parent of a kindergartner
ramah-day-camp-philadelphia.text: Gentle first camp experience with real Jewish content. Counselors clearly love the place - many grew up there.

ramah-day-camp-chicago.author: Northbrook parent
ramah-day-camp-chicago.text: Sweet, spirited camp that got our kids excited for overnight Ramah. Facilities are shared and can feel a bit tight midsummer.

ramah-sports-academy.author: Parent of a soccer kid
ramah-sports-academy.text: Finally a serious sports camp that doesn't make us choose between athletics and Shabbat. Coaching quality genuinely impressed us.

urj-goldman-union-camp-guci.author: GUCI alum
urj-goldman-union-camp-guci.text: There's a reason so many of us went into Jewish professional life. Not fancy, deeply beloved.

urj-osrui.author: Chicago parent
urj-osrui.text: The unit structure means the camp grows with your kid. Chalutzim's Hebrew immersion actually works.

urj-6-points-sports-academy.author: Parent of a basketball camper
urj-6-points-sports-academy.text: Real skill development, not babysitting with balls. Our son improved visibly and loved the Jewish community around it.

urj-6-points-sci-tech.author: Parent of a robotics kid
urj-6-points-sci-tech.text: First camp where our son felt fully himself. Less outdoor time than traditional camp, but he didn't miss it.

urj-6-points-creative-arts.author: Parent of a theater kid
urj-6-points-creative-arts.text: Serious arts instruction plus a community where creative kids thrive. Campus is a school, not a woodsy camp - know that going in.

camp-young-judaea-sprout-lake.author: Parent of an 8-year-old
camp-young-judaea-sprout-lake.text: Perfect size for a first overnight camp, and the Israeli counselors made a real impression on our kids.

camp-young-judaea-new-hampshire.author: Boston-area parent
camp-young-judaea-new-hampshire.text: Sweet, old-school camp with real Jewish substance. Cabins show their age, and we mean that mostly fondly.

camp-galil-pa.author: Philly alum
camp-galil-pa.text: Galil taught me that kids can run things. Avodah in the garden, arguments about justice, best friends for life.

camp-galil-pa.author: Parent of a camper
camp-galil-pa.text: Wonderfully unpolished and values-driven. If you want manicured facilities, look elsewhere.

camp-moshava-habonim-md.author: DC-area alum
camp-moshava-habonim-md.text: Mosh people are Mosh people for life. Muddy, loud, democratic, and completely formative.

camp-tavor-mi.author: Parent of two campers
camp-tavor-mi.text: Tavor made our kids more thoughtful people. It's small and rustic, which is exactly the point.

camp-gilboa-ca.author: LA parent
camp-gilboa-ca.text: Our kids come home talking about fairness and community, not just s'mores. Mountain air is a bonus.

camp-shomria-ny.author: Movement alum
camp-shomria-ny.text: Ken-style scouting, real ideological education, and zero pretension. Bring bug spray and an open mind.

bnai-brith-camp-oregon.author: Portland parent
bnai-brith-camp-oregon.text: BB Camp welcomes absolutely everyone and means it. The coastal fog burns off and the lake days are perfect.

beber-camp.author: Chicago parent
beber-camp.text: Great facilities, kids pick their own activities, and Shabbat still feels special. Worth the price tag, barely.

camp-chi-wi.author: Chicago parent
camp-chi-wi.text: Something for every kid - ours did climbing and pottery all summer. Popular sessions waitlist early.

emma-kaufmann-camp.author: Pittsburgh parent
emma-kaufmann-camp.text: EKC is Pittsburgh Jewish life in the summer. The lake is gorgeous and the staff feel like family.

camp-louise.author: Louise alum
camp-louise.text: The friendships from Louise carried me through life. The traditions - some a hundred years old - still give me goosebumps.

camp-airy.author: Baltimore parent
camp-airy.text: An all-boys camp that lets every kind of boy fit in - jocks, gamers, theater kids. That's rarer than it should be.

camp-zeke.author: Parent of a non-sporty kid
camp-zeke.text: The cooking classes and no-cut culture changed our son's relationship with movement and food. Genuinely different.

camp-ben-frankel.author: Southern Illinois parent
camp-ben-frankel.text: Tiny camp, huge heart, and a price that doesn't require a second mortgage. Our kids adore it.

camp-daisy-harry-stein.author: Phoenix parent
camp-daisy-harry-stein.text: Escaping Phoenix heat for Jewish camp in the pines is the best trade in Arizona. Warm, well-run program.

camp-nah-jee-wah-pa.author: NJ parent of a 7-year-old
camp-nah-jee-wah-pa.text: Great on-ramp to sleepaway camp with lots of hand-holding. It's a big operation, so ask questions early.

cedar-lake-camp-njy.author: Camper, age 14
cedar-lake-camp-njy.text: So many kids to meet and the lake is huge. Food is fine, lines are long, friendships are forever.

camp-stone.author: Cleveland parent
camp-stone.text: The chalutziut theme makes Torah feel hands-on - my kids churned butter and learned Gemara in the same day.

camp-moshava-alevy.author: LA parent
camp-moshava-alevy.text: Our kids get real Torah content and real mountain air. Younger camp still building out its facilities.

camp-mesorah.author: Five Towns parent
camp-mesorah.text: Serious sports, serious Torah, zero contradiction. The facilities keep getting better every summer.

camp-lavi.author: Queens parent
camp-lavi.text: Lavi nails the balance - real davening and learning, but the kids talk about the leagues and trips all year.

camp-nageela-east.author: Long Island parent
camp-nageela-east.text: Our public-school kid came home proud to be Jewish and asking for Shabbat dinner. Zero pressure, all warmth.

sephardic-adventure-camp.author: Seattle Sephardic parent
sephardic-adventure-camp.text: Where else do kids sing Ladino romances at campfire? A treasure of our community, small and mighty.

camp-gan-israel-parksville.author: Crown Heights parent
camp-gan-israel-parksville.text: The ruach of a CGI summer stays with a boy all year. Rustic bunks, unmatched chayus.

camp-gan-israel-running-springs.author: LA parent
camp-gan-israel-running-springs.text: Counselors with endless energy and real Jewish pride. Facilities are simple; nobody seems to mind.

bima-at-brandeis.author: Parent of a young writer
bima-at-brandeis.text: College-level arts mentorship inside a warm Jewish community. More studio than campfire - know your kid.

z-frank-apachi-day-camp.author: Northbrook parent
z-frank-apachi-day-camp.text: Well-oiled machine with caring counselors. Fridays are adorable. Drop-off traffic, less so.

camp-settoga-ny.author: Upper West Side parent
camp-settoga-ny.text: Feels like sleepaway camp that comes home for dinner. Gorgeous facilities; the bus ride is the trade-off.

camp-yomi-92ny.author: Manhattan parent
camp-yomi-92ny.text: Polished program and superb swim instruction. You pay Manhattan prices for it.

new-country-day-camp.author: East Village parent
new-country-day-camp.text: Nature immersion for city kids with values we share. The ferry-and-bus logistics take commitment.

camp-grossman.author: Newton parent
camp-grossman.text: Organized, warm, and genuinely Jewish without overdoing it. Extended-day options save working parents.

camp-teko.author: Minneapolis parent
camp-teko.text: A day camp on Minnetonka with Shabbat ruach - the best of both worlds for our family.

camp-butwin-mn.author: St. Paul parent
camp-butwin-mn.text: Feels like overnight camp compressed into a day. Simple facilities, big heart.

beth-tfiloh-camps.author: Baltimore parent
beth-tfiloh-camps.text: The lake and boating make it feel like sleepaway camp. Strong swim program and genuinely kosher kitchen.

trails-end-camp.author: Parent of two returning campers
trails-end-camp.text: The food program is genuinely unreal for a summer camp, and the facilities keep getting upgraded. My kids count down the days all winter.

trails-end-camp.author: Former camper, now in college
trails-end-camp.text: TEC friendships are the ones that stick. Color War is life-changing. It skews modern and comfortable, which some call cushy — I call it great.

trails-end-camp.author: First-time camp parent
trails-end-camp.text: Wonderful summer, super communicative staff. Only gripes are the price tag and how fast enrollment fills for popular age groups.

camp-towanda.author: Third-generation Towanda parent
camp-towanda.text: The traditions and spirit here are the real thing — Olympics, the alma mater, all of it. Same family ownership for decades and it shows in every detail.

camp-towanda.author: Alum and former counselor
camp-towanda.text: Unplugged, old-school, and deeply intentional about character. Kids come home more confident and genuinely kinder. There is a reason families wait years to get in.

camp-towanda.author: Parent of a first-year camper
camp-towanda.text: Our daughter had a magical summer. Be warned: the full 7 weeks is a big commitment (and a big check), and the no-phones policy is strict — which we ended up loving.

camp-ihc.author: Parent of returning campers
camp-ihc.text: IHC nails the balance of spirit and warmth. Leagues, Carnival, the lake — my kids talk about their counselors like family. Communication from the office is excellent.

camp-ihc.author: Ten-year alum
camp-ihc.text: Equinunk summers shaped who I am. The community is tight-knit across generations and the campus is gorgeous. Getting a spot has gotten genuinely hard.

camp-ihc.author: Parent of a camper with food allergies
camp-ihc.text: They handled our son's allergies carefully and he thrived. Only downsides: cost keeps climbing and the waitlist for younger divisions is no joke.

lake-bryn-mawr-camp.author: Mother of two Mawr girls
lake-bryn-mawr-camp.text: An all-girls bubble in the best way — sisterhood, tradition, and real confidence-building. The campus on the hill is stunning and the horseback program is a standout.

lake-bryn-mawr-camp.author: Alum from the 2010s
lake-bryn-mawr-camp.text: Angel/Amazon spirit runs deep. I still tear up at the songs. It is polished and premium, and yes, plenty of families come from the same few zip codes.

lake-bryn-mawr-camp.author: First-time parent
lake-bryn-mawr-camp.text: Our daughter grew so much in one summer. It is expensive and the full-season model was intimidating for a 9-year-old, but she never wanted to come home.

camp-wayne.author: Parent of a boys-side camper
camp-wayne.text: Warm, down-to-earth, and less flashy than some Poconos neighbors — which is exactly why we chose it. Brother-sister setup was perfect for our kids.

camp-wayne.author: Former counselor
camp-wayne.text: Great directors and a genuinely kind camper culture. Facilities are solid if not luxurious, and the separate boys/girls campuses each have their own personality.

camp-wayne.author: Alum
camp-wayne.text: Wayne summers were pure classic camp — lake swims, campfires, lifelong friends. The food was hit or miss but nobody cared.

pine-forest-camp.author: Second-generation Pine Forest parent
pine-forest-camp.text: One of the oldest Jewish camps in the Poconos and it still feels like family — same owners' family for generations. Traditions over glitz, and it works.

pine-forest-camp.author: Parent of a returning camper
pine-forest-camp.text: Our son loves it and the staff clearly care. Bunks are on the rustic side compared to the newer luxury camps nearby, so set expectations accordingly.

pine-forest-camp.author: Alum
pine-forest-camp.text: The Pine Forest spirit is real — song contests, the lake, friendships across decades. Reunions feel like coming home.

camp-canadensis.author: Parent of two campers
camp-canadensis.text: Incredible facilities — the pool complex, courts, and adventure park are top notch — but what won us over is how well the staff know each kid.

camp-canadensis.author: Camper, age 14
camp-canadensis.text: The electives are the best part. You actually get to pick what you do and the counselors hype everything up. Color War week is insane.

camp-canadensis.author: First-time camp parent
camp-canadensis.text: Great communication and a smooth first summer. It is pricey and the camp is big, so quieter kids may need a little help finding their footing early.

camp-nock-a-mixon.author: Bucks County parent
camp-nock-a-mixon.text: A hidden gem closer to Philly than the Poconos crowd. Beautiful grounds, strong sports, and the directors are hands-on every single day.

camp-nock-a-mixon.author: Alum and former counselor
camp-nock-a-mixon.text: Classic, well-run brother-sister camp with real warmth. Not the fanciest bunks in Pennsylvania, but the camper experience is what matters and they deliver.

camp-nock-a-mixon.author: Parent of a returning camper
camp-nock-a-mixon.text: Our kids beg to go back every year. The four-week structure fit our family better than the seven-week camps.

camp-lohikan.author: Parent of an animal-loving camper
camp-lohikan.text: The sheer range of activities is unmatched — our daughter did horseback, wakeboarding, and the animal farm in one session. Flexible session lengths were a huge plus.

camp-lohikan.author: Former international counselor
camp-lohikan.text: Fun, energetic place with campers from everywhere. It is big, so organization can feel chaotic at changeover weekends, but kids have a blast.

camp-lohikan.author: Parent
camp-lohikan.text: Good value for shorter sessions and my son enjoyed it, but with so many campers it felt less personal than we hoped. Bunk upkeep varies a lot.

camp-weequahic.author: Parent of two campers
camp-weequahic.text: The gratitude-and-attitude culture is not just a slogan — our kids came home noticeably more thoughtful. Director communication is the best we have experienced.

camp-weequahic.author: Camper, age 13
camp-weequahic.text: GAC is my favorite place on earth. Cookie the golden retriever, campfires, the lake — and counselors who actually listen.

camp-weequahic.author: First-time parent
camp-weequahic.text: Fantastic first sleepaway experience with a real focus on kindness. Just know the popular sessions and bunks fill very early.

camp-starlight.author: Parent of returning campers
camp-starlight.text: Gorgeous lakefront campus and a staff culture that keeps counselors coming back for years. Our kids' confidence has grown every summer.

camp-starlight.author: Alum
camp-starlight.text: Starlight is pure classic Poconos camp — spirit, sing, lifelong friends. The brother-sister setup meant my sister and I both got our own experience.

camp-starlight.author: Parent
camp-starlight.text: Wonderful summer overall. The seven-week commitment and tuition are serious, and homesick calls home are limited — great for growth, hard for parents.

camp-ramah-poconos.author: Parent of day-school kids
camp-ramah-poconos.text: Ramah gave our kids a joyful, immersive Jewish summer — Shabbat at camp is magical. The community carries into the school year too.

camp-ramah-poconos.author: Alum and former madricha
camp-ramah-poconos.text: The friendships and Hebrew-infused camp culture shaped my whole Jewish identity. Facilities are improving year over year.

camp-ramah-poconos.author: Parent
camp-ramah-poconos.text: Strong values and caring staff. Bunks and some buildings show their age, and the level of religious observance is a real commitment — know what you are signing up for.

camp-morasha.author: Modern Orthodox parent
camp-morasha.text: Morasha strikes the balance we wanted — serious learning and davening plus a genuinely fun, sports-heavy camp. Our kids' friend group is now their year-round community.

camp-morasha.author: Alum
camp-morasha.text: The lake, the leagues, the ruach at Shabbat — Morasha summers were the highlight of growing up. Spots go fast through word of mouth.

camp-morasha.author: Parent of three campers
camp-morasha.text: Excellent staff and warm community. Food is decent kosher camp fare, and enrollment basically requires knowing the timeline cold.

camp-harlam.author: Reform congregation parent
camp-harlam.text: Harlam made Judaism joyful for our kids — song sessions, Shabbat walks, inclusive community. Counselors grew up there and it shows.

camp-harlam.author: Alum from the 2000s
camp-harlam.text: Twenty years later my closest friends are still Harlam friends. The place has a warmth that is hard to explain until you have stood at Shabbat services in the grove.

camp-harlam.author: Parent of a first-time camper
camp-harlam.text: Caring staff and great inclusion support. Facilities are comfortable but not fancy, and popular sessions waitlist early.

woodward-pa.author: Parent of a skateboarder
woodward-pa.text: There is nowhere else like it — world-class ramps, foam pits, Olympic-level coaches. My son progressed more in one week than in a year at home.

woodward-pa.author: Camper, age 15
woodward-pa.text: You ride literally all day. Pros show up randomly. The indoor parks mean rain does not matter. Best week of my year, every year.

woodward-pa.author: Parent
woodward-pa.text: Incredible facilities and instruction, but it is expensive per week and the vibe is more action-sports training ground than traditional camp — less hand-holding.

independent-lake-camp.author: Parent of a creative kid
independent-lake-camp.text: ILC let our son be 100% himself — he built his own schedule around circus, magic, and skate park. Truly the camp for kids who do not fit the sports-camp mold.

independent-lake-camp.author: Alum
independent-lake-camp.text: The freedom to choose your own day is what makes ILC special. Weird in the best possible way, and wildly accepting.

independent-lake-camp.author: Parent
independent-lake-camp.text: Great fit for our independent kid, though the loose structure was an adjustment. Bunks are basic and the camp store bill added up.

summit-camp.author: Parent of a camper with ADHD
summit-camp.text: For the first time our son came home from camp with friends. The staff ratio and social coaching are extraordinary — they truly get kids with ADHD and social learning differences.

summit-camp.author: Parent of an autistic teen
summit-camp.text: Summit is one of the only places our daughter feels fully accepted. Structured, warm, and staffed by people who understand neurodivergent kids.

summit-camp.author: Parent
summit-camp.text: Life-changing for our child, and worth it — but the tuition is steep even by private camp standards, and sessions fill many months ahead.

camp-lee-mar.author: Parent of a camper with special needs
camp-lee-mar.text: Lee Mar has been doing this longer than almost anyone and it shows. Speech therapy, life skills, and real camp fun in one place. Our daughter counts down the days.

camp-lee-mar.author: Longtime returning family
camp-lee-mar.text: The consistency of staff year to year is remarkable. They celebrate every camper's growth like it is their own kid.

camp-lee-mar.author: Parent
camp-lee-mar.text: A wonderful, safe summer for our son. Cost is significant with the therapeutic components, but few programs offer this combination.

timber-lake-camp.author: Parent of two campers
timber-lake-camp.text: Beautiful Catskills setting, elite facilities, and a leadership team that runs a tight ship. Our kids' counselors knew them inside out by week two.

timber-lake-camp.author: Alum
timber-lake-camp.text: Olympics at TLC is still the most fun I have ever had. Strong sports culture but room for arts kids too.

timber-lake-camp.author: Parent
timber-lake-camp.text: Terrific summer, very polished operation. It is one of the pricier Catskills options and the camp spirit can feel intense for a low-key kid at first.

camp-schodack.author: Parent of returning campers
camp-schodack.text: Family-owned warmth you can feel from the first phone call. Mid-size means every kid is known by name — including by the directors.

camp-schodack.author: Former counselor from the UK
camp-schodack.text: The kindest camp community I have worked in. Schodack takes its no-bullying culture seriously and the returning-staff rate proves it.

camp-schodack.author: Parent
camp-schodack.text: Our son thrived. Facilities are lovely if not over-the-top luxurious, and the popular sessions fill up fast with sibling legacies.

brant-lake-camp.author: Father of two BLC boys
brant-lake-camp.text: Over a century of tradition on a stunning Adirondack lake. Sports instruction is superb but the real product is the brotherhood — my sons idolize their counselors.

brant-lake-camp.author: Alum, third generation
brant-lake-camp.text: BLC green and gray runs in the family. The place barely changes and that is precisely the point.

brant-lake-camp.author: Parent
brant-lake-camp.text: Exceptional camp, old-school in the best way. Just know it is a big financial and full-summer commitment, and legacy families dominate enrollment.

point-o-pines.author: Mother of a returning camper
point-o-pines.text: The most nurturing all-girls environment we found anywhere. The Himoff family knows every camper, the waterfront is spectacular, and the food is famously good.

point-o-pines.author: Alum
point-o-pines.text: P-O-P girls are P-O-P girls for life. Full-summer, screen-free, deeply traditional — it built a confidence in me nothing else has.

point-o-pines.author: Parent
point-o-pines.text: Truly special place. The seven-week single session and top-of-market tuition make it a huge commitment, and spots are essentially inherited.

raquette-lake-camps.author: Parent of a boys-camp camper
raquette-lake-camps.text: Deep-woods Adirondack setting that most camps only pretend to have. Wilderness trips are the crown jewel — our son summited peaks he still talks about.

raquette-lake-camps.author: Alum of the girls camp
raquette-lake-camps.text: A century of tradition on the most beautiful lake in New York. Sister-brother camps across the water make it feel like a little world of its own.

raquette-lake-camps.author: Parent
raquette-lake-camps.text: Gorgeous and well-run. It is remote — visiting day is a trek — and the classic bunks are rustic by design.

camp-echo-lake.author: Parent of two campers
camp-echo-lake.text: Echo Lake walks the walk on emotional intelligence — the staff training is famous in the industry and you see it in how counselors handle every situation.

camp-echo-lake.author: Alum and former staff member
camp-echo-lake.text: The 'Echo Lake bubble' is real: inclusive, silly, deeply kind. Main Village to Senior Village, the progression is thoughtfully designed.

camp-echo-lake.author: Parent
camp-echo-lake.text: Wonderful people and program. Tuition is at the top of the Adirondack market and the camp's popularity means enrolling early is essential.

french-woods.author: Parent of a theater kid
french-woods.text: Full-scale musicals cast, rehearsed, and performed in three weeks — the training is basically pre-professional. Our daughter found her people here.

french-woods.author: Alum, worked in theater since
french-woods.text: French Woods taught me more about performing than college did. The self-scheduled day means motivated kids fly; circus, rock bands, magic — it is all real.

french-woods.author: Parent
french-woods.text: Amazing for driven performers, but it is big and intense — rehearsals can eat the whole session, food is mediocre, and a shy kid can drift without self-advocacy.

long-lake-camp-arts.author: Parent of a young filmmaker
long-lake-camp-arts.text: Total creative freedom — my son wrote and shot a short film in two weeks. No auditions, no cuts, every kid performs. The Adirondack setting is a bonus.

long-lake-camp-arts.author: Camper, age 14
long-lake-camp-arts.text: You pick everything you do, which I loved. Some studios are better equipped than others and the bunks are pretty old-school.

long-lake-camp-arts.author: Parent
long-lake-camp-arts.text: Warm, quirky arts community. Organization can feel loose compared to more polished camps, but our daughter came home glowing.

camp-ramah-berkshires.author: Conservative-movement parent
camp-ramah-berkshires.text: Shabbat at Ramah Berkshires is unlike anything else — hundreds of kids singing at the lake. Our children's Jewish identity was built here.

camp-ramah-berkshires.author: Alum and gap-year staffer
camp-ramah-berkshires.text: The edah system creates ridiculously tight friend groups. Hebrew everywhere, joy everywhere. Went back to staff because I could not stay away.

camp-ramah-berkshires.author: Parent
camp-ramah-berkshires.text: Meaningful community, but facilities lag the price point and the daily religious schedule is intense for kids from less observant homes.

camp-dudley.author: Father of a third-year camper
camp-dudley.text: The oldest camp in the country and the motto — The Other Fellow First — is lived daily. Leaders are former campers who model real character. Worth every penny.

camp-dudley.author: Alum from the 1990s
camp-dudley.text: Dudley is a brotherhood measured in generations. Chapel talks, hiking trips, Sunday shows — it quietly builds men of substance.

camp-dudley.author: Parent
camp-dudley.text: Exceptional values and staff. The Christian-rooted chapel tradition is gentle but present, and cabin life is proudly rustic — no AC, no screens.

camp-takajo.author: Father of two Takajo boys
camp-takajo.text: Takajo is the gold standard for boys' sports camps — pristine fields on Long Lake, superb coaching, and a warrior spirit tempered by real sportsmanship.

camp-takajo.author: Alum
camp-takajo.text: Seven weeks a summer on Long Lake made me who I am. The traditions and the friendships are permanent.

camp-takajo.author: Parent
camp-takajo.text: Superbly run and our son loves it. It is among the most expensive camps in Maine and quite structured — free spirits may prefer an elective-heavy camp.

camp-vega.author: Mother of a Vega girl
camp-vega.text: The facilities on Echo Lake are jaw-dropping and the programming is first-class, but what stands out is how deliberately they build sisterhood and confidence.

camp-vega.author: Alum
camp-vega.text: Vega summers were pure magic — waterskiing at dawn, campfires, friends who are now bridesmaids. The spirit days still live in my head.

camp-vega.author: Parent
camp-vega.text: Our daughter had an incredible summer. Just be ready: top-of-market pricing, competitive enrollment, and a polished culture that is not for every family.

tripp-lake-camp.author: Mother of two campers
tripp-lake-camp.text: Over a century old and it wears its traditions beautifully. Strong athletics for girls without the cutthroat edge — plus the White and Green spirit is contagious.

tripp-lake-camp.author: Alum, third generation
tripp-lake-camp.text: Tripp Lake is in our family's blood. The songs, the lake, the loyalty — nothing else compares.

tripp-lake-camp.author: Parent
tripp-lake-camp.text: Beautifully run classic camp. The full-season commitment and legacy-heavy enrollment make it hard to break into as a new family.

camp-laurel.author: Parent of returning campers
camp-laurel.text: Resort-level facilities on Echo Lake and a program that runs like clockwork. Our kids each found their niche — equestrian for one, waterski for the other.

camp-laurel.author: Former counselor
camp-laurel.text: Gorgeous campus and professional operation. Expectations of staff are high, which translates into a really consistent camper experience.

camp-laurel.author: Parent
camp-laurel.text: Fantastic summer, though the polish comes at a serious price and it can feel more like a country club than a rustic Maine camp — depends what you want.

camp-manitou.author: Father of a Manitou boy
camp-manitou.text: Elite sports instruction with a surprisingly warm, brotherly culture. The staff-to-camper attention is remarkable for a camp of this caliber.

camp-manitou.author: Alum
camp-manitou.text: Manitou men stay Manitou men. Best summers of my life on East Pond — the coaching legitimately made me a varsity athlete.

camp-manitou.author: Parent
camp-manitou.text: Our son grew tremendously. It is a premium-priced, sports-forward environment, so a kid who mostly wants arts should look elsewhere.

camp-skylemar.author: Parent of two boys
camp-skylemar.text: Skylemar somehow combines serious sports with the kindest culture in Maine. The food is legendary — our kids brag about it — and the directors know every boy.

camp-skylemar.author: Alum
camp-skylemar.text: The Naples hills, the golf program, cookouts, lifelong brothers. Skylemar does not chase flash; it just does everything well.

camp-skylemar.author: Parent
camp-skylemar.text: Wonderful first sleepaway summer. Only nitpicks: Maine is a haul from the mid-Atlantic and tuition stings.

camp-matoaka.author: Mother of a Matoaka girl
camp-matoaka.text: All-girls magic on East Pond — spirit, sisterhood, and a waterfront program that had our daughter slalom skiing by August. Communication home is excellent.

camp-matoaka.author: Alum
camp-matoaka.text: Blue and Gold forever. The song contests and banquet still give me chills. Matoaka friendships have outlasted everything else.

camp-matoaka.author: Parent
camp-matoaka.text: A polished, joyful summer. The full-season format and price are the entry barrier, and returning-family enrollment leaves few new spots.

camp-modin.author: Jewish parent from Boston
camp-modin.text: The oldest Jewish camp in New England, and it feels both proudly traditional and totally current. Culturally Jewish without being heavy-handed — exactly our speed.

camp-modin.author: Alum
camp-modin.text: Modin's lakefront, the Shabbat white-clothes walk, four weeks that felt like a lifetime. The community is warm and surprisingly low-drama.

camp-modin.author: Parent
camp-modin.text: Beautifully run with great food for camp. Premium tuition and fast-filling sessions are the trade-off.

camp-winnebago.author: Father of a Winnebago boy
camp-winnebago.text: A century-old boys camp that has resisted every fad — no screens, real woodcraft, singing after dinner. Our son came home more grounded and capable.

camp-winnebago.author: Alum, second generation
camp-winnebago.text: Winnebago is timeless. The same fields, the same songs my father sang, and friendships that have lasted forty years in our family.

camp-winnebago.author: Parent
camp-winnebago.text: Wonderful, intentionally old-fashioned camp. Cabins are rustic and the full-season model is a leap for first-timers — but that is rather the point.

camp-winadu.author: Father of a young athlete
camp-winadu.text: The sports facilities rival small colleges — pro-grade courts, turf fields, and coaches who have played at high levels. Our son's skills exploded in one summer.

camp-winadu.author: Alum
camp-winadu.text: Winadu pride is real. Competitive but brotherly — the counselors made even the worst athlete in the bunk feel like a starter.

camp-winadu.author: Parent
camp-winadu.text: Fantastic for sporty kids; less so for a camper who wants a mellow, artsy summer. And Berkshires premium pricing applies.

camp-danbee.author: Mother of two Danbee girls
camp-danbee.text: High-energy, spirit-soaked all-girls camp. The Olympics breakout is theatrical genius and the counselors are role models our daughters still text.

camp-danbee.author: Alum
camp-danbee.text: Danbee taught me to be loud, proud, and kind. The sisterhood is intense in the best way.

camp-danbee.author: Parent
camp-danbee.text: Terrific summer and strong communication. The rah-rah spirit culture is a lot for a quiet kid at first, and tuition is top-tier Berkshires.

camp-greylock.author: Father of a Greylock boy
camp-greylock.text: A hundred-plus years of boys' camping done right — superb athletics, serious traditions, and counselors who hold kids to a high standard with warmth.

camp-greylock.author: Alum, legacy family
camp-greylock.text: Greylock vs. Winadu games were the Super Bowl of my childhood. The brotherhood and the Becket campus are unmatched.

camp-greylock.author: Parent
camp-greylock.text: Excellent, well-oiled camp. It is sports-centric and full-session, so make sure that matches your son before writing the (large) check.

camp-romaca.author: Mother of a returning camper
camp-romaca.text: Small enough that every girl is truly known. Romaca's sisterhood is gentle and inclusive — our shy daughter found her voice on that lake.

camp-romaca.author: Alum
camp-romaca.text: The intimacy is the magic. A hundred-year-old camp that feels like a big family, banjo hill sunsets and all.

camp-romaca.author: Parent
camp-romaca.text: Lovely, nurturing environment. Being smaller means fewer niche activities than the mega-camps, and sessions fill quickly.

camp-watitoh.author: Parent of brother-sister campers
camp-watitoh.text: One of the few true coed family-run camps left in the Berkshires. Same family for generations, and the directors know every camper's story.

camp-watitoh.author: Alum
camp-watitoh.text: Watitoh on Center Lake was home. Low-key, warm, classic — camp the way it is supposed to feel.

camp-watitoh.author: Parent
camp-watitoh.text: Wonderful people and a great coed dynamic. Facilities are traditional rather than shiny, which we liked but flashier families may not.

camp-walt-whitman.author: Parent of two campers
camp-walt-whitman.text: The rare coed camp that is elite across the board — sports, waterfront on its private lake, and a culture of kindness the directors enforce personally.

camp-walt-whitman.author: Alum
camp-walt-whitman.text: CWW summers in Piermont were everything. Coed done right: brothers and sisters across the camp, hiking Moosilauke, friendships for life.

camp-walt-whitman.author: Parent
camp-walt-whitman.text: Superb camp with thoughtful staff. New Hampshire is a trek, tuition is steep, and spots for new families are genuinely scarce.

camp-wicosuta.author: Mother of a first-time camper
camp-wicosuta.text: Wico is built for confidence — the whole program pushes girls to try, fail, and try again in the most supportive way. The lake and campus are beautiful.

camp-wicosuta.author: Alum and former counselor
camp-wicosuta.text: The sisterhood is real and the two-and-four-week options make it accessible for newer camp families. Spirit days are unmatched.

camp-wicosuta.author: Parent
camp-wicosuta.text: Great communication and a very happy kid. Costs run high and the bunks, while clean, are more classic than luxe.

camp-tecumseh-nh.author: Father of a Tecumseh boy
camp-tecumseh-nh.text: Over 120 years of character-building on Lake Winnipesaukee. Sportsmanship is taught like a religion — win humbly, lose graciously. Our son is a better person for it.

camp-tecumseh-nh.author: Alum, multi-generation family
camp-tecumseh-nh.text: Tecumseh does not chase trends: same green shirts, same values, same lake. That constancy is exactly why it produces such loyal men.

camp-tecumseh-nh.author: Parent
camp-tecumseh-nh.text: A treasure of a camp. Proudly rustic cabins, a full-season culture, and enrollment that runs through family lines — plan years ahead.

urj-eisner-camp.author: Reform Jewish parent
urj-eisner-camp.text: Eisner is joyful Judaism — song sessions that shake the chadar, Shabbat in the Berkshires hills, and staff who grew up there. Our kids' happiest place.

urj-eisner-camp.author: Alum
urj-eisner-camp.text: My entire adult Jewish community traces back to Eisner bunkmates. The ruach is unmatched in the Reform movement.

urj-eisner-camp.author: Parent
urj-eisner-camp.text: Warm, inclusive, values-driven. Facilities are camp-standard rather than fancy, and popular sessions waitlist by winter.

camp-longhorn.author: Third-generation Longhorn parent
camp-longhorn.text: Attaway to go! The merit system, the blob, Inks Lake — Longhorn is a Texas institution and the counselor culture is phenomenal.

camp-longhorn.author: Alum
camp-longhorn.text: Everybody is somebody at Longhorn. Forty years later I can still sing every chant. My kids go now and it is barely changed — thank goodness.

camp-longhorn.author: Parent
camp-longhorn.text: Our kids love it, but be realistic: Texas heat is brutal, cabins are old-school, and the waitlist is legendary — sign up at birth, seriously.

camp-champions.author: Parent of two campers
camp-champions.text: The intentionality is what sets Champions apart — the directors talk about raising capable adults, not just filling summers. Lake LBJ waterfront is superb.

camp-champions.author: Former senior counselor
camp-champions.text: Best staff training I have had anywhere. The Trojan-Spartan traditions and the genuine focus on each camper's growth make it special.

camp-champions.author: Parent
camp-champions.text: Wonderful experience and great communication. Pricier than most Texas camps, and summer sessions fill nearly a year ahead.

camp-ozark.author: Christian parent from Dallas
camp-ozark.text: Ozark blends serious adventure — ropes, wakeboarding, mountain biking — with faith woven naturally through the day. Our kids come home on fire in the best way.

camp-ozark.author: Camper, age 13
camp-ozark.text: War Canoe, tribal competitions, the craziest counselors ever. Two weeks is not enough.

camp-ozark.author: Parent
camp-ozark.text: Very well-run and high-energy. Know that the Christian programming is central, the Arkansas summer is hot, and popular weeks sell out extremely fast.

falling-creek-camp.author: Father of a returning camper
falling-creek-camp.text: The warrior spirit meets genuine gentleness — Falling Creek builds boys up through mountain adventure and a code of honor they actually live by.

falling-creek-camp.author: Alum and Eagle Scout
falling-creek-camp.text: Backpacking the Blue Ridge, whitewater, mountain biking — the outdoor program is the best in the Southeast, and morning assembly still echoes in my head.

falling-creek-camp.author: Parent
falling-creek-camp.text: Superb staff and values. Cabins are intentionally rustic and the no-electronics rule is absolute — great for kids, an adjustment for some parents.

rockbrook-camp.author: Mother of a Rockbrook girl
rockbrook-camp.text: A heart in the wooded mountain — the stone lodges, the waterfalls, whitewater kayaking for girls. Rockbrook is unhurried and deeply confidence-building.

rockbrook-camp.author: Alum, third generation
rockbrook-camp.text: Over a century of girls growing brave in those mountains. The crafts program and the community feel are unlike anywhere else.

rockbrook-camp.author: Parent
rockbrook-camp.text: Magical, screen-free, wonderfully rustic — which also means cold-ish cabins and spiders. Our daughter did not mind; pack accordingly.

camp-greystone.author: Mother of two Greystone girls
camp-greystone.text: Greystone is simply the standard: gorgeous facilities, gracious Christian culture, and a program that makes every girl feel chosen. The morning assemblies are famous for a reason.

camp-greystone.author: Alum
camp-greystone.text: Table talks, the lake pavilion, Sunday whites — Greystone shaped my faith and my friendships for life.

camp-greystone.author: Parent
camp-greystone.text: Extraordinary camp, extraordinary demand — enrollment is a near-lottery and the faith component is central, so make sure both fit your family.

camp-highlander.author: Parent of coed siblings
camp-highlander.text: One of the few strong coed options in the NC mountains. Tribes, the lake slide, mountain views — our kids loved sharing camp stories at the same table.

camp-highlander.author: Former counselor
camp-highlander.text: Beautiful setting under Mount Pisgah and a fun tribal tradition. Solid program overall, though it lives in the shadow of the area's century-old single-gender camps.

camp-highlander.author: Parent
camp-highlander.text: Great first sleepaway camp with kind staff. Cabins are basic mountain-camp style and a few activities had long waits at peak session.

interlochen-arts-camp.author: Parent of a violinist
interlochen-arts-camp.text: World-class faculty, ensembles that sound professional by week three, and peers who take art as seriously as your kid does. Interlochen changed our daughter's trajectory.

interlochen-arts-camp.author: Alum, now a working musician
interlochen-arts-camp.text: Playing in those orchestras under the pines was my first taste of the real thing. The uniform knickers are goofy; the training is not.

interlochen-arts-camp.author: Parent
interlochen-arts-camp.text: Extraordinary instruction, but know what it is: auditions, practice hours, and real competition for chairs. Intense kids thrive; casual kids can feel outmatched. Also: expensive.

camp-foley.author: Minneapolis parent
camp-foley.text: A century-old northwoods gem. Sailing on Whitefish Lake, real skill progressions, and staff who write actual letters home about your kid. Midwest camping at its best.

camp-foley.author: Alum
camp-foley.text: Foley taught me to sail, to lose gracefully at Ragnarok, and to love being unplugged. The four-week session flew by.

camp-foley.author: Parent
camp-foley.text: Wonderful traditional camp. Mosquitoes are a Minnesota fact of life and cabins are rustic, but our kids never noticed.

cheley-colorado-camps.author: Parent of a returning camper
cheley-colorado-camps.text: Cheley is the real Rocky Mountain deal — backpacking, horses, and a code of living that kids internalize. The term-long friendships and mountain summits changed our son.

cheley-colorado-camps.author: Alum, fourth generation
cheley-colorado-camps.text: The Cheley code hangs in my office today. Estes Park sunrises, pack trips, top-of-the-world hikes — a century of doing it right.

cheley-colorado-camps.author: Parent
cheley-colorado-camps.text: Phenomenal wilderness program. The 27-day term and the altitude adjustment are big leaps for first-timers, and it books out a year ahead.

gold-arrow-camp.author: Bay Area parent
gold-arrow-camp.text: Huntington Lake is spectacular and GAC's unplugged philosophy is the antidote to California screen life. Waterskiing, sailing, and rock climbing with truly warm staff.

gold-arrow-camp.author: Alum and former counselor
gold-arrow-camp.text: Sleeping outdoors under the Sierra stars, the island outpost trips, the lifelong friendships — GAC is a California institution for a reason.

gold-arrow-camp.author: Parent
gold-arrow-camp.text: Our daughter adored it. Note the open-air shelters mean genuinely rustic living, and two-week sessions sell out very early.

catalina-island-camps.author: SoCal parent
catalina-island-camps.text: Snorkeling with garibaldi before breakfast — the ocean IS the camp. Howland's Landing is beautiful and the marine science program is genuinely educational.

catalina-island-camps.author: Camper, age 12
catalina-island-camps.text: Kayaking, paddleboarding, night snorkel with bioluminescence. The boat ride over feels like leaving the real world.

catalina-island-camps.author: Parent
catalina-island-camps.text: Unique ocean experience with caring staff. Island logistics mean strict drop-off windows, and the rustic cabins plus salt-water everything is not for every kid.

ymca-camp-orkila.author: Seattle parent
ymca-camp-orkila.text: Orcas Island is a magical place to be a kid — beach fires, sea kayaking among seals, forest trails. Orkila's YMCA pricing and scholarships make it refreshingly accessible.

ymca-camp-orkila.author: Alum
ymca-camp-orkila.text: A century of PNW kids grew up on that shoreline. The salmon bake and the ferry ride in are core memories.

ymca-camp-orkila.author: Parent
ymca-camp-orkila.text: Great value and beautiful setting, but it is a big operation — counselor quality varies year to year and the cabins are quite bare-bones.

willow-lake-day-camp.author: Morris County parent
willow-lake-day-camp.text: Feels like a sleepaway camp that goes home at 4 — lake with waterslides, go-karts, great swim instruction. Bus counselors even text updates.

willow-lake-day-camp.author: Parent of two campers
willow-lake-day-camp.text: Our kids love the electives and the lake. Costs run high for day camp and the popular weeks fill by early winter.

willow-lake-day-camp.author: Former group counselor
willow-lake-day-camp.text: Well-organized, safety-obsessed, and genuinely fun. The staff culture keeps people coming back summer after summer.

spring-lake-day-camp.author: Bergen County parent
spring-lake-day-camp.text: The facilities are stunning — heated pools, the lake, mini golf — but it is the warmth of the owners walking around greeting kids by name that sold us.

spring-lake-day-camp.author: Parent of a kindergartner
spring-lake-day-camp.text: Our nervous 5-year-old was singing camp songs by day three. Junior camp staff are exceptional with little ones.

spring-lake-day-camp.author: Parent
spring-lake-day-camp.text: Fantastic program, premium price. The bus ride from our end of the county runs long on traffic days.

camp-riverbend.author: Parent of returning campers
camp-riverbend.text: Family-run for generations and it shows — the directors know every child. The wooded riverside campus feels like real camp, not a converted school yard.

camp-riverbend.author: Alum, now a parent there
camp-riverbend.text: I went in the 90s and now my kids go. Same warmth, same tube rides on the river, better facilities.

camp-riverbend.author: Parent
camp-riverbend.text: Lovely traditional day camp with excellent swim instruction. Shorter season options are limited and it books up fast with legacy families.

harbor-hills-day-camp.author: Mendham-area parent
harbor-hills-day-camp.text: Gorgeous hilltop campus with a private lake, and a family-run feel that larger camps cannot fake. Our kids' swim strokes improved dramatically.

harbor-hills-day-camp.author: Parent of three campers
harbor-hills-day-camp.text: The counselor consistency is what keeps us — many are former campers, and the staff genuinely love the place.

harbor-hills-day-camp.author: Parent
harbor-hills-day-camp.text: Great classic day camp. Tuition is on the higher side for the area and the camp day felt short once bus time was factored in.

camp-deeny-riback.author: JCC member parent
camp-deeny-riback.text: Warm Jewish community feel without being heavy on religion — Shabbat sing-alongs, great swim program, and inclusive staff who took time with our anxious camper.

camp-deeny-riback.author: Parent of a camper with an IEP
camp-deeny-riback.text: Their inclusion program is the real deal. Our son was supported, not just accommodated.

camp-deeny-riback.author: Parent
camp-deeny-riback.text: Sweet community camp at a fair price. Facilities are older than the private day camps nearby and some specialist activities rotate infrequently.

rambling-pines-day-camp.author: Hopewell Valley parent
rambling-pines-day-camp.text: Big wooded campus, horses, pools, and a nice mix of structure and choice. Our kids come home exhausted and happy — the goal, achieved.

rambling-pines-day-camp.author: Parent of two campers
rambling-pines-day-camp.text: Solid, well-run camp with flexible week options that saved our patchwork childcare summer. Communication apps could be better.

rambling-pines-day-camp.author: Former counselor
rambling-pines-day-camp.text: Management actually invests in staff training, and the campers are sweet. The horseback program is a highlight most day camps cannot offer.

breezemont-day-camp.author: Westchester parent
breezemont-day-camp.text: Beautiful facilities, strong swim instruction, and an energy level that borders on theatrical. Our kids sprint to the bus every morning.

breezemont-day-camp.author: Parent of a young camper
breezemont-day-camp.text: Great programming and the pools are fantastic. It is one of the priciest day camps around, and the size means some days feel a bit factory-line at drop-off.

breezemont-day-camp.author: Parent
breezemont-day-camp.text: Happy kids, responsive office, gorgeous campus. Wish sessions were more flexible for partial weeks.

deerkill-day-camp.author: Rockland County parent
deerkill-day-camp.text: Family-owned for decades and every bit of it feels personal — the owners are on campus daily. Heated pools, ropes course, and truly warm staff.

deerkill-day-camp.author: Alum, now sends her kids
deerkill-day-camp.text: Deerkill was my childhood and now it is my kids'. Small enough to be known, big enough to never be bored.

deerkill-day-camp.author: Parent
deerkill-day-camp.text: Excellent counselors and communication. A few of the buildings are showing their age, but the program more than makes up for it.

gate-hill-day-camp.author: Stony Point parent
gate-hill-day-camp.text: The wooded campus feels like sleepaway camp by day — zip lines, pools, a real lake. Staff greeted our daughter by name on day one.

gate-hill-day-camp.author: Parent of two campers
gate-hill-day-camp.text: Communication is superb — photos daily, quick calls when anything comes up. The flexible enrollment weeks are parent-friendly.

gate-hill-day-camp.author: Parent
gate-hill-day-camp.text: Great summer overall. Bus routes can run long, and the most popular weeks book out by midwinter.

galileo-camps.author: Palo Alto parent
galileo-camps.text: Innovation curriculum that actually teaches design thinking — my daughter came home prototyping cardboard inventions. Week-by-week flexibility fits working-parent life.

galileo-camps.author: Parent of a STEM-obsessed kid
galileo-camps.text: Creative, well-designed projects and enthusiastic staff. It is school-campus-based, so do not expect lakes and campfires — this is maker camp, not woodsy camp.

galileo-camps.author: Parent
galileo-camps.text: Good curriculum, but quality depends heavily on your site's director, and per-week pricing adds up fast for a full summer.

steve-and-kates-camp.author: Working parent of two
steve-and-kates-camp.text: The buy-days-use-them-whenever model is a lifesaver for unpredictable schedules — and unused days get refunded. Kids choose their own activities all day.

steve-and-kates-camp.author: Brooklyn parent
steve-and-kates-camp.text: My son loves the freedom — bread making one hour, stop-motion the next. It is indoors-heavy and less 'camp' than a traditional program, but the flexibility wins.

steve-and-kates-camp.author: Parent
steve-and-kates-camp.text: Convenient and the kids are content, but the self-directed model means screens and sewing machines can dominate if your kid does not seek variety. Daily rate is steep.

oasis-day-camp-central-park.author: Upper West Side parent
oasis-day-camp-central-park.text: Camp in Central Park is a genius premise — fields, trips, swim at a nearby pool, and city kids getting real outdoor time without leaving Manhattan.

oasis-day-camp-central-park.author: Parent of a first-time camper
oasis-day-camp-central-park.text: Convenient drop-off and sweet counselors. The park setting means schlepping to facilities for swim, and hot days can feel long without a dedicated campus.

oasis-day-camp-central-park.author: Parent
oasis-day-camp-central-park.text: Flexible weeks, reasonable-for-NYC pricing, happy kid. Communication was occasionally slow in peak season.
